426	3. Methodology: Learning from Next-State Signals
427	We convert next-state signals from heterogeneous interaction streams, including personal conversations,
428	terminal interactions, GUI interactions, SWE tasks, and tool-call traces, into policy gradients.
429	We first show that the next state yields two complementary signal types, evaluative and directive,
430	and introduce a hybrid RL objective that unifies them in a single per-token loss (§3.1). We then
431	address the central stability challenge of hint-conditioned distillation by proposing overlap-guided
432	hint selection and a log-probability-difference clip (§3.2). Finally, we describe how step-wise process
433	rewards are integrated with outcome rewards to handle long-horizon general agentic settings (§3.3).

482	3.1. Two Complementary Signals and A Hybrid RL Objective
483	Evaluative signal.
484	Given (𝑎𝑡, 𝑠𝑡+1), the PRM is queried 𝑚times and each query returns a vote in
485	{+1, −1, 0}. We take the majority 𝑟𝑡∈{+1, −1, 0} as the scalar reward at step 𝑡. The evaluative signal
486	is dense by construction: every scored turn contributes a sample, regardless of whether the user
487	reaction is explicit, such as “that worked,” or implicit, such as a re-query or a passing test.
488	Directive signal.
489	When evaluating (𝑎𝑡, 𝑠𝑡+1), the PRM is also asked to decide whether 𝑠𝑡+1 contains
490	a meaningful correction in the first place: extracting a hint is only worthwhile when the next state
491	actually carries directive content, and forcing extraction otherwise would yield low-quality hints
492	that destabilize training. If the answer is yes, the PRM distills 𝑠𝑡+1 into a concise hint ℎenclosed in
493	[HINT_START]...[HINT_END]; if no, the turn produces no directive signal and contributes only
494	the evaluative one. The hint is appended to the prompt as 𝑠ℎ

500	instance, a user’s terse “thanks, looks good” or a passing test result carries strong evaluative content
501	but no directive content; while an error trace pointing at a specific line yields a usable hint.

533	3.2. Overlap-Guided Hint Selection
534	A central failure mode of on-policy distillation is teacher-student distribution mismatch: when the
535	teacher distribution 𝜋𝑇places mass on tokens the student has near-zero density on, the off-policy
536	importance ratio explodes and the gradient becomes unstable (Li et al., 2026). Because we obtain the
537	teacher by conditioning the same model on a hint ℎ, the choice of hint directly controls the magnitude
538	of mismatch: a vague or off-target hint pulls the teacher far from the student, while a precise one
539	keeps the two distributions close on the tokens that matter.

606	3.3. Step-wise Reward for General Agentic RL
607	How to combine the outcome and process rewards in general agentic RL?
608	3.3.1. Why Process Rewards Are Vital for Agentic Tasks
609	In long-horizon agentic tasks, outcome-only rewards provide gradient signal only at the terminal
610	step, leaving the vast majority of turns unsupervised. A PRM assigns a reward to each turn based
611	on the next-state signal, providing dense credit assignment throughout the trajectory. Recent work
612	has provided strong empirical evidence for this. RLAnything (Wang et al., 2026) demonstrates that
613	integrating step-wise PRM signals with outcome rewards consistently outperforms outcome-only
614	training across GUI agents, text-game agents, and coding tasks. We build directly on this insight
615	in OpenClaw-RL: our PRM judges each turn using the live next-state signal as evidence, and we
616	demonstrate empirically (§4.5) that this dense signal is helpful for long-horizon RL settings.
617	3.3.2. Integrate Outcome and Process Rewards
618	Verifiable outcomes are standard supervision signals in RLVR settings. Following RLAnything (Wang
619	et al., 2026), we integrate outcome and process rewards by simply adding them together, using
620	𝑜+Í𝑚
621	𝑖=1 𝑟𝑖/𝑚as the reward for step 𝑡, where the 𝑟𝑖are independently assigned by PRM(𝑎𝑡, 𝑠𝑡+1). Unlike
622	GRPO, the presence of step-wise rewards makes it less straightforward to compute advantages. Feng
623	9
624	
625	--- Page 10 ---
626	OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking
627	et al. (2025b) group similar states and perform standardization within each group. However, in
628	real-world settings such as terminal agents, states are not easily clustered. Therefore, we directly
629	group actions with the same step index, which we find effective in our empirical studies.
630	4. Experiments
631	4.1. Personal Agent Setup
632	We use LLMs to simulate users from different professions interacting with OpenClaw on work tasks,
633	and evaluate how efficiently the model learns to align with each user’s preferences. Three settings
634	are considered—student, TA, and teacher—described below. In every session, the simulated user
635	delegates a single task to OpenClaw on their personal computer; tasks are drawn from GSM8K (Cobbe
636	et al., 2021). The user’s first message in each session is hard-coded and does not disclose their
637	preferences, allowing us to assess whether the model has internalized prior experience by examining
638	its response to this opening message. We consider the optimization effect to have been achieved once
639	the model’s response to the first message satisfies the user’s preferences in three consecutive sessions.
640	The OpenClaw policy and reward model in this setting is Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2507 (Yang et al.,
641	2025a). We set the learning rate to 1 × 10−5 and the log-probability-difference clipping coefficient
642	to 𝐶= 1, and trigger a training step after every 16 collected samples. Users are simulated with
643	Qwen3-32B to ensure faithful role-following. See more details in Appendix A.1.
644	• Student who uses OpenClaw to do homework. In this setting, a student uses OpenClaw on a
645	personal computer to complete homework while trying to avoid the appearance of relying on AI.
646	A response is identified as AI-like when it contains markers such as bold text, numbered lists, or
647	over-formatting like boxed final answers. The student interacts with OpenClaw to complete the
648	homework in a non-AI-like style and asks for revisions whenever the response is AI-like.
649	• TA who uses OpenClaw to grade homework. Once the student finishes the homework, the TA
650	uses OpenClaw to grade the assignments. The TA wants the grading to be specific and detailed,
651	and considers a grading response insufficient when its length is below 100 tokens.
652	• Teacher who uses OpenClaw to comment. After grading, the teacher uses OpenClaw to write
653	comments for the student based on the TA’s feedback. The teacher wants comments to be friendly
654	and patient, identified by warm phrases such as “well done!” or “excellent!”
655	4.2. General Agent Setup
656	Models.
657	We use Qwen3-8B (Team, 2025), Qwen3VL-8B-Thinking (Bai et al., 2025), Qwen3-
658	4B (Team, 2025), and Qwen3-4B-SFT in terminal, GUI, SWE, and tool-call settings, respectively.
659	Qwen3-4B-SFT is the model from Zhu et al. (2025), fine-tuned on the dataset of Feng et al. (2025a).
660	The PRMs for GUI and tool-call agents are Qwen3VL-8B-Thinking and Qwen3-4B, respectively.
661	Datasets.
662	We use SETA RL data (Shen et al., 2026), OSWorld-Verified (Xie et al., 2024), SWE-Bench-
663	Verified (Jimenez et al., 2023), and DAPO RL data (Yu et al., 2025a) to train the terminal, GUI, SWE,
664	and tool-call agents, respectively. The GUI agent is evaluated on the training set (excluded chrome
665	and multi-apps tasks). The tool-call agent is evaluated on AIME 2024 (Mathematical Association of
666	America, American Mathematics Competitions, 2024). For the terminal and SWE agents, we report
667	the average rollout-task accuracy over a window of RL steps.

731	We set the learning rate to 10−6, the KL coefficient to 0.01, the lower and upper

733	for the tool-call setting. For each task, we draw 8 samples. The maximum numbers of interaction
734	steps for GUI, SWE, and terminal are 30, 20, and 10, respectively. See more details in Appendix A.2.
735	4.3. Hybrid RL Extension Setup
736	We study our algorithm’s extension to general RL settings, focusing on multi-turn tool-call (Feng
737	et al., 2025a) and RLVR. For the tool-call setting, we use Retool-4B, which is supervised-fine-tuned
738	on the Retool dataset (Feng et al., 2025a), with Qwen3-8B as the PRM. For the RLVR setting, we
739	use DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B as the policy model and Qwen3-4B as the PRM, training on
740	DAPO (Yu et al., 2025a) and evaluating on AIME (Mathematical Association of America, American
741	Mathematics Competitions, 2024). We set the learning rate to 10−6, the KL coefficient to 0.01, the
742	log-probability-difference clipping coefficient to 𝐶= 2, and the lower and upper PPO clip ratios to
743	𝜀lo = 0.2 and 𝜀hi = 0.28. We sample 32 tasks per training step, with the policy drawing 8 independent
744	rollouts per task. We provide more details in Appendix A.3.
745	4.4. OpenClaw-RL Effectively Optimizes Personal Agents
746	We evaluate optimization efficiency by measuring how quickly the model learns to align with user
747	preferences during OpenClaw usage. We consider three types of users, each with distinct jobs and

836	As shown in Table 3, our algorithm achieves the desired optimization effect with only about 10

838	its efficiency and practicality for real-world applications. We also provide concrete before-and-after

840	shifts toward the user’s preferences through simple conversational interaction.
841	4.5. Unified RL Framework Across Terminal, GUI, SWE, and Tool-Call
842	We conduct experiments across widely used, real-world agent settings, including terminal, GUI,
843	SWE, and tool-call scenarios (Figure 5). Our framework handles diverse model sizes and modalities,
844	with large-scale environment parallelization to improve training scalability: we use 128 parallel
845	environments for terminal agents, 64 for GUI and SWE agents, and 32 for tool-call agents. To evaluate
846	the importance of process rewards in long-horizon tasks, we conduct RL training with integrated
847	outcome and process rewards in the tool-call (250 steps) and GUI (120 steps) settings. Combining
848	both reward types further improves performance: integrated rewards reach 0.25 and 0.33 in tool-call
849	and GUI respectively, compared with 0.19 and 0.31 under outcome-only optimization. One trade-off
850	is that hosting a PRM requires additional resources compared with outcome-only training.

909	4.6. Hybrid RL is More Efficient
910	We compare the optimization efficiency of hybrid RL against its simple ablations using LGRPO
911	𝑖
912	(GRPO)
913	and LOPD
914	𝑖
915	(OPD), as well as memory and skill-evolution methods Mem0 (Chhikara et al., 2025) and
916	Cognee (Markovic et al., 2025). As shown in Table 3, hybrid RL is substantially more efficient than
917	either GRPO or OPD alone, further validating our analysis of the complementary nature of these
918	two objectives. Hybrid RL also reaches the target performance faster than the skill- and memory-
919	evolving baselines. Notably, these baselines impose additional context overhead at inference time,
920	whereas our RL approach only updates model weights, offering a more sustainable long-term solution.
921	Under the joint optimization setting, the memory and skill-evolution methods perform comparably
922	to their separate-optimization counterparts, while hybrid RL benefits significantly more from joint
923	optimization. We hypothesize that this is because the three optimization objectives are inherently
924	coupled for the policy model.
925	4.7. Hybrid RL Generalizes to Agentic RL
926	We apply our hybrid RL framework to large-batch training settings, including multi-turn agentic RL
927	and RLVR. For multi-turn RL, hint generation focuses on tool-call feedback at each turn, and only
928	turns deemed worth annotating by the PRM are included in the OPD data. For RLVR, the PRM has
929	access to the ground-truth answer when generating hints for incorrect solutions, but is prompted to
930	avoid leaking any specifics of the solution itself. As shown by the training curves in Figure 6, hybrid
931	RL generalizes well to both agentic RL and RLVR under these large-batch settings, outperforming
932	both the outcome-only and integrated-reward baselines.
933	4.8. Overlap-Guided Hint Selection Improves Efficiency and Stability
934	We compare different hint selection strategies in this section. As shown in the left panel of Figure 7,
935	randomly selecting hints leads to training instability in the agentic RL setting. In the RLVR setting
936	(Figure 7, right), both training efficiency and stability are adversely affected. Moreover, Table 4 shows
937	that random selection yields substantially worse optimization efficiency than the sequence-optimal
938	and token-optimal methods. Together, these results demonstrate the effectiveness of selecting hints
939	via top-𝑘overlap. Comparing the two optimal-selection variants, we find that sequence-optimal

992	selection tends to be more stable than token-optimal selection in large-batch training (Figure 7).
993	4.9. Log-Probability Difference Is Vital for Stability
994	We analyze training stability through token-level log-probability shifts. After sampling responses from
995	the base generator, we fix each response and rescore the same token sequence under the original
996	prompt and a hint-augmented prompt. The per-token difference measures how much the hint changes
997	the likelihood of already-generated tokens, thereby isolating conditioning effects from generation
998	randomness. As shown in Figure 7(3), the difference can take highly extreme values, indicating
999	sharp divergence between teacher and student distributions on the same response. Such unbounded
1000	log-probability differences can amplify noisy updates and destabilize optimization. This is also
1001	observed in the Retool setting: without sufficient outcome-supervision control, the average response
1002	length keeps increasing, leading to final truncation ratios of 0.2 and 0.5 for clipping and non-clipping
1003	methods, respectively. These results show that clipping improves stability by suppressing extreme
1004	log-probability shifts and preventing uncontrolled length growth (see details in Appendix B.2).
1005	4.10. Ablation on 𝑘and Support Set 𝑆𝑖
1006	We first study the influence of 𝑘on the optimization effect. From Table 5, we observe that larger
1007	values of 𝑘lead to stronger optimization effects only when 𝑘≤4, which aligns with the finding
1008	in (Li et al., 2026). In the degenerate case, namely token-level OPD, the performance drops very
1009	significantly. Therefore, in all our main experiments, we choose 𝑘= 4 to maintain efficiency while
1010	preserving the maximum effect. We also explore using top-𝑘overlap as the support set 𝑆𝑖, which
1011	offers an efficiency advantage, and find that the performance drops slightly.
1012	4.11. Explore Different Policy and Reward Models
1013	We also evaluate OpenClaw-RL using Qwen3-32B as the policy in the joint optimization setting
1014	(Table 4), demonstrating the robustness of our method on a larger model. However, we find that a
1015	larger model does not guarantee a faster optimization than a smaller one. We further use Qwen3-8B
1016	as a PRM teacher to guide the training of Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2057 in OpenClaw (Table 7), where
1017	we find the performance is very similar to using Qwen3-4B-Thinking-2057 as the teacher itself.

1021	OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking
1022	5. Related Work
1023	5.1. RL for LLMs
1024	RLHF (Christiano et al., 2017; Ziegler et al., 2019) established the PPO-based alignment pipeline.
1025	DPO (Rafailov et al., 2023) further bypasses explicit reward modeling via closed-form preference
1026	optimization; GRPO (Shao et al., 2024) eliminates the critic network through group-relative advantage
1027	estimation, and was further scaled by DeepSeek-R1 (Guo et al., 2025) and DAPO (Yu et al., 2025a).
1028	ReasonFlux (Yang et al., 2025b) takes an orthogonal approach by applying hierarchical RL to optimize
1029	sequences of thought templates rather than raw token-level CoTs, achieving significant gains through
1030	structured reasoning. These systems operate in batch-offline mode, where data collection and training
1031	happen in separate phases with fixed datasets. OpenClaw-RL instead trains continuously from live
1032	interaction signals without any data pre-collection phase.
1033	5.2. Agentic RL and Tool-Use
1034	Foundational agent paradigms such as ReAct (Yao et al., 2023), Toolformer (Schick et al., 2023), and
1035	FireAct (Chen et al., 2023) enable multi-step interaction with external tools, but rely on demonstrations
1036	rather than online RL. Recent work applies RL to specific agent settings, SWE-agent (Yang et al., 2024)
1037	and ReTool (Feng et al., 2025a) for code and tool-use, DigiRL (Bai et al., 2024) and WebRL (Qi et al.,
1038	2024) for GUI agents, ArCHer (Zhou et al., 2024) and LOOP (Chen et al., 2025) for multi-turn credit
1039	assignment, but each targets a single environment with a dedicated training pipeline. DemyAgent (Yu
1040	et al., 2025b), RLAnything (Wang et al., 2026), and CURE (Wang et al., 2025d) advance agentic RL
1041	further by investigating data quality and closed-loop reward model co-optimization.
1042	5.3. Process Reward Models
1043	PRMs demonstrate that step-level supervision outperforms outcome-only supervision for math reason-
1044	ing. Math-Shepherd (Wang et al., 2024) automates step-wise supervision via Monte Carlo estimation
1045	without human annotations; GenPRM (Zhao et al., 2025) scales PRM with generative chain-of-thought
1046	verification. ReasonFlux-PRM (Zou et al., 2025) extends PRMs to trajectory-aware evaluation for
1047	long-CoT reasoning, providing both offline data selection and online dense process-level rewards.
1048	PRIME (Cui et al., 2025) learns implicit process rewards from outcome labels. RLAnything (Wang
1049	et al., 2026) provides the large-scale evidence that step-wise PRM signals are essential for long-horizon
1050	agentic tasks, with jointly optimized reward model signals surpassing human-labeled supervision.
1051	We extend PRM-style judging to the online setting, where process rewards are inferred from live
1052	next-state signals rather than pre-collected ground truth, across heterogeneous long-horizon agentic
1053	settings.
1054	5.4. On-Policy Distillation and Hindsight Methods
1055	On-policy distillation (Agarwal et al., 2024; Hübotter et al., 2026; Shenfeld et al., 2026) trains a
1056	student on token-level supervision from a teacher evaluated at the student’s own rollouts, transferring
1057	fine-grained guidance that scalar-reward RLVR (Guo et al., 2025; Hu et al., 2025; Shao et al., 2024;
1058	Yu et al., 2025a) cannot. Self-distillation conditions a strong teacher on a corrective hint: hindsight
1059	relabeling (Hübotter et al., 2026; Shi et al., 2026; Zhang et al., 2023) formalizes this on fixed datasets
1060	rather than extracting hints automatically, while concurrent work by Buening et al. (2026) prompts
1061	online with next-state information but leaves the hints implicit rather than as explicit training signals.
1062	A central challenge is teacher-student distribution mismatch, which causes instability (Li et al., 2026)
1063	and worsens with low-quality hints. We propose an overlap-guided selection criterion that picks
1064	15
1065	
1066	--- Page 16 ---
1067	OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking
1068	the hint whose teacher distribution most overlaps with the student’s top-𝑘tokens; combined with a
1069	log-probability-difference clip, this improves efficiency and stability.
1070	5.5. RL Training Infrastructure
1071	OpenRLHF (Hu et al., 2024), AReal (Fu et al., 2025), veRL (Sheng et al., 2025), ROLL (Wang et al.,
1072	2025b) and slime (Zhu et al., 2025) decouple rollout and training engines for scalable RL training.
1073	Built on slime, OpenClaw-RL enables four fully decoupled asynchronous loops, serving, rollout, PRM
1074	judging, and training, allowing continuous training from live multi-stream interactions with zero
1075	interruption to serving. This capability is absent from prior RL infrastructure, which assumes batch
1076	data collection rather than live deployment.
1077	6. Conclusion
1078	Every agent interaction produces a next-state signal that encodes how the agent performed and, often,
1079	how it should have acted differently. OpenClaw-RL is built on a single insight: these signals are stream-
1080	agnostic, and one policy can learn from all of them simultaneously. Personal conversations, terminal
1081	executions, GUI interactions, SWE tasks, and tool-call traces all flow into the same training loop.
1082	OpenClaw-RL shows that deployed agent interactions can be converted into useful online supervision
1083	by combining frequent evaluative signals with sparser but richer directive signals, with stable learning
1084	ensured through overlap-guided hint selection and log-probability-difference clipping. Two important
1085	challenges remain for real-world deployment. First, negative or adversarial user feedback, such as
1086	misleading corrections or malicious instructions, may poison the model if used directly for online
1087	updates, necessitating stronger training-data filtering. Second, a model optimized for personal usage
1088	may encode user-specific preferences and private information, making it an attractive target for
1089	attacks; protecting privacy and improving the safety of personalized online learning remain important
1090	directions for future work.

1272	A. Experiment Details
1273	A.1. Personal RL Configurations
1274	Training Configurations.
1275	In the OpenClaw setting, we set 𝑤RL = 𝑤OPD = 1, the clipping constant
1276	to 𝐶= 1, 𝑘= 4, the number of hints generated per sample to 3, and the learning rate to 1 × 10−5.
1277	We trigger asynchronous training after collecting 16 training samples. Because each prompt has
1278	only one response in this real-time RL setting, the GRPO advantage is set directly to the GRPO
1279	reward. Therefore, the PRM assigns an evaluative reward in {0, 1, −1}. The infrastructure hosts four
1280	components: a policy actor for training, a policy server for inference, a PRM actor for log-probability
1281	computation, and a PRM server for obtaining evaluative and directive signals. Specifically, we use 4
1282	GPUs for the policy actor, 2 GPUs for the policy server, 1 GPU for the PRM actor, and 1 GPU for the
1283	PRM server. We use Adam as the optimizer (Kingma and Ba, 2014).
1284	Evaluation Configurations.
1285	To evaluate the three OpenClaw settings, we first deploy the RL server,
1286	which hosts both model inference and model training. We then run the evaluation on personal
1287	computers with OpenClaw installed. In the student evaluation setting, the script automatically creates
1288	a working directory containing a series of homework files based on GSM8K. By default, we set the
1289	conversation-session limit to 72, meaning that at most 72 tasks are used for evaluation. The TA setting
1290	can only be evaluated after the student setting has been completed. Similarly, the teacher setting can
1291	only be evaluated after the TA setting has been completed. In the joint optimization setting, we first
1292	save the directory completed by the student as homework1, and save the directory completed by the
1293	TA as homework2. We then start joint optimization, where the three simulated users use OpenClaw
1294	to conduct their work simultaneously.

1341	A.3. Hybrid RL Extension Experiment Configurations
1342	In the general RL setting, we set 𝑤RL = 𝑤OPD = 1, the clipping constant to 𝐶= 2, 𝑘= 4, the number
1343	of hints generated per sample to 3, the learning rate to 1 × 10−6, the lower and upper PPO clipping
1344	ratios to 𝜀lo = 0.2 and 𝜀hi = 0.28, the KL coefficient to 𝛽= 0.01, and the sampling temperature to 0.6.
1345	The response-length limit per turn is 8192. The context-length limit per task is 32768, which also
1346	limits the maximum number of turns the model can take. We sample 32 tasks per training step, with
1347	the policy generating 8 independent rollouts per task. We use Adam as the optimizer (Kingma and
1348	Ba, 2014). For evaluation, we use AIME 2024 and report the average accuracy over 20 independent
1349	runs. The sampling temperature is set to 0.6. The response-length limit per turn is 16384 in the
1350	ReTool setting and 30000 in the RLVR setting.

1395	A.5. Token-Level Log-Probability Shift Analysis
1396	We study how hint conditioning changes the token-level likelihood of generated mathematical so-
1397	lutions. We use Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct as the base generator and Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct as the hint-
1398	conditioned scorer. The evaluation set consists of the first 32 examples from the MATH-500 test
1399	split. For each problem, the base model is prompted with Question:
1400	{problem}\nAnswer: and
1401	generates one response using SGLang. Generation uses tensor parallelism 4, data parallelism 2, tem-
1402	perature 0.7, top-𝑝= 0.9, and a maximum response length of 2048 tokens. After generation, we fix
1403	the sampled response and only change the conditioning context used for scoring. For each generated
1404	response 𝑥= (𝑥1, . . . , 𝑥𝑇), we compute token-level log probabilities under two prompts. The base log
1405	probability is log 𝑝base(𝑥𝑡| 𝑞, 𝑥<𝑡), where 𝑞is the original question prompt. The hint-conditioned log
1406	probability is log 𝑝hint(𝑥𝑡| 𝑞, ℎ, 𝑥<𝑡), where ℎis a length-dependent hint. We use the median generated
1407	22
1408	
1409	--- Page 23 ---
1410	OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking
1411	response length as the threshold. In this run, the threshold is 466 tokens. Responses with length
1412	at most 466 tokens receive the hint “Your response is too brief. Please be more specific and show
1413	detailed reasoning steps.” Responses longer than 466 tokens receive the hint “Your response is too
1414	verbose. Please be more succinct.”
1415	We then measure the per-token log-probability shift Δ𝑡= log 𝑝hint(𝑥𝑡| 𝑞, ℎ, 𝑥<𝑡) −log 𝑝base(𝑥𝑡|
1416	𝑞, 𝑥<𝑡). Both base and hint-conditioned scores are computed with Hugging Face Transformers using
1417	bfloat16 precision, automatic device placement, and scoring batch size 4. Since the generated response
1418	is fixed, Δ𝑡measures how the hint changes the likelihood assigned to the same token sequence rather
1419	than the quality of a newly sampled response. We visualize three groups: all tokens, tokens from short
1420	responses, and tokens from long responses. In the box plot, the orange line denotes the median, the
1421	red diamond denotes the mean, and hollow black circles denote outliers. The 𝑦-axis uses a symmetric
1422	logarithmic scale with base 2, which keeps negative shifts visible.

1514	A.7. Personal RL Prompt Templates
1515	OpenClaw Evaluative Signal Prompt
1516	PRM Evaluation; System Prompt
1517	’’’<|im_start|>system
1518	You are a process reward model (PRM) evaluating an AI assistant.
1519	You will see the assistant’s output and the subsequent next state.
1520	Your task: decide whether the assistant’s output **successfully fulfilled** the
1521	↩→user’s intent at that step, using the next state as evidence.
1522	## Understanding the next state’s role
1523	- role=’user’: A reply from the user.
1524	- role=’tool’: The return value of a tool the assistant invoked. This content was
1525	↩→NOT available before the assistant’s action -- it exists BECAUSE the
1526	↩→assistant called the tool. A successful, non-error tool output means the
1527	↩→assistant’s action worked correctly and should be scored positively.
1528	## Scoring rules
1529	- \boxed{1} (good): The next state shows the task progressed as expected -- e.g.
1530	↩→the user moves on, says thanks, the environment confirms success, or a tool
1531	↩→returns a successful, non-error result.
1532	- \boxed{-1} (bad): The next state signals the assistant’s output was wrong,
1533	↩→incomplete, or unwanted. **Key negative signals include:**
1534	* The user asks the assistant to **redo, retry, or repeat** the same action ("do
1535	↩→it again", "try again", "one more time").
1536	* The user requests a **correction or modification** to what the assistant just
1537	↩→did ("change X to Y", "no, I meant ...", "not that, ...", "please fix ...").
1538	* The user **rephrases or restates** the same request, implying the assistant did
1539	↩→not understand or execute it correctly.
1540	* The environment returns an **error, failure, or unexpected result** caused by
1541	↩→the assistant’s action.
1542	- \boxed{0} (neutral): The next state is ambiguous -- e.g. the user gives an
1543	↩→unrelated follow-up that neither confirms nor denies success, or there is
1544	↩→insufficient information to judge.
1545	## Important
1546	A change request IS negative feedback -- it means the previous output did not meet
1547	↩→the user’s need. Do NOT treat it as a neutral new instruction.
1548	Think step-by-step, then give your final score inside \boxed{}.

1887	↩→per roll * 3/6 * 3/6 = 1/4).
1888	3. The difference in likelihood is **25 percentage points** (50% - 25%).
1889	To express this as a *percentage increase relative to the second event*:
1890	$$
1891	\frac{50\% - 25\%}{25\%} \times 100\% = 100\%
1892	$$
1893	**Answer:** 100% more likely.

1895	The answer is 100%. Here’s the breakdown: The probability of rolling a number
1896	↩→greater than 3 on a six-sided die is 3/6 = 50%. The probability of rolling
1897	↩→two even numbers in a row is (3/6) * (3/6) = 25%. The relative difference is
1898	↩→(50% - 25%) / 25% = 100%.

2007	C. OPD Objective is Token-level KL
2008	Although OPD can be motivated from a trajectory-level KL objective, the loss used in practice is a
2009	sum of token-local terms. Let 𝑥denote the original prompt, 𝑥ℎdenote the hint-augmented prompt,
2010	and 𝑦= (𝑦1, . . . , 𝑦𝑇) denote a rollout sampled from the old policy. A full sequence-level KL would
2011	compare the teacher and student distributions over entire continuations. In practice, however, OPD
2012	evaluates both models on the observed prefix 𝑦<𝑖and supervises only the next-token distribution at
2013	each position:
2014	LOPD =
2015	𝑇
2016	∑︁
2017	𝑖=1
2018	KL
2019	
2020	𝜋𝑇(· | 𝑥ℎ, 𝑦<𝑖)
2021	 𝜋𝜃(· | 𝑥, 𝑦<𝑖)
2022	
2023	.
2024	Thus, each term updates the student according to the teacher distribution over the next token under
2025	a fixed prefix. It does not evaluate counterfactual continuations that would arise if a different token
2026	were chosen at position 𝑖. For example, changing 𝑦𝑖to another token 𝑣would alter all future prefixes
2027	𝑦< 𝑗for 𝑗> 𝑖, but these downstream effects are omitted by the local OPD loss. This distinction matters
2028	for our method because hint selection and support-set construction should be aligned with the actual
2029	optimization objective. Since OPD provides token-level supervision under fixed prefixes, we can select
2030	hints by measuring teacher-student overlap at the token-level.
2031	33
2032	
2033

1	---
2	id: concept-grpo
3	date: 2026-04-21
4	aliases: [GRPO, Group Relative Policy Optimization]
5	tags: [概念]
6	---
7	
8	# GRPO（Group Relative Policy Optimization）
9	
10	## 定义
11	DeepSeek 提出的一种无需 critic 模型的强化学习算法。对每个问题采样一组回答，在组内计算相对优势（advantage），用 clipped ratio + KL 惩罚更新策略。
12	
13	## 关键属性
14	- **无需 value model / critic**：与传统 PPO 不同，GRPO 通过组内相对比较计算优势，省去 critic 网络
15	- **组相对优势**：对同一任务采样 G 个轨迹，每个 token 的优势 = 组内归一化（自身 reward - 组均值）/ 组标准差
16	- **clipped ratio**：类似 PPO，限制新旧策略的重要性比率在 [1-ε, 1+ε] 内，防止策略更新过大
17	- **KL 惩罚**：约束策略不要偏离参考模型太远
18	
19	## 数学形式
20	$$GRPO(\pi) = \mathbb{E} \left[ \frac{1}{G} \sum_{g=1}^{G} \frac{1}{|\mathcal{Y}|} \sum_{i=1}^{|\mathcal{Y}|} \min\left( \frac{\pi_{\theta}(y_i)}{\pi_{old}(y_i)} \hat{A}_i, \text{clip}(\cdot, 1-\epsilon, 1+\epsilon) \hat{A}_i \right) - \beta \, KL(\pi_{\theta} \| \pi_{ref}) \right]$$
21	
22	## 相关概念
23	- [[Agent RL]] — GRPO 是 Agent RL 训练中常用的策略优化算法
24	- [[Lyapunov 优化]] — 同为在线优化方法，但 Lyapunov 用于队列稳定性，GRPO 用于策略学习
25	
26	## 来源
27	- [[agent-world]] — 使用 GRPO 作为 Agent RL 的策略优化算法
28

1	---
2	id: concept-agent-rl
3	date: 2026-04-21
4	aliases: [Agent RL, Agentic Reinforcement Learning, Agent 强化学习]
5	tags: [概念]
6	---
7	
8	# Agent 强化学习（Agent RL）
9	
10	## 定义
11	让 LLM Agent 在可执行环境中通过"生成→执行→反馈"的闭环交互来学习策略的强化学习方法。与传统的静态 SFT 训练不同，Agent RL 利用环境的真实反馈信号来优化模型行为。
12	
13	## 关键属性
14	- **闭环交互**：Agent 生成动作 → 环境执行 → 返回观测 → Agent 决定下一步
15	- **可验证奖励**：不同于 LLM-as-judge 的主观评分，使用沙箱执行、rubric 检查等可程序化验证的奖励
16	- **长链推理**：Agent 需要在多步交互中维护状态一致性，挑战远大于单轮问答
17	- **探索-利用平衡**：训练过程中需要平衡探索新工具组合和利用已知有效策略
18	
19	## 相关概念
20	- [[GRPO]] — 常用的 Agent RL 策略优化算法
21	- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论建模框架
22	- [[Model Context Protocol (MCP)]] — 为 Agent RL 提供标准化环境接口
23	
24	## 来源
25	- [[agent-world]] — 核心方法之一，多环境 Agent RL
26	
27	## 参见
28	- [[微服务部署与路由优化概览]] — 优化方法对比（RL vs 启发式 vs Lyapunov）
29

toolCalls.name: Read
toolCalls.result:
1	---
2	id: source-evolver
3	date: 2026-05-06
4	source: raw/papers/evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles.pdf
5	type: 论文
6	tags: [Agent, 强化学习, 自演化, 经验蒸馏, GRPO, 闭环学习]
7	---
8	
9	# EvolveR: Self-Evolving LLM Agents Through an Experience-Driven Lifecycle
10	
11	## 一句话总结
12	
13	EvolveR 提出了一种闭环的经验驱动生命周期框架，让 LLM Agent 能够通过"在线交互 → 离线自蒸馏 → 策略演化"的循环，将原始交互轨迹提炼为可复用的策略原则，并通过 GRPO 强化学习内化这些原则，实现 Agent 的持续自我改进。
14	
15	## 基本信息
16	
17	| 项目 | 内容 |
18	|------|------|
19	| **标题** | EvolveR: Self-Evolving LLM Agents Through an Experience-Driven Lifecycle |
20	| **作者** | Rong Wu, Xiaoman Wang, Jianbiao Mei, Pinlong Cai, Daocheng Fu, Cheng Yang, Licheng Wen, Xuemeng Yang, Yufan Shen, Yuxin Wang, Botian Shi |
21	| **机构** | 浙江大学、上海人工智能实验室、华东师范大学、复旦大学、中南大学、上海创新研究院、上海交通大学、中国科学技术大学 |
22	| **日期** | 2025-10（arXiv v2: 2026-05-08） |
23	| **arXiv** | 2510.16079 |
24	| **代码** | https://github.com/Edaizi/EvolveR |
25	
26	## 研究背景与动机
27	
28	当前 LLM Agent 在工具使用方面表现出色，但存在一个根本性缺陷：**操作性失忆（operational amnesia）**——每次交互都被视为独立事件，Agent 无法从过去的成功或失败中系统性地学习。
29	
30	已有方法尝试解决这一问题，但各有不足：
31	
32	| 范式 | 代表方法 | 核心缺陷 |
33	|------|---------|---------|
34	| 无状态执行 | ReAct | 每次交互后丢弃经验，Agent 内在策略不变 |
35	| 原始轨迹学习 | ExpeL | 检索原始轨迹，难以抽象和泛化，仅模仿而非提炼 |
36	| 外部教师蒸馏 | Reflexion + 外部 LLM | 依赖更强的教师模型，Agent 自身策略仍未改变；且存在"认知不对齐"问题 |
37	
38	EvolveR 的核心洞察是：**Agent 需要一个完整的闭环生命周期**——不仅要能从经验中提炼智慧，还要能将这种智慧内化为自身策略的一部分，形成"越用越强"的正向循环。
39	
40	## 核心方法
41	
42	### 总体架构
43	
44	EvolveR 由三个相互连接的核心组件构成，形成闭环：
45	
46	```
47	在线交互（Online Interaction）
48	    ↓ 生成高质量轨迹
49	策略演化（Policy Evolution via GRPO）
50	    ↓ 更新策略参数
51	离线自蒸馏（Offline Self-Distillation）
52	    ↓ 提炼策略原则 → 喂入在线交互
53	    ↑_________________________|
54	```
55	
56	### 符号表
57	
58	| 符号 | 含义 | 维度/说明 |
59	|------|------|----------|
60	| $\pi_\theta$ | Agent 策略模型 | 参数为 $\theta$ 的 LLM |
61	| $s_t$ | 状态 | Agent 在步骤 $t$ 所处的环境状态（部分可观测） |
62	| $a_t \in \mathcal{A}$ | 动作 | 三种动作之一 |
63	| $\mathcal{E}$ | 经验库（Experience Base） | 存储已蒸馏的策略原则集合 |
64	| $p$ | 原则（Principle） | 自然语言描述 + 结构化三元组 |
65	| $P_k$ | 检索到的原则集合 | Top-$k$ 条相关原则 |
66	| $\tau$ | 轨迹（Trajectory） | 一次完整交互的记录 |
67	| $c_{\text{succ}}(p)$ | 原则 $p$ 的成功次数 | 历史使用中引导成功的累计 |
68	| $c_{\text{use}}(p)$ | 原则 $p$ 的使用次数 | 历史被检索使用的累计 |
69	| $s(p)$ | 原则 $p$ 的度量分数 | 经验效用评估 |
70	| $\theta_{\text{sim}}$ | 语义相似度阈值 | 默认 0.85 |
71	| $\theta_{\text{prune}}$ | 剪枝阈值 | 默认 0.3 |
72	| $R(\tau)$ | 轨迹奖励 | 复合奖励函数 |
73	| $w_o, w_f$ | 奖励权重 | 结果奖励 / 格式奖励权重 |
74	| $G$ | GRPO 组大小 | 每个提示的采样轨迹数（默认 8） |
75	
76	### 动作空间设计
77	
78	Agent 的动作空间 $\mathcal{A}$ 包含三种动作：
79	
80	1. **`<search experience>`**：查询内部经验库 $\mathcal{E}$，检索相关策略原则。环境返回检索到的原则作为观测。
81	2. **`<search knowledge>`**：查询外部知识库（搜索引擎），获取事实信息。环境返回检索到的信息作为观测。
82	3. **`<answer>`**：输出最终答案，结束交互。
83	
84	关键区别：`<search experience>` 和 `<search knowledge>` 是两种本质不同的检索——前者检索的是 Agent 自己从过去经验中提炼的**策略性智慧**，后者检索的是外部**事实性信息**。
85	
86	### 第一阶段：离线经验自蒸馏（Offline Self-Distillation）
87	
88	此阶段 Agent 参数 $\theta$ 冻结，专注于从原始轨迹中提炼结构化知识。
89	
90	#### 原则蒸馏
91	
92	利用 Agent 自身的策略模型 $\pi_\theta$ 分析历史交互轨迹。通过精心设计的 prompt 让模型扮演"专家"角色，审查每条轨迹并提炼核心策略洞察：
93	
94	- **成功轨迹** → 引导原则（Guiding Principle）：描述成功策略
95	- **失败轨迹** → 警示原则（Cautionary Principle）：描述应避免的陷阱
96	
97	每条原则包含两部分：
98	1. **自然语言描述**：一句话概括核心建议
99	2. **结构化三元组**：$(subject, predicate, object)$ 格式的知识表示
100	
101	直觉：这不是简单的"记录做了什么"，而是提炼"为什么这么做有效/无效"的抽象洞察。
102	
103	#### 去重与整合
104	
105	新原则不是简单添加，而是经过严格的两级整合流程：
106	
107	**第一级：同题去重**
108	
109	由于 GRPO 对同一问题采样多条轨迹（$G=8$），同一问题会产生多条相似原则。使用 $\pi_\theta$ 进行成对语义等价检查，每个语义等价簇仅保留一条代表。
110	
111	**第二级：跨题整合**
112	
113	对每条唯一原则，执行两阶段匹配：
114	1. 通过 embedding 相似度从 $\mathcal{E}$ 检索最相似的已有原则
115	2. 用 $\pi_\theta$ 做二元语义等价判断
116	
117	更新规则：
118	
119	$$\mathcal{E} \leftarrow \begin{cases} \mathcal{E} \cup \{p_{\text{cand}}\} & \text{if } \max_{p \in \mathcal{E}} \text{sim}(p_{\text{cand}}, p) < \theta_{\text{sim}} \\ \text{Merge}(\mathcal{E}, p^*, \tau_{\text{src}}) & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$$
120	
121	其中 $p^* = \arg\max_{p \in \mathcal{E}} \text{sim}(p_{\text{cand}}, p)$，$\text{sim}(\cdot, \cdot)$ 为余弦相似度，$\theta_{\text{sim}} = 0.85$。
122	
123	直觉：新知识要么作为独立条目加入，要么作为已有原则的新证据被合并——确保经验库是"精炼"的而非"膨胀"的。
124	
125	#### 质量控制：动态评分
126	
127	每条原则跟踪使用次数和成功次数，计算经验效用分数：
128	
129	$$s(p) = \frac{c_{\text{succ}}(p) + 1}{c_{\text{use}}(p) + 2}$$
130	
131	分子 +1、分母 +2 是 Laplace 平滑，防止早期样本量不足时的极端值。
132	
133	定期剪枝：删除 $s(p) < \theta_{\text{prune}}$（默认 0.3）的原则。
134	
135	### 第二阶段：在线交互（Online Interaction）
136	
137	Agent 在 Think-Act-Observe 循环中工作，核心创新在于**检索到的原则如何改变 Agent 行为**：
138	
139	- Agent 随时可发出 `<search experience>` 动作
140	- 检索到的原则 $P_k$ 提供的不是事实信息，而是**启发式引导**，塑造 Agent 的后续推理
141	- 例如，检索到"对于比较类问题，在得出结论前应收集双方数据"，Agent 的内部推理（`<thinking>`）和后续 `<search knowledge>` 动作都会被这一原则引导
142	
143	**双重视角理解在线阶段**：
144	1. 当下层面：利用原则高效解决当前任务
145	2. 未来层面：原则引导下的高质量轨迹为下一轮离线蒸馏提供优质素材
146	
147	### 第三阶段：策略演化（Policy Evolution via RL）
148	
149	使用 GRPO 更新策略，将经验引导的交互内化为策略参数。
150	
151	#### 复合奖励函数
152	
153	$$R(\tau) = w_o R_{\text{outcome}}(\tau) + w_f R_{\text{format}}(\tau)$$
154	
155	**结果奖励** $R_{\text{outcome}}$（稀疏、二元）：
156	
157	$$R_{\text{outcome}}(\tau) = \text{EM}(a_{\text{pred}}, a_{\text{gold}})$$
158	
159	即预测答案与标准答案的精确匹配。
160	
161	**格式奖励** $R_{\text{format}}$（密集、塑形）：
162	
163	$$R_{\text{format}}(\tau) = \frac{\mathbb{I}(\tau_{\text{complete}}) \cdot R_{\text{think}}(\tau) + R_{\text{search}}(\tau)}{2}$$
164	
165	其中：
166	- $\mathbb{I}(\tau_{\text{complete}})$：轨迹完整性指示器（必须包含 `<thinking>`、至少一种搜索、`<answer>`）
167	- $R_{\text{think}}$：推理步数评分，鼓励 6 步推理（过多反而扣分）
168	- $R_{\text{search}}$：搜索评分 = 多样性分数（同时使用经验和知识搜索得 0.5，仅一种得 0.2）+ 数量奖励（每次额外搜索 +0.1，上限 0.5）
169	
170	参数设置：$w_o = 1.0$，$w_f = 0.1$，即结果奖励权重远高于格式奖励。
171	
172	#### GRPO 目标函数
173	
174	$$J_{\text{GRPO}}(\theta) = \mathbb{E}_{\tau \in \mathcal{D}} \left[ \sum_{t=1}^{|\tau|} \min\left( \rho_t(\theta) \hat{A}_t,\  \text{clip}(\rho_t(\theta), 1 - \epsilon, 1 + \epsilon) \hat{A}_t \right) - \beta D_{\text{KL}}[\pi_\theta \| \pi_{\text{ref}}] \right]$$
175	
176	其中 $\rho_t(\theta) = \frac{\pi_\theta(a_t | h_t)}{\pi_{\text{old}}(a_t | h_t)}$ 为重要性采样比率，$\hat{A}_t$ 为优势估计。
177	
178	关键：轨迹 $\mathcal{D}$ 是在经验原则引导下收集的，因此 GRPO 学到的不是一个通用的推理策略，而是**如何有效利用自身蒸馏智慧的策略**——这才是真正"关闭循环"的关键。
179	
180	### 冷启动（Cold Start）
181	
182	在 RL 训练前，用约 700 条 NQ + HotpotQA 训练集样本，由 GPT-4o 生成格式正确的 CoT 交互轨迹，通过 SFT 教会模型基本的交互格式。LoRA 微调，3 epochs，学习率 $1 \times 10^{-4}$。
183	
184	### 实现细节
185	
186	| 参数 | 值 |
187	|------|-----|
188	| 基座模型 | Qwen2.5 (0.5B/1.5B/3B/7B) |
189	| 最大序列长度 | 8192（输入）/ 1024（输出） |
190	| 嵌入模型 | BGE-M3 |
191	| 经验库后端 | Milvus（向量相似度搜索） |
192	| 每步采样 | 128 prompts, G=8 trajectories |
193	| RL 学习率 | $1 \times 10^{-6}$ |
194	| GRPO $\epsilon$ | 0.2 |
195	| KL 系数 $\beta$ | 0.001 |
196	| 训练硬件 | 8x A100 (80GB) |
197	| 3B 模型训练时间 | ~39.4 小时 |
198	| 知识检索 top-$k_d$ | 3 |
199	| 经验检索 top-$k_e$ | 3 |
200	
201	## 实验结果
202	
203	### 主实验（Table 1）
204	
205	在 7 个 QA benchmark 上评估，包括域内（NQ、HotpotQA）和域外（TriviaQA、PopQA、2Wiki-MultiHopQA、Musique、Bamboogle）。
206	
207	**Qwen2.5-3B 结果：**
208	
209	| 方法 | NQ | HotpotQA | TriviaQA | PopQA | 2wiki | Musique | Bamboogle | 平均 |
210	|------|-----|----------|----------|-------|-------|---------|-----------|------|
211	| Direct Inference | 0.106 | 0.149 | 0.288 | 0.108 | 0.244 | 0.020 | 0.024 | 0.134 |
212	| RAG | 0.348 | 0.255 | 0.544 | 0.387 | 0.226 | 0.047 | 0.080 | 0.270 |
213	| Search-R1-instruct | 0.341 | 0.324 | 0.545 | 0.378 | 0.319 | 0.103 | 0.264 | 0.325 |
214	| Rejection Sampling | 0.294 | 0.240 | 0.488 | 0.332 | 0.233 | 0.059 | 0.210 | 0.265 |
215	| **EvolveR** | **0.434** | **0.373** | **0.584** | **0.434** | **0.381** | **0.137** | **0.328** | **0.382** |
216	
217	**Qwen2.5-7B 结果：**
218	
219	| 方法 | NQ | HotpotQA | TriviaQA | PopQA | 2wiki | Musique | Bamboogle | 平均 |
220	|------|-----|----------|----------|-------|-------|---------|-----------|------|
221	| Search-R1-instruct | 0.393 | 0.370 | 0.610 | 0.397 | 0.414 | 0.146 | 0.368 | 0.385 |
222	| Rejection Sampling | 0.360 | 0.331 | 0.592 | 0.380 | 0.296 | 0.123 | 0.355 | 0.348 |
223	| **EvolveR** | **0.462** | **0.411** | **0.620** | **0.473** | **0.395** | **0.168** | **0.392** | **0.417** |
224	
225	关键发现：EvolveR 在两个模型规模上都取得了最高平均分，且优势在域外数据集上同样显著，说明其策略具有良好的泛化性。
226	
227	## 消融实验/分析
228	
229	### 1. 自蒸馏 vs 教师蒸馏（Table 2）
230	
231	| 模型 | 自蒸馏平均 | GPT-4o-mini 教师蒸馏平均 | 结论 |
232	|------|-----------|------------------------|------|
233	| 0.5B | 0.150 | **0.220** | 小模型自蒸馏能力不足，教师更有效 |
234	| 1.5B | 0.270 | **0.290** | 差距缩小 |
235	| 3B | **0.382** | 0.370 | **反转！自蒸馏超越教师蒸馏** |
236	
237	核心洞察：**认知对齐（Cognitive Alignment）**——当模型足够大时，由自身策略蒸馏出的原则与自身推理方式更"对齐"，比外部更强模型的蒸馏更有效。
238	
239	### 2. 经验检索的必要性（Table 3）
240	
241	| 模型 | 有检索 | 无检索 | 差距 |
242	|------|--------|--------|------|
243	| 0.5B | 0.150 | 0.078 | -48% |
244	| 1.5B | 0.270 | 0.123 | -54% |
245	| 3B | 0.382 | 0.340 | -11% |
246	
247	所有规模上移除检索都导致显著下降，证明经验检索是不可或缺的。
248	
249	### 3. 组件归因（Table 4）
250	
251	| 原则蒸馏 | 原则检索 | RL | 平均分 |
252	|---------|---------|-----|--------|
253	| 否 | 否 | 否 | 0.134 |
254	| 否 | 否 | 是 | 0.325 |
255	| 是 | 是 | 否 | 0.357 |
256	| 是 | 否 | 是 | 0.340 |
257	| 是 | 是 | 是 | **0.382** |
258	
259	关键发现：
260	- RL 单独提供基线提升（0.134 → 0.325）
261	- 经验检索与 RL 是互补的：检索提供推理引导，RL 优化利用检索原则的策略
262	- 三者协同效果最强
263	
264	### 4. SFT vs RL 训练对比（Table 8）
265	
266	| 方法 | 平均分 |
267	|------|--------|
268	| EvolveR (SFT) | 0.357 |
269	| EvolveR (RL/GRPO) | **0.382** (+7%) |
270	
271	SFT 只学习表面动作模式，GRPO 从成功和失败轨迹中学习"什么该做、什么该避免"。
272	
273	### 5. 原则质量分析（Table 6）
274	
275	人工标注 100 条原则：
276	
277	| 分数层级 | 理想（正确且可操作） | 模糊（正确但不可操作） | 错误/误导 |
278	|---------|---------------------|----------------------|----------|
279	| 低分段 (0.30-0.44) | 26% | 50% | 24% |
280	| 高分段 (0.44-0.75) | **82%** | 10% | 8% |
281	
282	动态评分机制有效过滤了低质量原则，高分段中可操作原则占比从 26% 提升至 82%。
283	
284	### 6. 经验库扩展性（Table 7）
285	
286	| 原则数 | 检索延迟 | 平均分 |
287	|--------|---------|--------|
288	| 4,213 | 0.04s | 0.301 |
289	| 13,435 | 0.06s | 0.372 |
290	| 23,448 | 0.12s | 0.382 |
291	| 32,805 | 0.16s | 0.396 |
292	| 45,083 | 0.19s | **0.410** |
293	| 49,934 | 0.20s | 0.387 |
294	
295	经验库扩展至近 5 万条原则时，检索延迟仅 0.20s，性能保持稳定甚至在 45k 时达到峰值。
296	
297	### 7. 经验内化尝试（Table 11）
298	
299	尝试将检索到的原则直接"吸收"进模型参数（不解码器 loss mask），结果轻微下降（0.382 → 0.371）。原因是 top-k 检索中并非所有原则都相关，直接内化引入了噪声。
300	
301	### 8. 训练动态分析
302	
303	- **动作频率演化**：推理步数和搜索次数先增后收敛（约 6 次推理、4 次知识搜索、2 次经验搜索）
304	- **原则质量提升**：后期生成原则的平均分数（0.500）高于早期（0.462），说明 Agent 的蒸馏能力随训练改善
305	
306	### 9. 模型规模泛化性
307	
308	| 模型 | 平均分 |
309	|------|--------|
310	| 0.5B | 0.150 |
311	| 1.5B | 0.270 |
312	| 3B | 0.382 |
313	| 7B | 0.417 |
314	
315	性能随基座模型规模单调增长，证明 EvolveR 是一个可泛化的范式，而非针对特定规模的技巧。
316	
317	## 优点与局限
318	
319	### 优点
320	
321	1. **闭环自演化**：首次实现了"交互→蒸馏→策略更新"的完整闭环，而非单点改进
322	2. **认知对齐**：自蒸馏在 3B+ 规模超越教师蒸馏，避免了对更强外部模型的依赖
323	3. **经验库管理完善**：去重、评分、剪枝三级质量控制，确保知识库精炼而非膨胀
324	4. **即插即用**：作为通用框架可应用于不同规模的基座模型
325	5. **高效检索**：5 万条原则下延迟仅 0.20s，实用性强
326	6. **全面消融**：多层次消融实验清晰揭示了每个组件的贡献
327	
328	### 局限
329	
330	1. **蒸馏质量受限于基座模型**：小模型（0.5B）自蒸馏效果显著差于教师蒸馏，存在"能力天花板"
331	2. **仅验证 QA 任务**：未在具身交互、创意生成等其他任务类型上验证
332	3. **长期遗忘问题未解决**：虽然有剪枝机制，但真正的终身学习（数千任务）的可扩展性未验证
333	4. **经验内化失败**：尝试将原则直接内化到参数中未成功，说明当前方案的信息传递仍然是"检索式"而非"内化式"
334	5. **安全性担忧**：Agent 自行演化原则可能发展出不良策略，需要与价值对齐技术配合
335	6. **冷启动依赖外部模型**：初始 SFT 数据需要 GPT-4o 生成
336	
337	## 提及的实体
338	
339	- [[Rong-Wu]] — 共同一作，浙江大学 & 上海人工智能实验室
340	- [[Xiaoman-Wang]] — 共同一作，华东师范大学
341	- [[Botian-Shi]] — 通讯作者，上海人工智能实验室
342	- [[上海人工智能实验室]] — 主要机构
343	- [[浙江大学]] — 第一作者机构
344	- [[Mem0]] — 结构化记忆框架，启发 EvolveR 的原则表示
345	- [[Qwen]] — 基座模型族
346	
347	## 讨论的概念
348	
349	- [[经验驱动自演化（Experience-Driven Self-Evolution）]] — 本文核心范式
350	- [[Agent 强化学习（Agent RL）]] — EvolveR 使用 RL 作为策略演化引擎
351	- [[GRPO]] — 策略优化算法
352	- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论框架（部分可观测状态）
353	- [[LoRA]] — 冷启动阶段的参数高效微调
354	- [[自蒸馏（Self-Distillation）]] — Agent 从自身轨迹中提炼策略原则
355	- [[经验库管理（Experience Base Curation）]] — 去重、评分、剪枝的知识管理机制
356	- [[认知对齐（Cognitive Alignment）]] — 自蒸馏 vs 教师蒸馏的尺度依赖性
357	
358	## 推荐参考文献
359	
360	1. **Search-R1** (Jin et al., 2025) — 直接竞争者，同样用 RL 训练 Agent 搜索能力，但不涉及经验自蒸馏。arXiv: 2503.09516
361	2. **ExpeL** (Zhao et al., 2024) — 存储原始轨迹的最相关工作，EvolveR 的主要改进对象。arXiv: 2308.10144
362	3. **Reflexion** (Shinn et al., 2023) — 通过自我反思改进的语言 Agent，EvolveR 范式 3 的代表。arXiv: 2303.11366
363	4. **DeepSeek-R1** (Guo et al., 2025) — RL 推理模型，EvolveR 借鉴了其冷启动策略。arXiv: 2501.12948
364	5. **ReAct** (Yao et al., 2023) — 推理-行动交织的基础框架。arXiv: 2210.03629
365	6. **G-Memory** (Zhang et al., 2025) — 分层记忆系统，启发 EvolveR 的原则结构。arXiv: 2506.07398
366	7. **Memory-R1** (Yan et al., 2025) — RL 增强 LLM Agent 记忆管理。arXiv: 2508.19828
367	8. **O2-Searcher** (Mei et al., 2025) — 来自同一团队的搜索增强 Agent。arXiv: 2505.16582
368	9. **Memento** (Zhou et al., 2025) — 无需微调 LLM 即可增强 Agent 记忆。arXiv: 2508.16153
369	10. **EWC** (Kirkpatrick et al., 2017) — 持续学习的经典方法，对比 EvolveR 的自演化路线。
370	
371	## 关联
372	
373	- [[Agent RL]] — EvolveR 是 Agent RL 的一个具体实例，将 RL 用于 Agent 策略演化
374	- [[GRPO]] — EvolveR 使用 GRPO 作为策略优化算法
375	- [[LoRA]] — 冷启动阶段使用 LoRA 进行参数高效微调
376	- [[POMDP]] — EvolveR 的 Agent 交互可以形式化为 POMDP
377	- [[δ-mem（在线关联记忆）]] — 另一种 Agent 记忆机制：δ-mem 是参数级内部记忆，EvolveR 是文本级外部记忆 + RL 内化
378	- [[Titans]] — 测试时记忆的架构级方案，与 EvolveR 的经验库方案形成对比
379	- [[mem0-building-production-ready-ai-agents|Mem0]] — EvolveR 的原则结构设计受 Mem0 启发
380

input.content:
---
id: source-openclaw-rl
date: 2026-05-06
source: raw/papers/openclaw-rl-turning-daily-usage-into-training-signals.pdf
type: 论文
tags: [Agent RL, 强化学习, 在线学习, 过程奖励模型, 自蒸馏, 个性化, 混合RL, GRPO, POMDP]
---

# OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking

## 一句话总结

OpenClaw-RL 提出了一种将 Agent 日常交互中的 next-state 信号（用户回复、工具输出、终端/GUI 状态变化）实时转化为在线训练信号的框架，通过服务器-客户端架构实现零中断推理，并引入混合 RL 目标（evaluative + directive 信号）和 overlap-guided hint selection 稳定策略优化，首次统一了个人 Agent 个性化与通用 Agent（terminal/GUI/SWE/tool-call）的 RL 训练基础设施。

## 基本信息

| 项目 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| **标题** | OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking |
| **作者** | Yinjie Wang*, Xuyang Chen*, Xiaolong Jin*, Mengdi Wang†, Ling Yang† |
| **机构** | Gen-Verse（GitHub: Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL） |
| **日期** | 2026-03（arXiv v2: 2026-05-11） |
| **arXiv** | 2603.10165 |
| **代码** | https://github.com/Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL |

## 研究背景与动机

### 核心问题

当前 LLM Agent 系统（如 Claude Code、OpenAI Codex、OpenClaw）已广泛部署，但一个根本性的浪费被忽视了：**每次 Agent 交互都会产生一个 next-state 信号**（用户回复、工具输出、终端状态变化、GUI 状态变化），这些信号蕴含了丰富的训练信息，却没有被任何现有 Agentic RL 系统回收为在线学习来源。

### 现有方法的局限

| 方法类型 | 代表 | 局限 |
|---------|------|------|
| 批处理离线 RL | RLHF, GRPO, DPO | 数据收集和训练分离，无法利用实时交互 |
| 记忆/技能演化 | Mem0, Cognee | 仅更新上下文/检索内容，不更新模型参数 |
| 纯标量奖励 RL | RLVR (DeepSeek-R1, DAPO) | 无法利用 next-state 中的 token-level 指令信息 |
| 固定数据集蒸馏 | Hindsight relabeling, OPD | 离线操作，无法在线学习 |
| 单环境 Agent RL | DigiRL, WebRL, SWE-agent | 各自为政，无统一基础设施 |

### 关键洞察

Next-state 信号包含两种互补的训练信号：
1. **Evaluative signal**（评估信号）：标量反馈，判断动作好坏（如用户重试=不满意，测试通过=成功）
2. **Directive signal**（指令信号）：token-level 的纠正信息（如用户说"你应该先检查文件"不仅指出错误，还说明如何改正）

现有 RLVR 方法只能消费前者，on-policy distillation 方法只能消费后者，**没有一个框架能同时利用两者**。

## 核心方法

### 一、基础设施：服务器-客户端架构

#### 架构概览

```
Personal Devices          Cloud Services
    · Confidential API      · Large Scale Environments
         ↓                          ↓
    OpenClaw (个人Agent)      Terminal/GUI/SWE/Tool-call Agent
         ↓                          ↓
         └──────────→ RL Server ←──────────┘
                        · Slime Async Framework
                        · Zero Serving Interruption
                        · Graceful Weight Update
                        
    RL Server 四组件（完全解耦异步）：
    ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐
    │ Policy      │  │ PRM/Judge   │  │ Megatron    │  │ SGLang      │
    │ Server      │  │ Server      │  │ Training    │  │ Serving     │
    │ (推理API)   │  │ (信号提取)  │  │ Engine      │  │ Engine      │
    └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘
```

#### 关键设计

1. **无状态推理 API**：RL 服务器以 API 形式托管策略 $\pi_\theta$，用户终端通过 HTTP 查询并回传交互数据。用户端框架可任意演化、更换工具，无需重新配置服务器。

2. **主线/辅线分类**：
   - **Main-line turns**：Agent 主要响应和工具执行结果，构成可训练样本
   - **Side turns**：辅助查询、记忆组织、环境转换，不产训练数据

3. **四组件完全解耦异步**：
   - 策略服务（SGLang）
   - PRM/评判（独立推理服务器，可投票、可用更强模型）
   - 策略训练（Megatron）
   - 环境托管（个人设备或云端并行环境）

   模型服务下一个请求时，PRM 评判前一个响应，训练器应用梯度更新。权重更新在同步边界推送到推理引擎，**用户永远看不到不一致的策略，也无需等待训练**。

### 二、方法论：从 Next-State 信号学习

#### 3.1 两种互补信号与混合 RL 目标

**Evaluative Signal（评估信号）**

给定 $(a_t, s_{t+1})$，PRM 查询 $m$ 次，每次返回 $\{+1, -1, 0\}$ 中的投票。取多数 $r_t \in \{+1, -1, 0\}$ 作为步骤 $t$ 的标量奖励。

- 密度：每个被评分的 turn 都贡献一个样本
- 粒度：序列级（整个 turn 压缩为 1 个标量）
- 信息：1 个标量

**Directive Signal（指令信号）**

PRM 首先判断 $s_{t+1}$ 是否包含有意义的纠正信息。如果是，将 $s_{t+1}$ 蒸馏为简洁的 hint $h$，包裹在 `[HINT_START]...[HINT_END]` 中。Hint 被追加到 prompt：$s_t^h = s_t \oplus h$，然后查询同一模型获得 teacher 分布 $\pi_T(\cdot | s_t^h)$。

- 密度：稀疏（仅在有可提取纠正的 turn 上触发）
- 粒度：token-level（完整的下一个 token 分布）
- 信息：$|S_i|$ 个 log-prob 差距

**互补性分析**

| 属性 | Evaluative | Directive | Hybrid (Ours) |
|------|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| 来源 | 标量 PRM 投票 | Hint-conditioned teacher | 两者结合 |
| 粒度 | 序列级 | Token-level | 混合 |
| 每样本信息 | 1 标量 | $\|S_i\|$ 个 log-prob 差距 | 1 标量 + $\|S_i\|$ 差距 |
| 频率 | 每个评分 turn | 有有意义 hint 的 turn | 每个评分 turn |

**混合目标函数**

对 token $i$：

$$L_i^{\text{hybrid}} = w_{\text{RL}} L_i^{\text{GRPO}} + w_{\text{OPD}} L_i^{\text{OPD}}$$

默认 $w_{\text{RL}} = w_{\text{OPD}} = 1$。两项共享同一轨迹和同一策略更新，整合了两者在频率和信息密度上的互补优势。

#### 3.2 Overlap-Guided Hint Selection

**核心问题**：On-policy distillation 的 central failure mode 是 teacher-student 分布不匹配。当 teacher 分布 $\pi_T$ 在 student 接近零密度的 token 上放置质量时，off-policy 重要性比率爆炸，梯度不稳定。

**关键洞察**：Hint 的选择直接控制不匹配程度。模糊的 hint 会把 teacher 拉离 student，精确的 hint 保持两者在重要 token 上接近。

**Overlap Signal 定义**

给定 student 的生成响应 $y$，令：
- $S_i^q = \text{top-}k\{\pi_{\text{old}}(\cdot | s_t, y_{<i})\}$：student 在位置 $i$ 的 top-$k$ 词表
- $S_{i,h}^p = \text{top-}k\{\pi_T(\cdot | s_t^h, y_{<i})\}$：hint $h$ 条件下 teacher 在位置 $i$ 的 top-$k$ 词表

Overlap signal：

$$O[h, i] = |S_i^q \cap S_{i,h}^p|$$

计数 student 的高概率 token 中有多少在 hint-conditioned teacher 下仍保持高概率。

**两种选择方案**

$$h^\star(i) = \begin{cases} \arg\max_h \sum_i O[h, i] & \text{sequence-level} \\ \arg\max_h O[h, i] & \text{token-level} \end{cases}$$

- Sequence-level：每个轨迹选一个 hint（更稳定，推荐用于 general agentic RL）
- Token-level：每个位置选不同 hint（与 OPD 的 token-level 本质对齐）

**Top-$k$ OPD Loss with Log-Probability-Difference Clip**

选定 $h^\star$ 后，将蒸馏损失限制在词表子集 $S_i$（默认 $S_i = S_i^q$）。对每个 $v \in S_i$：

$$A_v = \Delta_v \cdot w_v$$

其中：
- $\ell_{\text{old}}(v) = \log \pi_{\text{old}}(v | s_t, y_{<i})$
- $\ell_{T,h^\star}(v) = \log \pi_T(v | s_t^{h^\star}, y_{<i})$
- $w_v = \text{softmax}_{v \in S_i}(\ell_{\text{old}}(v))$：将优势集中在 student 实际可能采样的 token 上
- $\Delta_v = \text{clip}(\ell_{T,h^\star}(v) - \ell_{\text{old}}(v), -C, +C)$：裁剪 log-prob 差距

Per-vocab ratio：$\rho_v = \exp(\ell_{\text{cur}}(v) - \ell_{\text{old}}(v))$

蒸馏损失（clipped-surrogate 形式）：

$$L_i^{\text{OPD}} = \sum_{v \in S_i} \max\left( -A_v \rho_v,\ -A_v \cdot \text{clip}(\rho_v, 1 - \varepsilon_{\text{lo}}, 1 + \varepsilon_{\text{hi}}) \right)$$

其中 $\varepsilon_{\text{lo}} = 0.2$, $\varepsilon_{\text{hi}} = 0.28$（标准 PPO 裁剪）。

**稳定性机制的双重保障**：
1. Overlap-guided hint 选择使 $\rho_v$ 在监督 token 上接近 1
2. $\Delta$-clip 限制优势幅度

两者共同约束 surrogate 的两个因子，产生稳定更新。

#### 3.3 Step-wise Reward for General Agentic RL

**为什么过程奖励对 Agentic 任务至关重要**

在长程 Agentic 任务中，outcome-only 奖励仅在终止步骤提供梯度信号，绝大多数 turn 无监督。PRM 基于 next-state 信号为每个 turn 分配奖励，提供贯穿轨迹的密集信用分配。

**Outcome + Process Reward 集成**

遵循 RLAnything (Wang et al., 2026)，简单相加：

$$\text{Reward for step } t = o + \frac{1}{m}\sum_{i=1}^{m} r_i$$

其中 $o$ 为 outcome reward，$r_i$ 为 PRM$(a_t, s_{t+1})$ 独立分配的 $m$ 个过程奖励。

**标准化方法**：由于状态不易聚类（如 terminal agent），直接按相同步骤索引分组进行标准化。

### 符号表

| 符号 | 含义 | 维度/说明 |
|------|------|----------|
| $\pi_\theta$ | 策略模型 | 参数为 $\theta$ 的 LLM |
| $s_t$ | 状态 | 步骤 $t$ 的环境状态（部分可观测） |
| $a_t$ | 动作 | Agent 在步骤 $t$ 的响应 |
| $s_{t+1}$ | Next-state | 执行 $a_t$ 后的观测（用户回复/工具输出/环境状态） |
| $r_t$ | 评估信号 | $\{+1, -1, 0\}$，PRM 多数投票 |
| $h$ | 指令 hint | PRM 从 $s_{t+1}$ 蒸馏的纠正信息 |
| $s_t^h$ | Hint-augmented prompt | $s_t \oplus h$ |
| $\pi_T$ | Teacher 分布 | $\pi_T(\cdot \| s_t^h)$，与 student 同模型，条件不同 |
| $S_i^q$ | Student top-$k$ | $\text{top-}k\{\pi_{\text{old}}(\cdot \| s_t, y_{<i})\}$ |
| $S_{i,h}^p$ | Teacher top-$k$ | $\text{top-}k\{\pi_T(\cdot \| s_t^h, y_{<i})\}$ |
| $O[h, i]$ | Overlap signal | $\|S_i^q \cap S_{i,h}^p\|$ |
| $h^\star$ | 最优 hint | 最大化 overlap 的候选 hint |
| $S_i$ | Support set | 默认 $S_i = S_i^q$（student top-$k$） |
| $w_v$ | Softmax 权重 | $\text{softmax}_{v \in S_i}(\ell_{\text{old}}(v))$ |
| $\Delta_v$ | Log-prob 差距 | $\text{clip}(\ell_{T,h^\star}(v) - \ell_{\text{old}}(v), -C, +C)$ |
| $A_v$ | 蒸馏优势 | $\Delta_v \cdot w_v$ |
| $\rho_v$ | 重要性比率 | $\exp(\ell_{\text{cur}}(v) - \ell_{\text{old}}(v))$ |
| $C$ | Log-prob 裁剪系数 | Personal: 1, General RL: 2 |
| $k$ | Top-$k$ 宽度 | 默认 4 |
| $M$ | 候选 hint 数 | 默认 3 |
| $\varepsilon_{\text{lo}}, \varepsilon_{\text{hi}}$ | PPO 裁剪边界 | 0.2, 0.28 |
| $w_{\text{RL}}, w_{\text{OPD}}$ | 混合权重 | 默认均为 1 |

## 实验结果

### 4.1 个人 Agent 设置

使用 LLM 模拟三种职业用户（学生、TA、教师）使用 OpenClaw 完成工作，评估模型学习对齐用户偏好的效率。

**优化目标定义**：模型对首条消息的响应连续 3 个 session 满足用户偏好即视为优化成功。

| 用户类型 | 偏好描述 |
|---------|---------|
| 学生 | 用 OpenClaw 做作业，不想被发现用 AI（避免粗体、编号列表、过度格式化） |
| TA | 用 OpenClaw 批改作业，希望评语具体详细（长度 >= 100 tokens） |
| 教师 | 用 OpenClaw 写评语，希望评语友好耐心（包含"well done!"等温暖短语） |

**主结果（达到优化效果所需最少 session 数，越小越好）**

| 设置 | 方法 | Student | TA | Teacher | Average |
|------|------|---------|-----|---------|---------|
| **联合优化** | **Hybrid RL (Ours)** | **11.6** | **8.2** | **11.4** | **10.3** |
| | GRPO | 15.4 | 12.0 | 14.8 | 14.1 |
| | OPD | 30.8 | 34.0 | 24.4 | 29.7 |
| | Mem0 | 13.6 | 15.8 | 14.2 | 14.5 |
| | Cognee | 14.6 | 15.4 | 14.8 | 14.9 |
| **单独优化** | **Hybrid RL (Ours)** | **19.2** | **11.8** | **14.0** | **15.0** |
| | GRPO | 22.8 | 22.4 | 18.0 | 21.1 |
| | OPD | 34.6 | 36.0 | 17.6 | 29.4 |
| | Mem0 | 13.4 | 16.0 | 15.8 | 15.1 |
| | Cognee | 15.6 | 14.8 | 15.0 | 15.1 |

**关键发现**：
- Hybrid RL 在联合优化下仅需约 **10.3 个 session** 即可对齐，比 GRPO 快 27%，比 OPD 快 65%
- **联合优化显著放大 RL 收益**：Hybrid RL 联合 vs 单独差距大（10.3 vs 15.0），而记忆/技能演化方法几乎不受联合优化影响
- 假设原因：三个优化目标对策略模型是内在耦合的

### 4.2 通用 Agent 设置

覆盖 terminal、GUI、SWE、tool-call 四种最常用 real-world Agent 部署场景：

| 设置 | 环境 | Next-state 信号 | 交互步数 |
|------|------|----------------|---------|
| Terminal | Shell 执行沙箱 | stdout/stderr, exit code | 长 |
| GUI | 屏幕状态 + 可访问性树 | 视觉状态差异, 任务进度 | 长 |
| SWE | 代码仓库 + 测试套件 | 测试判决, diff, lint 输出 | 长 |
| Tool-call | API/函数执行 | 返回值, 错误追踪 | 中 |

**大规模并行环境**：128 个并行 terminal 环境、64 个 GUI/SWE、32 个 tool-call。

**过程奖励的价值**：在 tool-call（250 步）和 GUI（120 步）长程设置中，集成 outcome + process reward 分别达到 0.25 和 0.33，而仅 outcome 为 0.19 和 0.31。

### 4.3 Hybrid RL 泛化到大规模设置

- **Multi-turn tool-call (ReTool)**：Hybrid RL 优于 outcome-only 和 PRM+Outcome 基线
- **RLVR (AIME 2024)**：Hybrid RL 同样优于 outcome-only 基线

### 4.4 Overlap-Guided Hint Selection 的效果

| 设置 | Hybrid RL sequence-optimal | Hybrid RL token-optimal | Hybrid RL random | GRPO | OPD |
|------|---------------------------|------------------------|-----------------|------|-----|
| Student | 14.0 | 13.8 | 18.6 | 17.2 | 34.4 |
| TA | 9.6 | 10.0 | 12.6 | 12.0 | 29.8 |
| Teacher | 13.8 | 13.4 | 17.0 | 18.2 | 25.6 |
| Average | **12.5** | **12.4** | 16.1 | 15.8 | 29.9 |

- Sequence-optimal 和 token-optimal 性能相近，均显著优于 random
- Sequence-optimal 在 general agentic RL 中更稳定

### 4.5 Log-Probability Difference Clip 的必要性

Token-level log-probability shift 分析显示，hint conditioning 可在某些 token 上产生极端的 log-prob 差异（图 7(3)）。无裁剪时：
- 平均响应长度持续增长
- 最终截断比率：0.5（无裁剪）vs 0.2（有裁剪）
- 说明裁剪通过抑制极端 log-prob 偏移和防止长度失控来提升稳定性

### 4.6 $k$ 和 Support Set $S_i$ 的消融

| $S_i = S_i^q$ (student top-$k$) | $k=2$ | $k=4$ | $k=8$ | $k=20$ |
|--------------------------------|-------|-------|-------|--------|
| Student | 30.4 | **11.6** | 12.8 | 11.4 |
| TA | 12.0 | **8.2** | 7.6 | 7.8 |
| Teacher | 17.2 | **11.4** | 10.0 | 10.2 |
| Average | 20.2 | **10.3** | 10.1 | 9.8 |

- $k \leq 4$ 时增大 $k$ 有收益，$k \geq 4$ 后收益很小
- 退化情况（token-level OPD）性能大幅下降
- 使用 top-$k$ overlap 作为 $S_i$ 性能轻微下降

### 4.7 不同策略和奖励模型的探索

使用 Qwen3-32B 作为策略（联合优化，表 4）：方法鲁棒性在大模型上得到验证，但大模型不保证比小模型优化更快。

使用 Qwen3-8B 作为 PRM teacher 指导 Qwen3-4B-Thinking 训练（表 7）：性能与使用同模型作为 teacher 非常接近。

## 消融实验

### 核心消融总结

| 消融维度 | 关键发现 |
|---------|---------|
| **Hybrid vs 单一目标** | Hybrid RL 显著优于 GRPO 或 OPD 单独使用，验证互补性 |
| **vs 记忆/技能演化** | Hybrid RL 比 Mem0/Cognee 更快，且不增加推理时上下文开销 |
| **Hint 选择策略** | Overlap-guided >> Random；Sequence-optimal ≈ Token-optimal |
| **Log-prob clip** | 必要：防止极端差异导致长度失控和训练不稳定 |
| **$k$ 值** | $k=4$ 为最佳平衡点 |
| **Support set** | Student top-$k$ 略优于 overlap top-$k$ |
| **PRM 模型规模** | 8B PRM 与 4B PRM 效果相近 |
| **联合 vs 单独优化** | 联合优化显著放大 RL 收益，对记忆方法无影响 |

## 优点与局限

### 优点

1. **首次实现实时个人 Agent 在线 RL**：将日常交互转化为训练信号，无需预收集数据
2. **混合目标整合互补信号**：evaluative（频繁、稀疏）+ directive（稀少、丰富）的统一
3. **稳定的蒸馏机制**：Overlap-guided hint selection + log-prob clip 双重保障 teacher-student 匹配
4. **首个统一通用 Agent RL 基础设施**：覆盖 terminal/GUI/SWE/tool-call 四种核心场景
5. **零中断服务**：四组件完全解耦异步，训练不阻塞推理
6. **实用性强**：约 10 个 session 即可对齐个人偏好

### 局限

1. **负面/对抗反馈风险**：恶意纠正或误导指令可能毒化模型，需要更强的训练数据过滤
2. **隐私与安全**：为个人使用优化的模型可能编码用户特定偏好和私人信息，成为攻击目标
3. **PRM 资源开销**： hosting PRM 需要额外计算资源（vs outcome-only）
4. **Hint 质量依赖 PRM**：低质量 hint 仍可能 destabilize，尽管 overlap 选择缓解了这一问题
5. **未验证跨用户泛化**：联合优化在共享模型上的多用户场景已验证，但真正的跨用户泛化未深入探讨

## 提及的实体

- [[OpenClaw]] — 开源个人 AI 助手，本文主要实验平台
- [[Mem0]] — 结构化记忆框架，对比基线
- [[Cognee]] — 知识图谱记忆框架，对比基线
- [[Qwen]] — 基座模型族（Qwen3-4B/8B/32B, Qwen3VL-8B）
- [[DeepSeek-R1]] — RLVR 代表方法
- [[DAPO]] — 大规模 RL 系统
- [[slime]] — RL 训练基础设施框架，OpenClaw-RL 基于此构建
- [[SGLang]] — 推理服务引擎
- [[Megatron]] — 训练引擎
- [[GSM8K]] — 个人 Agent 评估数据集
- [[SETA]] — Terminal Agent 训练数据
- [[OSWorld-Verified]] — GUI Agent 评估数据集
- [[SWE-Bench-Verified]] — SWE Agent 评估数据集
- [[AIME 2024]] — Tool-call/RLVR 评估数据集

## 讨论的概念

- [[Agent RL]] — Agent 强化学习，本文核心领域
- [[GRPO]] — 组相对策略优化，混合目标的 RL 分支基础
- [[POMDP]] — 部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程，Agent 交互的理论框架
- [[过程奖励模型 (PRM)]] — 步骤级奖励判断，本文信号提取核心
- [[经验驱动自演化]] — 与 EvolveR 等工作的关联：OpenClaw-RL 是在线信号驱动，EvolveR 是离线经验蒸馏
- [[自蒸馏 (Self-Distillation)]] — OPD 分支的本质：同模型在不同条件下的蒸馏
- [[On-Policy Distillation]] — 在 student 自己的 rollout 上评估 teacher
- [[Hindsight Relabeling]] — 事后纠正重标注，hint extraction 的理论基础
- [[混合RL (Hybrid RL)]] — 本文提出的核心方法论创新
- [[Overlap-Guided Hint Selection]] — 本文提出的 hint 选择机制
- [[Log-Probability-Difference Clip]] — 本文提出的稳定性机制
- [[Next-State Signal]] — 本文核心概念：交互中的下一个状态作为训练信号源

## 推荐参考文献

### 强烈推荐

1. **RLAnything** (Wang et al., 2026) — 过程奖励对长程 Agentic 任务至关重要的大规模证据，OpenClaw-RL 直接基于此洞察。arXiv: 2602.02488
2. **Rethinking On-Policy Distillation** (Li et al., 2026) — 系统分析 teacher-student 不匹配问题，OpenClaw-RL 的 overlap-guided 选择是对该问题的直接回应。arXiv: 2604.13016
3. **Aligning Language Models from User Interactions** (Buening et al., 2026) — 同期工作，用 next-state 信息直接 prompt 在线策略，但 hints 隐含而非显式信号。arXiv: 2603.12273

### 值得阅读

4. **Reinforcement Learning via Self-Distillation** (Hubotter et al., 2026) — 自蒸馏 RL 的理论基础， hindsight relabeling 形式化。arXiv: 2601.20802
5. **DAPO** (Yu et al., 2025a) — 大规模开源 RL 系统，OpenClaw-RL 的 RLVR 实验基于此。arXiv: 2503.14476
6. **DeepSeek-R1** (Guo et al., 2025) — RLVR 推理模型的里程碑，展示纯标量奖励的潜力与局限。arXiv: 2501.12948
7. **slime** (Zhu et al., 2025) — OpenClaw-RL 的基础设施基础，解耦 rollout 和训练引擎。GitHub: THUDM/slime

### 背景补充

8. **GRPO** (Shao et al., 2024) — 无需 critic 的 RL 算法，OpenClaw-RL 混合目标的 RL 分支基础。arXiv: 2402.03300
9. **Math-Shepherd** (Wang et al., 2024) — 自动化步骤级监督，PRM 自动化标注的早期工作。ACL 2024
10. **PPO** (Schulman et al., 2017) — 策略优化基础算法，clipped surrogate 的来源。arXiv: 1707.06347

## 关联

- [[Agent RL]] — OpenClaw-RL 是 Agent RL 的在线实时扩展，将批处理离线范式转为连续在线范式
- [[GRPO]] — 混合目标的 RL 分支基于 GRPO，但扩展了 token-level 蒸馏损失
- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互可形式化为 POMDP，next-state 即为观测 $o_{t+1}$
- [[经验驱动自演化]] — 与 EvolveR 形成互补：EvolveR 是"离线蒸馏 + 在线检索"，OpenClaw-RL 是"在线实时学习"；两者共同指向 Agent 持续自我改进的方向
- [[evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles|EvolveR]] — 经验闭环自演化框架，OpenClaw-RL 可视为其在线实时化的补充
- [[自蒸馏-Agent策略自蒸馏]] — OPD 分支本质是自蒸馏，但增加了 hint-conditioned 和 overlap-guided 选择
- [[Mem0]] — 记忆基线对比，OpenClaw-RL 通过参数更新实现持久学习，Mem0 通过上下文检索
- [[过程奖励模型 (PRM)]] — 本文将 PRM 从离线数学推理扩展到在线异构 Agent 设置

# 混合强化学习（Hybrid RL）

## 定义

混合强化学习是一种将**标量奖励驱动的策略优化**与**token-level 监督信号（如蒸馏损失）**统一在单一更新中的强化学习范式。核心思想是利用两种互补训练信号的各自优势：标量奖励信号频率高但信息稀疏，token-level 信号信息丰富但出现频率低，两者结合实现更高效、更稳定的策略学习。

该概念由 OpenClaw-RL (Wang et al., 2026) 正式提出并系统验证，但其思想根源可追溯至 RL 与模仿学习的结合（如 DQfD、RL+BC）。

## 关键属性

- **双信号源**：
  - **Evaluative signal（评估信号）**：标量反馈，判断动作好坏（如 PRM 投票 $r_t \in \{+1, -1, 0\}$）
  - **Directive signal（指令信号）**：token-level 的纠正/指导信息（如 hint-conditioned teacher 分布）

- **互补性**：
  | 维度 | Evaluative | Directive |
  |------|-----------|-----------|
  | 频率 | 高（每个评分步骤） | 低（仅含可提取纠正的步骤） |
  | 信息密度 | 低（1 标量） | 高（完整 token 分布） |
  | 粒度 | 序列级 | Token-level |

- **统一更新**：两种信号共享同一轨迹，在同一策略更新步骤中联合优化

## 数学形式

在 OpenClaw-RL 中的实现：

对 token $i$ 的混合损失：

$$L_i^{\text{hybrid}} = w_{\text{RL}} L_i^{\text{GRPO}} + w_{\text{OPD}} L_i^{\text{OPD}}$$

其中：
- $L_i^{\text{GRPO}}$：标准 PPO/GRPO clipped surrogate，由标量优势 $A_i^{\text{grpo}}$ 驱动
- $L_i^{\text{OPD}}$：On-policy distillation 损失，由 teacher-student log-prob 差距驱动
- $w_{\text{RL}}, w_{\text{OPD}}$：权重，通常设为 1

## 与相关概念的区别

| 方法 | 信号类型 | 更新方式 | 关键局限 |
|------|---------|---------|---------|
| 纯 RLVR (GRPO/DAPO) | 标量奖励 | 策略梯度 | 无法利用 token-level 指令 |
| 纯 OPD (On-Policy Distillation) | Token-level 蒸馏 | 监督学习 | 稀疏，teacher-student 不匹配 |
| **Hybrid RL** | **两者结合** | **统一更新** | **需要精心设计稳定性机制** |
| RL + BC (如 DQfD) | 奖励 + 演示 | 并行损失 | BC 数据需人工标注，非自动提取 |

## 稳定性挑战与解决方案

Hybrid RL 的核心挑战是 teacher-student 分布不匹配导致的训练不稳定：

1. **Overlap-guided hint selection**：选择使 teacher top-$k$ 与 student top-$k$ 重叠最大的 hint
2. **Log-probability-difference clip**：裁剪 teacher-student log-prob 差距，防止极端更新
3. **Support set 限制**：仅在 student 的高概率词表子集上计算蒸馏损失

## 相关概念

- [[GRPO]] — 混合目标的 RL 分支基础
- [[自蒸馏-Agent策略自蒸馏]] — OPD 分支的理论基础
- [[过程奖励模型 (PRM)]] — Evaluative signal 的提取器
- [[Agent RL]] — Hybrid RL 的应用领域

## 来源

---
id: concept-overlap-guided-hint-selection
date: 2026-05-06
aliases: [Overlap-Guided Hint Selection, OGHS, 重叠引导Hint选择]
tags: [概念]
---

# Overlap-Guided Hint Selection

## 定义

Overlap-Guided Hint Selection（重叠引导 Hint 选择）是一种在 hint-conditioned on-policy distillation 中选择最优纠正 hint 的准则。核心思想是：从候选 hint 中选择那个使得 hint-conditioned teacher 分布与 student 分布的**top-$k$ 词表重叠最大**的 hint，从而最小化 teacher-student 分布不匹配，稳定蒸馏训练。

由 OpenClaw-RL (Wang et al., 2026) 提出，用于解决 on-policy distillation 中 teacher-student 分布 mismatch 导致的训练不稳定问题。

## 问题背景

### On-Policy Distillation 的不稳定性

On-policy distillation 训练 student 在 student 自己的 rollout 上从 teacher 获取 token-level 监督。当 teacher 分布在 student 接近零密度的 token 上放置质量时：
- Off-policy 重要性比率 $\rho$ 爆炸
- 梯度方差增大
- 训练不稳定甚至发散

### Hint 质量的影响

在 hint-conditioned 自蒸馏中，teacher 通过在 prompt 后追加纠正 hint 获得。Hint 的选择直接控制 mismatch 程度：
- 模糊/偏题的 hint $\rightarrow$ teacher 远离 student $\rightarrow$ 不稳定
- 精确/相关的 hint $\rightarrow$ teacher 接近 student $\rightarrow$ 稳定

## 方法

### 符号定义

给定 student 的生成响应 $y = (y_1, \ldots, y_T)$：

- $S_i^q = \text{top-}k\{\pi_{\text{old}}(\cdot | s_t, y_{<i})\}$：student（旧策略）在位置 $i$ 的 top-$k$ 词表
- $S_{i,h}^p = \text{top-}k\{\pi_T(\cdot | s_t^h, y_{<i})\}$：hint $h$ 条件下 teacher 在位置 $i$ 的 top-$k$ 词表

### Overlap Signal

$$O[h, i] = |S_i^q \cap S_{i,h}^p|$$

计数 student 的高概率 token 中有多少在 hint-conditioned teacher 下仍保持高概率。

### 选择方案

**Sequence-level**（序列级，推荐）：

$$h^\star = \arg\max_h \sum_i O[h, i]$$

每个轨迹选择一个最优 hint，在整个序列上最大化总重叠。

**Token-level**（token 级）：

$$h^\star(i) = \arg\max_h O[h, i]$$

每个位置独立选择最优 hint，与 OPD 的 token-level 本质对齐。

### 直觉解释

高 overlap 意味着 teacher 和 student 在"响应应该长什么样"的词汇层面已经达成一致。此时蒸馏可以将 student 推向 teacher，但**是在 student 自己的高密度区域内移动**，而非推向陌生的 token。这保持了 off-policy 重要性比率接近 1，同时保留了 hint 携带的方向性信息。

## 实验验证

在 OpenClaw-RL 中的消融结果：

| 方法 | Student | TA | Teacher | Average |
|------|---------|-----|---------|---------|
| Sequence-optimal | 14.0 | 9.6 | 13.8 | 12.5 |
| Token-optimal | 13.8 | 10.0 | 13.4 | 12.4 |
| Random | 18.6 | 12.6 | 17.0 | 16.1 |

- 两种 optimal 方案性能相近，均显著优于 random
- Sequence-optimal 在 general agentic RL（大 batch）中更稳定

## 与相关方法的区别

| 方法 | 选择依据 | 适用场景 |
|------|---------|---------|
| Random selection | 无 | 基线，不稳定 |
| Teacher confidence | Teacher 分布的熵/峰值 | 未考虑 student 分布 |
| Hint length | 越短越好 | 启发式，无理论保证 |
| **Overlap-guided** | **Top-$k$ 词表交集大小** | **直接最小化 mismatch** |

## 相关概念

- [[混合RL (Hybrid RL)]] — Overlap-guided 是 Hybrid RL 稳定性的核心机制之一
- [[自蒸馏-Agent策略自蒸馏]] — 方法的应用场景
- [[过程奖励模型 (PRM)]] — 负责生成候选 hints

## 来源

- [[openclaw-rl-turning-daily-usage-into-training-signals|OpenClaw-RL]] — 首次提出并验证

## 参见

- [[Log-Probability-Difference Clip]] — 与 overlap-guided 配合使用的另一稳定性机制

input.content:
---
id: concept-log-prob-difference-clip
date: 2026-05-06
aliases: [Log-Probability-Difference Clip, Log-Prob Clip, Δ-clip, Log概率差裁剪]
tags: [概念]
---

# Log-Probability-Difference Clip

## 定义

Log-Probability-Difference Clip（对数概率差裁剪，简称 $\Delta$-clip）是一种在 on-policy distillation 中限制 teacher-student 对数概率差距幅度的机制。通过将 token-level 的 log-prob 差距裁剪到 $[-C, +C]$ 区间，防止极端的 teacher-student 差异导致过大的梯度更新，从而稳定训练。

由 OpenClaw-RL (Wang et al., 2026) 提出，与 overlap-guided hint selection 共同构成稳定的蒸馏训练框架。

## 问题背景

### Hint Conditioning 的极端效应

在 hint-conditioned 自蒸馏中，将纠正 hint 追加到 prompt 后重新评分同一 token 序列，可以产生极端的对数概率偏移。OpenClaw-RL 的实验（图 7(3)）显示：
- $\Delta_t = \log p_{\text{hint}}(x_t | q, h, x_{<t}) - \log p_{\text{base}}(x_t | q, x_{<t})$ 可呈现高度极端的分布
- 某些 token 的 log-prob 可被 hint 推高或压低数个数量级

### 无裁剪的后果

1. **训练不稳定**：极端的 log-prob 差异放大噪声更新
2. **长度失控**：在 outcome-only 监督不足时，平均响应长度持续增长
3. **截断率上升**：最终截断比率可达 0.5（无裁剪）vs 0.2（有裁剪）

## 数学形式

### 定义

$$\Delta_v = \text{clip}\left( \ell_{T,h^\star}(v) - \ell_{\text{old}}(v),\ -C,\ +C \right)$$

其中：
- $\ell_{T,h^\star}(v) = \log \pi_T(v | s_t^{h^\star}, y_{<i})$：hint-conditioned teacher 的 log-prob
- $\ell_{\text{old}}(v) = \log \pi_{\text{old}}(v | s_t, y_{<i})$：student（旧策略）的 log-prob
- $C$：裁剪系数（Personal agent: $C=1$；General RL: $C=2$）

### 蒸馏优势

$$A_v = \Delta_v \cdot w_v$$

其中 $w_v = \text{softmax}_{v \in S_i}(\ell_{\text{old}}(v))$ 将优势集中在 student 高概率 token 上。

### 与 PPO Clip 的关系

蒸馏损失采用双重裁剪：

$$L_i^{\text{OPD}} = \sum_{v \in S_i} \max\left( -A_v \rho_v,\ -A_v \cdot \text{clip}(\rho_v, 1 - \varepsilon_{\text{lo}}, 1 + \varepsilon_{\text{hi}}) \right)$$

- **$\Delta$-clip**：限制优势 $A_v$ 的幅度（teacher-student 差距）
- **PPO clip**：限制比率 $\rho_v$ 的幅度（新旧策略差距）

两者共同约束 surrogate 的两个因子：
1. $\Delta$-clip $\rightarrow$ 限制 $A_v$
2. Overlap-guided $\rightarrow$ 使 $\rho_v$ 接近 1
3. PPO clip $\rightarrow$ 限制 $\rho_v$ 偏离 1 的程度

## 实验验证

### Token-Level Log-Prob Shift 分析

使用 Qwen2.5-7B 生成响应，Qwen2.5-14B 作为 hint-conditioned scorer：
- 评估集：MATH-500 前 32 例
- Hint 类型："太简短，请更具体" 或 "太冗长，请更简洁"
- 结果：$\Delta_t$ 分布呈现高度极端值（图 7(3)）

### 训练动态对比

| 指标 | 有裁剪 (Ours) | 无裁剪 |
|------|--------------|--------|
| 最终截断比率 | 0.2 | 0.5 |
| 响应长度增长 | 受控 | 持续增长 |
| 训练稳定性 | 稳定 | 不稳定 |

## 参数选择

| 场景 | 推荐 $C$ | 理由 |
|------|---------|------|
| Personal agent（实时在线） | 1 | 保守，防止个人使用中的不稳定 |
| General RL（大 batch） | 2 | 允许更大的更新幅度，加速收敛 |

## 与相关机制的区别

| 机制 | 裁剪对象 | 作用 |
|------|---------|------|
| PPO clip | 重要性比率 $\rho$ | 限制新旧策略差异 |
| KL penalty | KL 散度 | 约束策略偏离参考模型 |
| **$\Delta$-clip** | **Log-prob 差距** | **限制 teacher-student 差异** |
| Overlap-guided | Hint 选择 | 从源头减少 mismatch |

四者协同：Overlap-guided 减少 mismatch 来源，$\Delta$-clip 限制剩余 mismatch 的幅度，PPO clip 限制策略更新步长，KL penalty 约束长期漂移。

## 相关概念

- [[Overlap-Guided Hint Selection]] — 与 $\Delta$-clip 配合的 hint 选择机制
- [[混合RL (Hybrid RL)]] — $\Delta$-clip 的应用场景
- [[GRPO]] — PPO clip 的来源算法

## 来源

- [[openclaw-rl-turning-daily-usage-into-training-signals|OpenClaw-RL]] — 首次提出并验证

## 参见

- [[自蒸馏-Agent策略自蒸馏]] — 方法的理论背景

---
id: concept-next-state-signal
date: 2026-05-06
aliases: [Next-State Signal, Next State Signal, 下一状态信号]
tags: [概念]
---

# Next-State Signal（下一状态信号）

## 定义

Next-State Signal 是指在 Agent 执行动作 $a_t$ 后，环境返回的下一个状态 $s_{t+1}$ 中所蕴含的、可用于训练策略模型的信息。在 LLM Agent 的语境下，$s_{t+1}$ 可以是用户回复、工具执行输出、终端状态变化、GUI 状态变化等。该信号包含两种互补的训练信息：evaluative（评估性）和 directive（指令性）。

由 OpenClaw-RL (Wang et al., 2026) 系统性地形式化为在线学习框架的核心概念。

## 形式化

在 POMDP 框架中：
- Agent 在状态 $s_t$ 下执行动作 $a_t \sim \pi_\theta(\cdot | s_t)$
- 环境返回观测 $o_{t+1}$（即 next-state $s_{t+1}$）
- $s_{t+1}$ 包含关于 $a_t$ 质量的丰富信息

## 两种信号类型

### Evaluative Signal（评估信号）

**定义**：从 $s_{t+1}$ 中提取的标量反馈，判断 $a_t$ 的好坏。

**来源示例**：
| Next-State 类型 | Evaluative Signal |
|----------------|-------------------|
| 用户回复"谢谢，很好" | $+1$（正面） |
| 用户回复"不对，你应该..." | $-1$（负面） |
| 用户重述/重问同一问题 | $-1$（暗示未理解） |
| 测试通过 | $+1$（成功） |
| 错误追踪/异常输出 | $-1$（失败） |
| 模糊跟进（无关话题） | $0$（中性） |

**提取方式**：PRM（Process Reward Model）查询 $m$ 次，取多数投票 $r_t \in \{+1, -1, 0\}$。

**特点**：
- 频率高：每个被评分的 turn 都有
- 信息稀疏：整个 turn 压缩为 1 个标量
- 隐式：用户不一定会明确说"好"或"坏"

### Directive Signal（指令信号）

**定义**：从 $s_{t+1}$ 中提取的 token-level 纠正信息，不仅指出 $a_t$ 的错误，还说明如何改正。

**来源示例**：
| Next-State 类型 | Directive Signal |
|----------------|------------------|
| "你应该先检查文件再修改" | Hint: "在修改前先验证文件状态" |
| 错误追踪指向第 42 行 | Hint: "注意边界条件的处理" |
| 用户要求"写得更详细些" | Hint: "增加逐步推理的细节" |
| "谢谢，看起来不错" | 无 directive（纯 evaluative） |
| 测试通过 | 无 directive（纯 evaluative） |

**提取方式**：PRM 判断 $s_{t+1}$ 是否包含有意义的纠正信息。如果是，蒸馏为简洁 hint $h$ 包裹在 `[HINT_START]...[HINT_END]` 中。

**特点**：
- 频率低：仅在有可提取纠正的 turn 上触发
- 信息丰富：完整的下一个 token 分布
- 显式：需要 next-state 包含具体的纠正内容

## 信号互补性

| 维度 | Evaluative | Directive |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| 频率 | 高 | 低 |
| 粒度 | 序列级 | Token-level |
| 信息密度 | 稀疏（1 标量） | 丰富（完整分布） |
| 覆盖范围 | 所有评分 turn | 有纠正内容的 turn |
| 利用方式 | RLVR/GRPO | On-policy distillation |

**关键洞察**：两者单独使用都无法充分利用 $s_{t+1}$ 的全部信息。只有结合使用（Hybrid RL）才能最大化 next-state 信号的价值。

## 与现有概念的关系

### 与传统 RL 观测的区别

传统 RL 中，观测 $o_{t+1}$ 主要用于状态更新，奖励 $r_t$ 由独立的环境奖励函数提供。在 Next-State Signal 框架中：
- $s_{t+1}$ **本身就是奖励信号的来源**
- 无需独立的奖励函数，PRM 从 $s_{t+1}$ 中推断奖励
- 特别适用于无法程序化定义奖励的开放域交互

### 与 Hindsight Experience Replay 的关系

HER 将失败轨迹的目标状态替换为实际达到的状态，使失败变为成功。Next-State Signal 是 HER 思想的在线扩展：
- HER：离线，替换目标
- Next-State Signal：在线，从实际 next-state 中提取纠正信息

### 与 POMDP 的关系

Next-State Signal 框架将 Agent 交互自然地映射到 POMDP：
- 状态 $s_t$：Agent 的历史上下文
- 动作 $a_t$：Agent 的响应/工具调用
- 观测 $o_{t+1}$：next-state（用户回复/工具输出）
- 奖励：从 $o_{t+1}$ 中提取的 evaluative + directive 信号

## 应用场景

| 场景 | Next-State 示例 | 信号类型 |
|------|----------------|---------|
| 个人对话 Agent | 用户回复 | Evaluative + Directive |
| Terminal Agent | stdout/stderr, exit code | Evaluative |
| GUI Agent | 视觉状态差异 | Evaluative + Directive |
| SWE Agent | 测试判决, diff | Evaluative + Directive |
| Tool-call Agent | 工具返回值, 错误追踪 | Evaluative + Directive |

## 提取流程

```
(a_t, s_{t+1})
    ↓
PRM 评估
    ↓
┌─────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐
│ Evaluative      │    │ Directive       │
│ m 次投票        │    │ 是否含纠正？    │
│ 取多数 r_t      │    │ 是 → 蒸馏 hint  │
│ ∈ {+1,-1,0}     │    │ 否 → 无信号     │
└─────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘
    ↓                        ↓
标量奖励              Token-level 监督
    ↓                        ↓
    └────────→ Hybrid RL ←───────┘
```

## 相关概念

- [[混合RL (Hybrid RL)]] — 利用两种信号的统一训练框架
- [[过程奖励模型 (PRM)]] — 信号提取的核心组件
- [[POMDP]] — Next-State Signal 的理论框架
- [[Agent RL]] — 应用场景

## 来源

- [[openclaw-rl-turning-daily-usage-into-training-signals|OpenClaw-RL]] — 首次系统提出

## 参见

- [[经验驱动自演化]] — 另一种利用交互历史的学习范式
- [[Hindsight Relabeling]] — 离线纠正重标注方法

# Gen-Verse

## 概述

Gen-Verse 是一个开源 AI 研究团队/组织，开发了 OpenClaw-RL 框架和 OpenClaw 个人 AI 助手。致力于将 Agent 日常交互转化为在线训练信号，实现 Agent 的持续自我改进。

## 关键事实

- **主要产品**：
  - [[OpenClaw]] — 开源个人 AI 助手（https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw）
  - OpenClaw-RL — 在线 RL 训练框架（https://github.com/Gen-Verse/OpenClaw-RL）
- **核心论文**：OpenClaw-RL: Train Any Agent Simply by Talking (arXiv:2603.10165, 2026)
- **研究方向**：Agent 强化学习、在线学习、个性化 AI、过程奖励模型

## 在来源中的出现

- [[openclaw-rl-turning-daily-usage-into-training-signals|OpenClaw-RL]] — 论文和代码的发布组织

### 创建的知识库页面

**1. 来源摘要页（1个）**

  - 包含完整的基本信息、研究背景、核心方法（含符号表和公式）、实验结果、消融实验、优点与局限、推荐参考文献
  - 与现有知识库页面建立了完整的 [[wikilink]] 交叉引用

**2. 概念页（4个）**
- `wiki/concepts/混合RL-Hybrid-RL.md` — 混合强化学习：统一 evaluative + directive 信号的范式
- `wiki/concepts/Overlap-Guided-Hint-Selection.md` — 重叠引导 Hint 选择：基于 top-k 词表交集的 hint 选择机制
- `wiki/concepts/Log-Probability-Difference-Clip.md` — 对数概率差裁剪：限制 teacher-student 差距的稳定性机制
- `wiki/concepts/Next-State-Signal.md` — 下一状态信号：从交互中提取训练信号的核心概念

**3. 实体页（6个，1个更新）**
- `wiki/entities/Yinjie-Wang.md` — 共同第一作者

- `wiki/entities/OpenClaw.md` — 更新已有页面，添加 OpenClaw-RL 实验关联

### 关键发现
- OpenClaw-RL 的核心创新是**将日常 Agent 交互的 next-state 信号实时转化为在线训练信号**
- **混合 RL 目标**（evaluative + directive）比单一方法效率高 27-65%
- **Overlap-guided hint selection + log-prob clip** 双重机制解决了 teacher-student 不匹配问题
- 约 **10.3 个 session** 即可实现个人偏好对齐
- 首个统一 terminal/GUI/SWE/tool-call 的 RL 训练基础设施

Agent0: Unleashing Self-Evolving Agents from Zero Data
via Tool-Integrated Reasoning
Peng Xia 1 Kaide Zeng 1 Jiaqi Liu 1 Can Qin 2 Fang Wu 3 Yiyang Zhou 1 Caiming Xiong 2 Huaxiu Yao 1
Abstract
Large Language Model (LLM) Agents, often
trained with Reinforcement Learning (RL), are
constrained by a dependency on human-curated
data, limiting scalability and tethering AI to
human knowledge.
Existing self-evolution
frameworks offer an alternative but are typically
restricted by the model’s inherent capabilities and
single-round interactions, hindering the devel-
opment of complex curricula involving tool use
or dynamic reasoning. We introduce Agent0,
a fully autonomous framework that evolves
high-performing agents without external data
through multi-step co-evolution and seamless
tool integration. Agent0 establishes a symbiotic
competition between two agents initialized from
the same base LLM: a curriculum agent that pro-
poses increasingly challenging frontier tasks, and
an executor agent that learns to solve them. We
integrate external tools to enhance the executor’s
problem-solving capacity; this improvement, in
turn, pressures the curriculum agent to construct
more complex, tool-aware tasks.
Through
this iterative process, Agent0 establishes a
self-reinforcing cycle that continuously produces
high-quality curricula.
Empirically, Agent0
substantially
boosts
reasoning
capabilities,
improving the Qwen3-8B-Base model by 18%
on mathematical reasoning and 24% on general
reasoning benchmarks.
Code is available at
https://github.com/aiming-lab/Agent0.

73	Wang et al., 2025d), offering a scalable pathway by en-
74	abling models to autonomously generate their own training
75	data. Yet, despite their potential, existing self-play or self-
76	challenging approaches face severe constraints. First, their
77	capabilities are capped by the model’s inherent knowledge
78	and reasoning abilities (Fang et al., 2025; Cheng et al., 2024;
79	Zhou et al., 2025a), causing the generated tasks to rarely
80	surpass the model’s current complexity (Zhou et al., 2025b),
81	leading to learning stagnation. Second, these frameworks
82	typically operate only in single-round interactions (Li et al.,
83	2025c), failing to capture the dynamic, context-dependent
84	nature of real-world problems. This dual limitation not only
85	restricts the complexity of the self-generated curriculum but,
86	more critically, hinders the model from mastering essential
87	skills that require complex tool use or multi-step reasoning.
88	To address these challenges, as demostrated in Figure 1,
89	we introduce Agent0, a fully autonomous framework de-
90	signed to guide the evolution of agents entirely from scratch.
91	Agent0 completely eliminates the dependence on any ex-
92	ternal data or human annotations, pioneeringly combining
93	tool integration with multi-round co-evolution. The frame-
94	work’s implementation begins with a base LLM from which

130	we initialize two functionally distinct agents: an execu-
131	tor agent and a curriculum agent. These agents co-evolve
132	through a symbiotic competition: the curriculum agent is
133	trained using RL (Shao et al., 2024) to propose frontier
134	tasks that precisely challenge the executor’s current capabil-
135	ities, using the executor’s uncertainty (i.e., self-consistency
136	across multiple answers) and its frequency of tool use as
137	reward signals. Concurrently, the executor agent is trained
138	via RL to successfully solve these tasks, optimizing on a
139	filtered set of challenging problems generated by the frozen
140	curriculum agent and using pseudo-labels derived from its
141	own majority voting. Equipping the executor with a tool
142	enhances its problem-solving abilities, which in turn com-
143	pels the tool-equipped curriculum agent to generate more
144	complex, tool-based curricula. This establishes a virtuous
145	cycle, driving a synchronous spiral of improvement in both
146	agent capability and curriculum complexity. Furthermore,
147	we extend this paradigm to support multi-turn interactions,
148	enabling the generation of context-rich, conversational tasks
149	that better reflect real-world problem-solving.
150	The primary contribution of this paper is Agent0, a novel
151	framework that autonomously evolves LLM agents from
152	scratch through tool-augmented reasoning without relying
153	on any external data. Across ten benchmarks spanning
154	mathematical and general reasoning, empirical results show
155	that Agent0 achieves substantial model agnostic capabil-
156	ity gains, improving mathematical reasoning performance
157	by 18% and general reasoning performance by 24%. In
158	addition, our analysis confirms this gain is driven by our
159	co-evolutionary loop, where the curriculum agent learns to
160	generate progressively complex tasks, creating a virtuous
161	cycle of the executor’s capability improvement.
