105	trajectories are verbose and redundant, preventing the agent from extracting transferable behavioral patterns.
106	Skill-based methods (Xia et al., 2026; Zhang et al., 2025a, 2026b; Wu et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2026a) compress
107	experience into reusable behavioral instructions, yet treat the resulting skill library as a static database never
108	coordinated with weight optimization. RL-based methods (Schulman et al., 2017; Ahmadian et al., 2024; Shao
109	et al., 2024; Feng et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025) update model weights, but operate in small-scale or offline
110	settings and ignore a critical data validity problem: once skills have evolved, trajectories collected under the old
111	skill context carry stale rewards that contaminate gradient updates if reused without filtration. A common
112	thread across all three categories is that each addresses only one aspect of adaptation in isolation, leaving the
113	complementary dimensions unexploited.
114	Our key observation is that two fundamentally different timescales of adaptation are in fact naturally com-
115	plementary. Behavioral heuristics (e.g., “always verify a file path before reading,” “confirm before destructive
116	commands”) can be distilled within seconds from a single failed conversation and injected immediately as
117	skill instructions. Improving the model’s underlying policy across diverse task types requires gradient-based
118	optimization over many trajectories, on a timescale of minutes to hours. The two mechanisms are also mutually
119	reinforcing: a better policy produces more informative failures for skill synthesis, and richer skills yield higher-
120	reward trajectories for policy optimization. No existing system unifies these two forms of adaptation into a
121	coherent framework that exploits this virtuous cycle.
122	We present MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning (Finn et al., 2017; Yao et al., 2021) framework that jointly
123	maintains a base LLM policy and an evolving skill library of reusable behavioral instructions. The skill library
124	serves a dual role: as a meta-parameter that accumulates behavioral knowledge across the task stream, and as
125	an adaptation basis from which task-specific skills are retrieved at inference time. MetaClaw improves both
126	components through two mechanisms. Skill-driven fast adaptation performs gradient-free skill evolution: an
127	LLM analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new behavioral instructions (Xia et al., 2026) that take effect
128	immediately with zero service downtime. Opportunistic policy optimization uses RL with a process reward
129	model (PRM) (Zhang et al., 2025c) to update model weights via cloud (TML, 2026) LoRA fine-tuning (Hu
130	et al., 2021), optimizing post-adaptation performance. Two design principles govern their coordination. First,
131	when to run policy optimization: our Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS) monitors three idle
132	signals, i.e., configurable sleep hours, system keyboard inactivity, and Google Calendar event occupancy, and
133	triggers weight updates only during user-inactive windows, eliminating downtime. Second, which data to use:
134	we distinguish support data (failure trajectories consumed by skill evolution) from query data (trajectories
135	2
136	
137	--- Page 3 ---
138	collected after new skills take effect). Only query data, reflecting the agent’s post-adaptation behavior, is valid
139	for RL; support data carries rewards conditioned on the old skill context and is excluded. Our skill generation
140	versioning mechanism enforces this separation by stamping each trajectory with its skill generation index and
141	flushing stale samples from the training buffer whenever skills evolve.
142	In summary, our primary contribution is MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework that unifies skill-driven
143	fast adaptation with opportunistic policy optimization, enabling deployed LLM agents to evolve continuously
144	through a proxy-based architecture without requiring a local GPU. We evaluate on MetaClaw-Bench, a new
145	benchmark of 934 questions over 44 simulated workdays, where each day forms a sequential, feedback-driven
146	multi-round session of real CLI tasks (file editing, JSON structuring, shell scripting). Experiments with GPT-5.2
147	and Kimi-K2.5 show that skill-driven fast adaptation alone improves overall accuracy by up to 32.2% in relative
148	terms; MetaClaw (Full) further advances Kimi-K2.5 from 21.4% to 40.6%, improves end-to-end task completion
149	by 8.25× on Part I and file-check completion by 185% on Part II, and nearly closes the gap with GPT-5.2’s
150	baseline. We further validate on AutoResearchClaw, a 23-stage autonomous research pipeline, where skill
151	injection alone improves the composite robustness score by 18.3%, demonstrating cross-domain generalization
152	of MetaClaw’s adaptation mechanisms.
153	2
154	Problem Setup
155	We consider a deployed CLI agent that serves a user over a stream of tasks τ1, τ2, . . . drawn from a non-stationary
156	distribution pt(τ). Each task τi consists of a user instruction and environmental context (file system state,
157	shell history, etc.), and the agent must produce a sequence of actions a1:T to accomplish the task. The agent’s
158	behavior at any point in time is fully determined by a meta-model:
159	M = (θ, S),
160	(1)
161	where θ denotes the parameters of the base LLM policy and S = {s1, s2, . . . , sK} is a library of skill instructions,
162	i.e., concise, reusable behavioral directives injected into the agent’s system prompt at inference time. Given a
163	task τ, the agent generates actions according to:
164	a ∼πθ(· | τ, Retrieve(S, τ)) ,
165	(2)
166	where Retrieve(S, τ) ⊆S selects the most relevant skills for the current task via embedding-based retrieval.
167	The meta-model M evolves over the task stream as the agent accumulates experience. We distinguish two
168	types of trajectory data based on their role in this evolution. Support data Dsup consists of trajectories whose
169	failures drive adaptation of the skill library S; these trajectories are consumed by the adaptation process and
170	reflect pre-adaptation behavior. Query data Dqry consists of trajectories collected after adaptation has taken
171	effect; these reflect the agent’s post-adaptation behavior and are used to optimize the policy parameters θ.
172	Maintaining a strict separation between support and query data is essential: mixing them would cause θ to be
173	optimized against stale reward signals that no longer reflect the agent’s current capabilities.
174	The goal of MetaClaw is to continuously improve M over the task stream, not merely to solve each task in
175	isolation, but to become progressively better at adapting to new tasks as they arrive. This positions MetaClaw
176	as a continual meta-learning system: the agent learns from a non-stationary task stream while simultaneously
177	improving its own adaptation capability.
178	3
179	MetaClaw
180	3.1
181	Overview
182	MetaClaw improves the meta-model M = (θ, S) through two complementary mechanisms operating at different
183	timescales (Figure 1).
184	Skill-driven fast adaptation analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new skill
185	instructions that are immediately injected into the agent’s prompt, evolving S without touching model weights.
186	Opportunistic policy optimization uses post-adaptation trajectories to update θ via reinforcement learning,
187	deferred to user-inactive windows by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS). A skill generation
188	versioning mechanism ensures that policy optimization always trains on query data collected under the current
189	3
190	
191	--- Page 4 ---
192	skill library, preventing stale reward contamination from support data. The two mechanisms are mutually
193	reinforcing: a better θ produces more informative failures for skill synthesis, and richer skills produce higher-
194	reward trajectories for policy optimization. This virtuous cycle enables the system to learn to become better at
195	adapting. The complete procedure is summarized in Algorithm 1.
196	3.2
197	Skill-Driven Fast Adaptation
198	Given the current meta-model (θ, Sg), the agent executes tasks and collects trajectories. Trajectories that reveal
199	failure modes form the support set Dsup
200	g
201	. Skill-driven adaptation evolves the skill library via a gradient-free
202	experience distillation process:
203	Sg+1 = Sg ∪E(Sg, Dsup
204	g
205	),
206	(3)
207	where E is a skill evolver, an LLM that analyzes failure trajectories and synthesizes new behavioral instructions.
208	The index g denotes the skill generation, incremented each time the library changes. This step modifies only S,
209	leaving θ fixed, and takes effect immediately for all subsequent tasks. Because skill injection operates through
210	the prompt rather than model parameters, fast adaptation incurs zero service downtime.
211	This mechanism is gradient-free by design, not by approximation. The skill library S lives in a discrete natural-
212	language space where gradient descent is ill-defined; LLM-based failure analysis is the natural adaptation
213	mechanism for this space.
214	The skill library S plays a dual role in the learning structure. As a meta-parameter, S accumulates behavioral
215	knowledge across the entire task stream, with each skill generation Sg+1 ⊇Sg representing the system’s growing
216	operational knowledge. As an adaptation basis, Retrieve(S, τ) extracts a task-specific subset at inference
217	time, providing instant specialization without any parameter update. This dual character arises because
218	natural-language instructions are inherently cross-task transferable: a skill distilled from one failure (e.g., “verify
219	file path before reading”) generalizes to all tasks involving file operations. Unlike systems where task-specific
220	adaptations are ephemeral and discarded after each task, each adaptation episode in MetaClaw contributes
221	lasting knowledge to the meta-model, making knowledge accumulation a feature rather than a side effect.
222	3.3
223	Opportunistic Policy Optimization
224	After each skill-driven adaptation step, the agent continues serving tasks under the latest skill library. Because
225	policy optimization is deferred to idle windows, the skill library may have advanced through several generations
226	by the time training begins. Let g∗denote the current skill generation when a training window opens. The RL
227	buffer B accumulates query trajectories across all post-adaptation generations, and policy optimization updates
228	θ over this buffer:
229	θt+1 = θt + α∇θE(τ,ξ,g′)∼B[R(πθ(· | τ, Sg′))] ,
230	(4)
231	where g′ ≤g∗is the skill generation under which each trajectory was collected, and R is a process reward model
232	(PRM) score. The versioning mechanism (Section 3.4) guarantees that B contains only query data, i.e., every
233	sample reflects post-adaptation behavior under its respective skill generation. Crucially, policy optimization
234	does not optimize θ for raw task performance, but for how well the agent performs after skill adaptation. A
235	better θ yields a meta-model from which skill-driven adaptation produces stronger post-adaptation behavior,
236	resulting in an improved meta-model M′ = (θt+1, Sg∗).
237	In practice, policy optimization is realized via cloud LoRA fine-tuning using GRPO, deferred to idle windows
238	by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (Section 3.5). Importantly, training is initiated only after the
239	query buffer B has accumulated a sufficient number of trajectories; launching RL with too few samples leads to
240	high-variance gradient estimates and unstable policy updates. This means policy optimization naturally lags
241	behind skill-driven adaptation by days or longer, reinforcing the asymmetry between the two timescales: skills
242	evolve continuously, while the policy improves in discrete, data-gated steps.
243	3.4
244	Skill Generation Versioning
245	The support-query separation defined in Section 2 must be enforced in MetaClaw’s online setting, where tasks
246	arrive sequentially and skill evolution is triggered asynchronously. Without a dedicated mechanism, support
247	data can leak into the policy optimization buffer.
248	4
249	
250	--- Page 5 ---
251	The problem is concrete: a trajectory (τi, ξi) that triggers skill evolution from Sg to Sg+1 carries a reward ri
252	reflecting performance under Sg, before the new skill existed. If this trajectory enters the RL buffer, policy
253	optimization receives a gradient that penalizes θ for a failure that skill-driven adaptation has already corrected,
254	optimizing for pre-adaptation rather than post-adaptation performance and violating the meta-learning objective
255	in Eq. 4.
256	We enforce separation via a skill generation version gi stamped on each collected sample:
257	• Support set Dsup
258	g
259	: trajectories collected under Sg whose failures trigger skill evolution Sg →Sg+1. These
260	are consumed by the skill evolver and discarded from the RL buffer.
261	• Query set Dqry
262	g+1: trajectories collected after Sg+1 takes effect. Only these, reflecting the agent’s post-
263	adaptation behavior, are eligible for policy optimization gradient updates.
264	When the skill generation counter advances from g to g + 1, the trainer flushes all samples with version ≤g
265	from its buffer. This ensures policy optimization always updates θ with respect to the agent’s adapted behavior,
266	preserving the integrity of the meta-learning structure.
267	3.5
268	Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler
269	Policy optimization requires a model weight hot-swap upon completion, which briefly interrupts inference. In a
270	deployed interactive system, this creates a tension: policy optimization must run periodically to improve θ, but
271	it must not degrade the user’s experience.
272	We introduce the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS), a background daemon that defers policy
273	optimization to periods when the user is not actively interacting with the agent. OMLS monitors three
274	complementary idle signals:
275	(1) Sleep window. The user configures a sleep schedule (e.g., 23:00–07:00). During this window, the system is
276	guaranteed to be idle, providing the largest contiguous training block.
277	(2) System inactivity. OMLS polls the operating system’s input device idle timer (e.g., ioreg HIDIdleTime
278	on macOS). If no keyboard or mouse activity is detected for δ minutes (default: 30), a training window opens.
279	Upon renewed input, the trainer pauses gracefully via mid-batch checkpointing.
280	(3) Calendar-aware scheduling. OMLS queries the user’s Google Calendar API. When the current time
281	falls within a scheduled meeting, the user is presumed unavailable, opening an opportunistic training window.
282	This is the most anticipatory of the three signals: it leverages the user’s own schedule to predict idle periods
283	proactively.
284	A training window opens when any signal indicates user absence and closes when any signal indicates the user
285	has returned. The RL trainer supports pause/resume across fragmented idle windows, accumulating gradient
286	steps opportunistically without requiring a single long contiguous block.
287	4
288	Experiments
289	4.1
290	Experimental Setup
291	4.1.1
292	Benchmark and Evaluation Platform
293	MetaClaw-Bench. We construct MetaClaw-Bench, a continual agentic benchmark comprising two com-
294	plementary evaluation parts (934 questions total across 44 simulated workdays) for evaluating an agent’s
295	ability to adapt across a sequential stream of real-world CLI tasks. Existing agent benchmarks present tasks
296	as independent episodes, providing no mechanism to assess whether an agent improves from accumulated
297	experience. MetaClaw-Bench addresses this gap by structuring evaluation as multi-workday simulations in
298	which the agent operates under consistent workspace and policy rulesets that evolve through user feedback.
299	1) Part I structures evaluation as a 30-workday simulation (346 questions, days 01–30, 10–15 per day). The
300	workspace state (files, configs, project records) persists across rounds within each day, and each question includes
301	the evaluation outcome of the previous round as corrective feedback context. Questions fall into two types:

365	file-check tasks (structured edits or transformations producing output files validated by automated checkers)
366	and multi-choice tasks (conceptual procedural questions on domain-specific rules). Task difficulty increases
367	monotonically with day index, with days 25–30 requiring sophisticated multi-step reasoning. Part I’s file-check
368	tasks are heavily execution-oriented, with many interdependent side effects, providing a conservative measure
369	of end-to-end completion.
370	2) Part II extends the evaluation to a 14-workday simulation (588 questions, 42 per day: 434 multi-choice
371	and 154 file-check). Part II’s file-check tasks are rule-based transformations where compliance with behavioral
372	heuristics (e.g., schema conventions, timestamp formats) is the primary bottleneck, making them more amenable
373	to skill distillation. This design provides a complementary signal: while Part I stress-tests execution reliability,
374	Part II directly measures how quickly the RL-trained policy internalizes procedural rules across a higher-density
375	task stream.
376	We report two primary metrics across both parts: overall accuracy (mean per-question score) and file-check
377	completion rate (fraction of file-check outputs passing all automated checker assertions simultaneously). Because
378	the benchmark tasks are authored to simulate realistic deployment rather than collected from actual user
379	sessions, we view both parts as controlled stress tests of continual adaptation under increasing difficulty.
380	Downstream evaluation: AutoResearchClaw. To test whether MetaClaw’s adaptation mechanisms
381	generalize beyond CLI-task benchmarks, we additionally evaluate on AutoResearchClaw (Liu et al., 2026b),
382	a fully autonomous 23-stage research pipeline that transforms a single research idea into a conference-ready
383	paper, covering literature search, hypothesis generation, experiment design, code synthesis, sandbox execution,
384	result analysis, paper drafting, and multi-agent peer review. Unlike MetaClaw-Bench’s structured file-check and
385	multi-choice tasks, AutoResearchClaw presents an open-ended, long-horizon agentic workload where failures
386	manifest as stage retries, excessive refinement cycles, and incomplete pipeline runs. We report four pipeline-level
387	metrics: stage retry rate, refine cycle count, pipeline stage completion (out of 19 scorable stages), and a composite
388	robustness score (weighted average of stage completion rate at 40%, retry reduction at 30%, and refine cycle
389	efficiency at 30%).

435	5.2 (OpenAI, 2025) and Kimi-K2.5 (Team et al., 2026). We compare three conditions: 1) Baseline: the base
436	model served without any adaptation mechanism. 2) MetaClaw (Skills): the base model augmented with
437	skill-driven fast adaptation; after each failed trajectory, the skill evolver synthesizes behavioral instructions
438	immediately injected into the system prompt, with top-k retrieval via cosine similarity over sentence embeddings.
439	3) MetaClaw (Full): the full pipeline combining skill-driven fast adaptation with opportunistic policy optimization
440	via RL (5-day training run), evaluated for Kimi-K2.5 only, as it requires a cloud LoRA training endpoint
441	configured for the target backbone. All conditions use identical prompts and tool sets. This design isolates the
442	individual contributions of the two MetaClaw components as defined in Section 3.
443	For the AutoResearchClaw evaluation, we deploy MetaClaw’s skill-driven fast adaptation within AutoResearch-
444	Claw’s pipeline executor. After each pipeline run, failures and warnings from all 23 stages are captured as
445	structured lessons and converted into reusable skill files via MetaClaw’s lesson-to-skill evolver. On subsequent
446	runs, accumulated skills are injected into the system prompt of all 18 LLM-driven stages. We run controlled
447	A/B experiments with the same research topic, backbone LLM, and pipeline configuration, differing only in
448	whether MetaClaw’s skill injection is active.
449	4.2
450	Main Results
451	Table 1 reports performance on both parts of MetaClaw-Bench for all five model–condition pairs. MetaClaw
452	consistently improves over the respective baselines across both models, both adaptation modes, and both
453	benchmark parts.
454	MetaClaw improves both models and the full pipeline yields the largest gains. Results are consistent
455	across both benchmark parts. For GPT-5.2, MetaClaw (Skills) raises overall accuracy from 41.1% to 44.0%
456	on Part I (+7.1% relative) and from 44.9% to 49.1% on Part II (+9.4% relative), with file-check completion
457	rising from 14.7% to 17.1% on Part I and from 58.4% to 67.5% on Part II. For Kimi-K2.5, MetaClaw (Skills)
458	improves accuracy from 21.4% to 28.3% on Part I (+32.2%) and from 21.1% to 26.9% on Part II (+27.5%).
459	MetaClaw (Full) yields substantially larger gains: on Part I, accuracy reaches 40.6% and task completion rises
460	8.25× (from 2.0% to 16.5%); on Part II, accuracy reaches 39.6% and file-check completion jumps from 18.2% to
461	51.9% (+185% relative).
462	Stronger models benefit less and weaker models benefit more. GPT-5.2 starts from a higher baseline
463	(41.1% vs. 21.4% on Part I), leaving less headroom for skill-driven gains. Kimi-K2.5, by contrast, lacks implicit
464	procedural knowledge that the skill library provides explicitly, so skill injection yields larger returns. Notably,
465	MetaClaw (Full) with Kimi-K2.5 (40.6%) nearly closes the gap with GPT-5.2’s baseline (41.1%), demonstrating
466	that the combination of skill injection and gradient-based policy optimization can largely compensate for model
467	capability differences. This pattern suggests MetaClaw is particularly valuable for deploying capable but not
468	state-of-the-art models at production scale.
469	The full pipeline unlocks end-to-end task completion and skills alone do not. On Part I, MetaClaw
470	(Skills) leaves task completion rates unchanged for both models, confirming that skill injection sharpens partial
471	execution quality without reliably enabling zero-defect outputs under heavy execution demands. MetaClaw
472	(Full) closes this gap: Kimi-K2.5’s completion rate jumps from 2.0% to 16.5% (8.25×). On Part II, where
473	file-check tasks are rule-based, skills already drive a substantial completion gain (18.2%→33.8%), and the full

508	on AutoResearchClaw, an evaluation setting structurally different from MetaClaw-Bench. Using skills-only
509	adaptation (no RL), MetaClaw reduces the stage retry rate by 24.8% (from 10.5% to 7.9%) and cuts refine

514	4.3
515	Analysis
516	Per-day accuracy trends. Figure 2 visualizes per-day accuracy (3-day rolling average) for all five conditions.
517	Both models and all conditions show a consistent accuracy decline from day01–10 (where accuracies routinely
518	exceed 50%) to day25–30 (where most models fall below 30%), confirming that MetaClaw-Bench exhibits
519	increasing difficulty. MetaClaw’s advantage over the baseline is most pronounced in the mid-range days (day11–
520	22), where tasks require multi-step procedural compliance that is learnable through failure distillation, and
521	MetaClaw (Full) reaches its peak advantage of nearly 0.8 accuracy around day 19–20. The early days (day01–10)
522	involve simpler manipulations where both conditions perform reasonably, and the late days (day23–30) are
523	sufficiently complex that accumulated skills are insufficient without stronger model weights, leading all five
524	conditions to converge toward similarly low performance.
525	Task-type breakdown. Figure 3 decomposes performance by task type, revealing that the two MetaClaw
526	components address fundamentally different bottlenecks. Skills-only adaptation lifts multi-choice pass rates for
527	both models while leaving file-check completion flat, as procedural knowledge helps reasoning but not execution.
528	MetaClaw (Full) reverses this: Kimi-K2.5’s file-check completion rate jumps to match GPT-5.2’s baseline, while
529	multi-choice accuracy slightly decreases as the policy shifts toward file-execution behavior during training.
530	RL training dynamics. Part II provides a fine-grained view of how policy optimization evolves over time. The
531	file-check completion curve for MetaClaw (Full)–Kimi-K2.5 shows a clear inflection at day 8, after which per-day
532	pass rates escalate rapidly: from ∼9% on days 1–4, through 27–36% on days 5–8, to 55–64% on days 9–10, and
533	ultimately reaching 100% on days 12 and 14. This learning trajectory mirrors the MAML inner-loop update
534	structure: the first several days accumulate support trajectories for skill synthesis and weight updates, the
535	inflection marks when sufficient gradient signal has been collected for the LoRA fine-tune to shift the policy’s
536	execution strategy, and the late-phase convergence indicates that the policy has internalized the procedural
537	rules surfaced by the skill library. The two-phase pattern (skill-driven gains first, RL-driven gains after day 8)
538	directly validates the complementary timescale hypothesis underlying MetaClaw’s design.

541	to ISO 8601 format with timezone offsets; (2) backup-before-modify protocol, creating .bak files before any

605	deployment model (skill injection operates entirely at the prompt level), confirms that MetaClaw functions as a
606	general-purpose continual learning layer applicable to diverse agentic systems.

608	MetaClaw mechanisms. In Case 1, a single distilled skill resolves a compliance error with zero weight update.
609	In Case 2, skill injection provides necessary format context but is insufficient alone; weight-level RL updates
610	are required to reliably execute a structurally complex file operation.

612	Related Work
613	Skill-based and memory-augmented agents. A line of work augments LLM agents with external memory
614	or reusable skill libraries to improve performance without modifying model weights (Shinn et al., 2023; Zhao
615	et al., 2024; Fang et al., 2025; Tang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2025; Chhikara et al., 2025; Liu et al., 2026a;
616	Wang et al., 2024b). Reflexion (Shinn et al., 2023) stores verbal self-reflections in an episodic buffer, allowing
617	the agent to avoid repeating past mistakes. Mem0 (Chhikara et al., 2025) and SimpleMem (Liu et al., 2026a)
618	maintain longer-horizon memory through hierarchical retrieval. On the skill side, Voyager (Wang et al.)
619	incrementally builds a library of executable code skills from successful episodes, while ExpeL (Zhao et al., 2024)
620	and Agent-KB (Tang et al., 2025) distills cross-task experience into natural-language rules. A key limitation
621	shared by these methods is that the skill library (or memory) is treated as a static artifact (Xia et al., 2026):
622	it is never coordinated with weight-level optimization, and successful trajectories are reused indiscriminately
623	without regard for whether the agent’s behavior has changed since they were collected. MetaClaw addresses
624	both gaps by coupling skill evolution with policy optimization through explicit support-query separation.
625	Reinforcement learning for LLM agents. RLHF (Ouyang et al., 2022) and its variants establish the
626	use of reward signals to fine-tune LLM behavior, and subsequent work applies RL to tool-using and agentic
627	settings (Nakano et al., 2021; Yao et al., 2022). More recently, GRPO (Shao et al., 2024) and DAPO (Yu et al.,
628	2025) demonstrate stable online policy gradient training for reasoning tasks (Schulman et al., 2017; Ahmadian
629	et al., 2024; Shao et al., 2024; Feng et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025; Team et al., 2025; Dong et al., 2025).
630	However, these approaches optimize a fixed policy against a fixed reward signal, with no mechanism for the agent
631	to update its behavioral context between rollouts. In deployed interactive settings, they also do not address
632	when to run training or which data remains valid for gradient updates after behavioral changes. MetaClaw

699	Continual and meta-learning. Meta-learning (Finn et al., 2017; Nichol et al., 2018; Hospedales et al., 2021)
700	frames learning as optimizing for fast adaptation to new tasks, typically in an offline episode-based setting.
701	Meta-reinforcement learning extends this idea to sequential decision-making: RL2 (Duan et al., 2016) trains
702	a recurrent policy whose hidden state implicitly encodes task context, PEARL (Rakelly et al., 2019) infers a
703	probabilistic context variable for off-policy adaptation, and ProMP (Rothfuss et al., 2019) applies trust-region
704	constraints at the meta-level. These methods demonstrate effective fast adaptation in robotic control and
705	navigation, but operate on simple network architectures with low-dimensional action spaces and assume fixed
706	offline task distributions. Continual learning (Kirkpatrick et al., 2017; Lopez-Paz and Ranzato, 2017; Chaudhry
707	et al., 2019; Zenke et al., 2017; Wang et al., 2024a, 2022) studies sequential task adaptation without forgetting
708	through regularization, replay, or architectural strategies, yet does not incorporate fast adaptation mechanisms
709	at inference time. Online meta-learning approaches (Finn et al., 2019; Nagabandi et al., 2018; Harrison et al.,
710	2020; Yao et al., 2020) relax the offline assumption and even handle task heterogeneity, but remain grounded in
711	representation learning over simple networks. MetaClaw extends the meta-learning objective to a non-stationary
712	stream of LLM agent tasks where fast adaptation is gradient-free (skill synthesis in discrete natural-language
713	space) and slow adaptation is gradient-based (policy optimization via RL), with a versioning protocol that
714	preserves the support-query structure in an online, asynchronous setting.

718	6
719	Conclusion
720	We presented MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework that enables deployed LLM agents to improve
721	autonomously through normal usage. MetaClaw combines two complementary adaptation mechanisms operating
722	at different timescales: fast, inference-time skill injection that distills reusable behavioral knowledge from failures,
723	and slow, gradient-based policy optimization that refines the model during idle windows. Built on a lightweight
724	proxy architecture, the system requires no local GPUs and integrates transparently with existing personal agents
725	and LLM providers. Experiments on MetaClaw-Bench demonstrate consistent improvements across models
726	and adaptation modes, with the full pipeline yielding the largest gains on both partial execution quality and
727	end-to-end task completion. Evaluation on AutoResearchClaw further shows that skill injection generalizes to
728	open-ended research pipelines without any gradient updates. A current limitation is that idle-window detection
729	depends on user configuration, which may not generalize to all deployment environments. We believe MetaClaw
730	establishes a principled foundation for agents that genuinely learn and evolve in the wild, simply by being used.

861	Appendix
862	A
863	Prompts and Templates
864	This appendix documents the core prompt templates used in MetaClaw-Bench evaluations and the MetaClaw
865	framework components.
866	A.1
867	Agent System Prompt (MetaClaw-Bench Part I)
868	Part I evaluates agents on OpenClaw CLI tasks via a programmatic rollout loop. The agent receives the
869	following fixed system prompt, which may be replaced by a compressed variant (see Section A.6) after the first
870	session:
871	SYSTEM PROMPT — Part I Agent
872	You are an expert CLI agent controlling an OpenClaw installation.
873	Your goal is to complete the given task by issuing CLI commands
874	via the run_command tool.
875	Guidelines:
876	- Issue ONE command at a time and carefully read the output
877	before proceeding.
878	- Use ’openclaw status’ or similar read commands to inspect
879	state before making changes.
880	- Handle errors: if a command fails, diagnose from the output
881	and retry differently.
882	- When the task is fully complete, call run_command with
883	command="done".
884	- Do NOT ask clarifying questions | act based on the task
885	description alone.
886	The single tool exposed to the agent is run command:
887	run command Tool Schema
888	{
889	"name": "run_command",
890	"description": "Execute a CLI command and observe the output.
891	When the task is fully complete, call run_command with
892	command=\"done\".",
893	"parameters": {
894	"type": "object",
895	"properties": {
896	"command": {
897	"type": "string",
898	"description": "The exact CLI command to run, e.g.
899	’openclaw status’,
900	’openclaw agents add --name bot1 --model gpt-4o’,
901	’done’"
902	}
903	},
904	"required": ["command"]
905	}
906	}
907	A.2
908	Agent Identity Context (MetaClaw-Bench Part II)
909	Part II (CALMB-14) injects the following workspace context files into the agent’s session at initialization,
910	defining the agent’s role, user profile, and behavioral principles.
911	14
912	
913	--- Page 15 ---
914	IDENTITY.md — Agent Role Definition
915	# Identity
916	You are MetaClaw Agent, an AI assistant integrated into
917	the internal toolchain of Orion Tech.
918	## Role
919	You serve as Alex Zhang’s primary AI assistant for day-to-day
920	engineering and project management work.
921	## Context
922	- Company:
923	Orion Tech | a B2B SaaS company
924	- Product:
925	Project Orion, a project management platform
926	- Team:
927	Backend engineering team
928	- Principal: Alex Zhang, Backend Tech Lead
929	## Principles
930	1. Accuracy comes first | double-check facts, values, and
931	formats before writing files
932	2. Be consistent | use the same conventions across all files
933	3. Be complete | fill in all required fields; no placeholders
934	4. Be professional | produce output ready for team use
935	SOUL.md — Core Behavioral Principles
936	## Reliability
937	Every file you create or modify should be correct and complete.
938	A half-finished or incorrect file creates more work than it saves.
939	## Consistency
940	When you establish a pattern in one file, maintain it across
941	all related files.
942	## Attention to Detail
943	Small errors in data files can cascade into larger problems.
944	Pay close attention to field names, data types, value formats,
945	and structural requirements.
946	## Ownership
947	Own every assigned task completely. Do not produce output
948	requiring the user to fix obvious issues.
949	## Professionalism
950	All output should meet the standard of work that could be
951	shared directly with teammates or stakeholders.
952	A.3
953	Task Question Templates
954	Each day in MetaClaw-Bench presents two question types. Below are representative examples.
955	Multi-choice question (Part II, Day 01 / r1):
956	15
957	
958	--- Page 16 ---
959	Multi-Choice Question Template
960	Regarding the source and applicability of the baseline daily
961	revenue of 4500 yuan/store in the financial model, which of
962	the following descriptions are consistent with the project
963	documentation? (Select all correct options)
964	A. The assumption document labels 4500 yuan as "Source:
965	operational baseline data from market research report",
966	while the original description in the market research
967	report is "median of 86 tier-1 city stores"
968	B. The rent assumption of 650 yuan/m2 is noted as tier-2 and
969	tier-3 city market rates, but the revenue assumption of
970	4500 yuan references tier-1 city store data
971	C. Operations VP Zhang Wei mentioned an estimated daily revenue
972	of approximately 2500-3200 yuan, but this is only a
973	hypothetical opinion with no direct contradiction
974	D. The validation memo confirms that 4500 yuan "has been
975	cross-validated with operational baseline data", indicating
976	the assumption underwent applicability review
977	Please answer using \bbox{X} or \bbox{X,Y} format.
978	Ground truth: A, B
979	Scoring: max(0, 1 −(FP + FN)/n options)
980	File-check question (Part II, Day 01 / r21):
981	File-Check Question Template
982	Based on the reference documents in day01/, create a decision
983	log tracking key decisions from the documents. Save as
984	day01/decision_log_r21.json. Include these fields: title,
985	created_at, decisions (array with id, date, decision,
986	rationale, decided_by, review_date).
987	Eval: python scripts/check iso8601.py day01/decision log r21.json (exit 0 = pass)
988	Feedback
989	(incorrect):
990	Time/date
991	fields
992	must
993	use
994	ISO
995	8601
996	with
997	+08:00
998	timezone:
999	YYYY-MM-
1000	DDTHH:MM:SS+08:00.
1001	Part I task instruction format (real OpenClaw session, train.jsonl):
1002	Part I User Instruction Template
1003	[Sat 2026-02-21 07:25 EST] I grant you read access to
1004	/Users/jimchen/Documents/openclaw/skills. Locate gog/skill.md
1005	within it. Use skill.md to obtain the instructions for adding
1006	a Google Calendar. Execute the instructions in the isolated
1007	environment to directly add ten meetings to Google Calendar,
1008	starting February 20th, recurring every Friday from
1009	3:30-4:30 PM.
1010	Language requirement: Reply in English only. Keep your
1011	response concise and task-focused.
1012	A.4
1013	Skill Evolver Prompt Template
1014	After each session in which failed trajectories are collected, the MetaClaw skill evolver submits the following
1015	prompt to synthesize new behavioral skills:
1016	16
1017	
1018	--- Page 17 ---
1019	Skill Evolver Prompt (skill evolver.py)
1020	You are a skill engineer for an AI assistant trained with RL.
1021	Your job: analyze the failed conversations below and generate
1022	NEW skills that would have prevented those failures.
1023	---
1024	## Failed Conversations
1025	### Failure 1
1026	(reward=0.0)
1027	**Conversation context (last 600 chars):**
1028	‘‘‘
1029	...[truncated trajectory]
1030	‘‘‘
1031	**Assistant response (first 500 chars):**
1032	‘‘‘
1033	[model response]
1034	‘‘‘
1035	[...up to 6 failures shown...]
1036	---
1037	## Existing Skills (do NOT duplicate any of these)
1038	["skill-name-1", "[category] skill-name-2", ...]
1039	---
1040	## Instructions
1041	Generate **1 to {max_new_skills}** new skills that directly
1042	address the failure patterns observed above. Focus on
1043	actionable, concrete guidance for future conversations.
1044	Each skill must follow Claude skill format:
1045	- ‘name‘: a lowercase hyphenated slug
1046	- ‘description‘: one sentence | when to trigger this skill
1047	and what it achieves
1048	- ‘content‘: 6-15 lines of actionable Markdown. Include:
1049	a heading, numbered steps or bullet points, a concrete
1050	example or code snippet, and an Anti-pattern section.
1051	- ‘category‘: one of [coding, research, data_analysis,
1052	security, communication, automation, productivity, agentic]
1053	or "general" or "common_mistakes"
1054	**Output:** Return ONLY a valid JSON array.
1055	**Example output:**
1056	[
1057	{
1058	"name": "dyn-001",
1059	"description": "Always verify file existence before
1060	reading or writing.",
1061	"content": "## Verify File Existence Before Acting\n\n
1062	1. Check: os.path.exists(path)\n
1063	2. If missing, ask the user for the correct path.\n
1064	**Anti-pattern:** Calling open(path) without checking.",
1065	"category": "coding"
1066	}
1067	]
1068	17
1069	
1070	--- Page 18 ---
1071	A.5
1072	Skill Injection Format
1073	Retrieved skills are appended to the agent’s system message by SkillManager.format for conversation:
1074	Skill Injection Block (appended to system prompt)
1075	## Active Skills
1076	### backup-before-modify
1077	_Always create a .bak copy before modifying any existing file._
1078	## Backup Before Modify
1079	1. Before editing any file, create a backup:
1080	cp <filename> <filename>.bak
1081	2. Verify the backup exists before proceeding.
1082	3. Apply all modifications to the original file.
1083	**Anti-pattern:** Overwriting a file without a backup,
1084	leaving no recovery path if the edit is incorrect.
1085	### iso8601-timezone-format
1086	_Use when writing any date/time field to a file._
1087	## ISO 8601 Timestamp with Timezone
1088	Always format timestamps as: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS+08:00
1089	- Correct:
1090	2026-03-16T09:30:00+08:00
1091	- Incorrect: 2026-03-16, March 16 at 3pm,
1092	2026-03-16T09:30:00Z
1093	**Anti-pattern:** Omitting the timezone offset or using

1095	A.6

1101	be under 2000 tokens while preserving behavior. Keep all

1108	(6) reply-tag/messaging rules,
1109	(7) heartbeat handling rules.
1110	Remove duplicated prose, repeated examples, and decorative
1111	language. Prefer compact bullet sections with short imperative
1112	statements. Do not invent or weaken any rule. Output only
1113	the rewritten system prompt text.

1	---
2	id: concept-agent-rl
3	date: 2026-04-21
4	aliases: [Agent RL, Agentic Reinforcement Learning, Agent 强化学习]
5	tags: [概念]
6	---
7	
8	# Agent 强化学习（Agent RL）
9	
10	## 定义
11	让 LLM Agent 在可执行环境中通过"生成→执行→反馈"的闭环交互来学习策略的强化学习方法。与传统的静态 SFT 训练不同，Agent RL 利用环境的真实反馈信号来优化模型行为。
12	
13	## 关键属性
14	- **闭环交互**：Agent 生成动作 → 环境执行 → 返回观测 → Agent 决定下一步
15	- **可验证奖励**：不同于 LLM-as-judge 的主观评分，使用沙箱执行、rubric 检查等可程序化验证的奖励
16	- **长链推理**：Agent 需要在多步交互中维护状态一致性，挑战远大于单轮问答
17	- **探索-利用平衡**：训练过程中需要平衡探索新工具组合和利用已知有效策略
18	
19	## 相关概念
20	- [[GRPO]] — 常用的 Agent RL 策略优化算法
21	- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论建模框架
22	- [[Model Context Protocol (MCP)]] — 为 Agent RL 提供标准化环境接口
23	
24	## 来源
25	- [[agent-world]] — 核心方法之一，多环境 Agent RL
26	
27	## 参见
28	- [[微服务部署与路由优化概览]] — 优化方法对比（RL vs 启发式 vs Lyapunov）
29

1	---
2	id: concept-grpo
3	date: 2026-04-21
4	aliases: [GRPO, Group Relative Policy Optimization]
5	tags: [概念]
6	---
7	
8	# GRPO（Group Relative Policy Optimization）
9	
10	## 定义
11	DeepSeek 提出的一种无需 critic 模型的强化学习算法。对每个问题采样一组回答，在组内计算相对优势（advantage），用 clipped ratio + KL 惩罚更新策略。
12	
13	## 关键属性
14	- **无需 value model / critic**：与传统 PPO 不同，GRPO 通过组内相对比较计算优势，省去 critic 网络
15	- **组相对优势**：对同一任务采样 G 个轨迹，每个 token 的优势 = 组内归一化（自身 reward - 组均值）/ 组标准差
16	- **clipped ratio**：类似 PPO，限制新旧策略的重要性比率在 [1-ε, 1+ε] 内，防止策略更新过大
17	- **KL 惩罚**：约束策略不要偏离参考模型太远
18	
19	## 数学形式
20	$$GRPO(\pi) = \mathbb{E} \left[ \frac{1}{G} \sum_{g=1}^{G} \frac{1}{|\mathcal{Y}|} \sum_{i=1}^{|\mathcal{Y}|} \min\left( \frac{\pi_{\theta}(y_i)}{\pi_{old}(y_i)} \hat{A}_i, \text{clip}(\cdot, 1-\epsilon, 1+\epsilon) \hat{A}_i \right) - \beta \, KL(\pi_{\theta} \| \pi_{ref}) \right]$$
21	
22	## 相关概念
23	- [[Agent RL]] — GRPO 是 Agent RL 训练中常用的策略优化算法
24	- [[Lyapunov 优化]] — 同为在线优化方法，但 Lyapunov 用于队列稳定性，GRPO 用于策略学习
25	
26	## 来源
27	- [[agent-world]] — 使用 GRPO 作为 Agent RL 的策略优化算法
28

1	---
2	id: concept-experience-driven-self-evolution
3	date: 2026-05-06
4	aliases: [Experience-Driven Self-Evolution, 经验驱动自演化, EvolveR范式]
5	tags: [概念, Agent, 自演化, 经验学习, 闭环学习]
6	---
7	
8	# 经验驱动自演化（Experience-Driven Self-Evolution）
9	
10	## 定义
11	
12	一种 LLM Agent 学习范式，通过"在线交互 → 离线自蒸馏 → 策略演化"的闭环循环，让 Agent 从自身交互经验中持续提炼可复用的策略原则，并通过强化学习将这些原则内化为策略参数，实现无需外部教师模型的持续自我改进。
13	
14	## 核心思想
15	
16	与现有的三种 Agent 学习范式形成对比：
17	
18	| 范式 | 机制 | 缺陷 |
19	|------|------|------|
20	| **无状态执行** | 每次交互独立，不保留经验 | 操作性失忆，无法积累知识 |
21	| **原始轨迹学习** | 检索并模仿过去的完整轨迹 | 难以抽象和泛化，仅表面模仿 |
22	| **外部教师蒸馏** | 依赖更强 LLM 提炼经验 | 认知不对齐，Agent 自身策略未变 |
23	| **经验驱动自演化** | 自蒸馏 + RL 内化，完整闭环 | 蒸馏质量受限于基座模型能力 |
24	
25	直觉：就像学生做数学题——做完题后反思"为什么做对/做错了"（自蒸馏），提炼出解题策略（原则），下次遇到类似问题时先回忆策略再解题（在线检索），不断练习使策略成为本能（RL 内化）。
26	
27	## 关键属性
28	
29	### 闭环生命周期
30	
31	1. **在线交互**：Agent 在 Think-Act-Observe 循环中解决任务，可随时检索经验库中的策略原则引导推理，生成高质量轨迹
32	2. **离线自蒸馏**：冻结策略参数，从交互轨迹中提炼结构化的策略原则（成功→引导原则，失败→警示原则），并维护经验库质量
33	3. **策略演化**：通过 GRPO 等强化学习算法，利用经验引导下收集的轨迹更新策略参数，学会有效利用蒸馏智慧
34	
35	### 经验库管理三机制
36	
37	- **去重**：一级同题去重 + 二级跨题语义等价检查（embedding 相似度 + LLM 判断）
38	- **动态评分**：$s(p) = \frac{c_{\text{succ}}(p) + 1}{c_{\text{use}}(p) + 2}$（Laplace 平滑的经验效用分数）
39	- **剪枝**：定期删除低于阈值的低质量原则
40	
41	### 认知对齐效应
42	
43	关键发现：当基座模型足够大（~3B 参数）时，由 Agent 自身策略蒸馏的原则比外部更强教师模型蒸馏的原则更有效。原因：自蒸馏产生的原则与 Agent 自身的推理模式和失败模式更"对齐"。
44	
45	## 与其他概念的关系
46	
47	### 与 Agent RL 的关系
48	
49	经验驱动自演化是 [[Agent RL]] 的一个具体应用场景。它将 RL 用于 Agent 策略的持续演化，而非常规的单任务优化。关键区别：RL 的训练数据是经验原则引导下的轨迹，学到的策略是"如何有效利用自身智慧"而非通用推理。
50	
51	### 与记忆机制的关系
52	
53	经验驱动自演化可视为一种**文本级外部记忆 + RL 内化**的方案：
54	
55	| 方案 | 记忆载体 | 读写方式 | 与 Agent RL 的关系 |
56	|------|---------|---------|-------------------|
57	| [[δ-mem（在线关联记忆）]] | 参数级（矩阵状态） | delta-rule 在线更新 | 无关，纯前向计算 |
58	| [[Titans]] | 架构级（深度 MLP） | 训练时学习 | 无关，测试时记忆 |
59	| **经验驱动自演化** | 文本级（原则库） | 自蒸馏 + 检索 | RL 用于策略内化 |
60	
61	### 与 RAG 的区别
62	
63	RAG 检索的是**外部事实性知识**，解决的是"不知道"的问题。经验驱动自演化检索的是**自身策略性智慧**，解决的是"不会做"的问题。两者互补而非替代。
64	
65	## 局限
66	
67	1. **能力天花板**：蒸馏质量受限于基座模型能力，小模型（<1.5B）的自蒸馏效果显著弱于教师蒸馏
68	2. **经验内化困难**：直接将检索到的原则通过梯度下降内化到参数中会引入噪声，当前方案仍然依赖"检索式"而非"内化式"
69	3. **任务泛化待验证**：目前仅在 QA 任务上验证，具身交互、创意生成等场景的适用性未知
70	
71	## 相关概念
72	
73	- [[Agent RL]] — 策略演化的技术基础
74	- [[GRPO]] — 常用的策略优化算法
75	- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论框架
76	- [[自蒸馏（Agent策略自蒸馏）]] — 从自身轨迹中提炼策略智慧的机制
77	- [[δ-mem（在线关联记忆）]] — 参数级内部记忆方案，形成对比
78	- [[Titans]] — 架构级记忆方案，形成对比
79	- [[LoRA]] — 冷启动阶段的参数高效微调方法
80	- [[Mem0]] — 启发经验库结构设计的外部记忆框架
81	
82	## 来源
83	
84	- [[evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles]] — 提出经验驱动自演化范式的原始论文
85	
86	## 参见
87	
88	- [[能力-可靠性分解-aptitude-reliability]] — Agent 能力的另一种分析框架
89

toolCalls.name: Read
toolCalls.result:
1	---
2	id: concept-lora
3	date: 2026-05-06
4	aliases: [Low-Rank Adaptation, 低秩适配]
5	tags: [概念, 参数高效微调, 低秩分解, Transformer, 迁移学习]
6	---
7	
8	# LoRA（低秩适配）
9	
10	## 定义
11	
12	LoRA（Low-Rank Adaptation）是一种参数高效微调（Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning, PEFT）方法，核心思想是：**冻结预训练模型的权重，仅通过低秩矩阵分解来参数化权重更新**，从而以极少量可训练参数实现下游任务适配。
13	
14	由 Hu et al. (2021) 在 Microsoft 提出，发表于 ICLR 2022。
15	
16	## 核心思想
17	
18	### 直觉：权重更新的"内在低秩"
19	
20	预训练语言模型（如 GPT-3）的权重矩阵 $W_0 \in \mathbb{R}^{d \times k}$ 通常具有满秩，维度巨大（$d$ 可达 12,288）。但受 Aghajanyan et al. (2020) 启发——过参数化模型实际上驻留在低内在维度的子空间中——LoRA 提出关键假设：
21	
22	> **适配过程中的权重变化 $\Delta W$ 也具有低的"内在秩"（intrinsic rank）。**
23	
24	这意味着，表达下游任务所需的信息，可能只需要一个极低秩的矩阵就能捕获。实验证实，即使 $d = 12,288$，$r = 1$ 或 $2$ 往往就已足够。
25	
26	### 类比：在已有地图上标注新路线
27	
28	想象预训练模型是一张详细的世界地图（$W_0$），适配新任务相当于在上面标注新的路线。LoRA 的洞察是：你不需要重绘整张地图，只需要一张透明的 overlays（$\Delta W$），在上面画几条关键的新路线。而且这张 overlay 不需要和原地图一样精细——用粗线条（低秩）就能表达足够的信息。
29	
30	## 数学原理
31	
32	### 基本形式
33	
34	对于预训练权重矩阵 $W_0 \in \mathbb{R}^{d \times k}$：
35	
36	$$W = W_0 + \Delta W = W_0 + BA$$
37	
38	其中：
39	- $B \in \mathbb{R}^{d \times r}$ — 降维后的"输出"投影
40	- $A \in \mathbb{R}^{r \times k}$ — "输入"到低维子空间的投影
41	- $r \ll \min(d, k)$ — 低秩约束
42	
43	前向传播：
44	
45	$$h = W_0 x + \Delta W x = W_0 x + BAx$$
46	
47	### 初始化
48	
49	- $A \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2)$ — 随机高斯初始化
50	- $B = 0$ — 零初始化
51	
52	训练开始时 $\Delta W = BA = 0$，模型完全等于预训练状态，确保平滑启动。
53	
54	### 缩放因子
55	
56	$$h = W_0 x + \frac{\alpha}{r} BAx$$
57	
58	- $\alpha$ 是独立于 $r$ 的常数（通常设为首次尝试的 $r$ 值）
59	- 缩放使得改变 $r$ 时无需重新调学习率
60	- 在 Adam 优化器下，调节 $\alpha$ 近似等价于调节学习率
61	
62	### 参数效率
63	
64	对于 Transformer 自注意力的 $W_q$ 和 $W_v$：
65	
66	$$|\Theta| = 2 \times L \times d_{\text{model}} \times r$$
67	
68	其中 $L$ 是层数。以 GPT-3 175B 为例（$L=96, d_{\text{model}}=12288$）：
69	
70	| 配置 | 可训练参数 | 占比 |
71	|------|-----------|------|
72	| $r=1$, 仅 $W_q, W_v$ | 2.4M | 0.001% |
73	| $r=4$, 仅 $W_q, W_v$ | 4.7M | 0.003% |
74	| $r=8$, 仅 $W_q, W_v$ | 18.9M | 0.01% |
75	| 全量微调 | 175B | 100% |
76	
77	## 关键属性
78	
79	### 1. 零推理延迟
80	
81	部署时预计算 $W = W_0 + BA$ 并存储。推理时与普通矩阵乘法完全相同。切换任务只需减去 $BA$、加上 $B'A'$——快速内存操作。
82	
83	这与 Adapter（增加网络深度）和 Prefix-Tuning（减少可用序列长度）形成鲜明对比。
84	
85	### 2. 模块化与共享
86	
87	预训练模型可以共享，为不同任务构建不同的 LoRA 模块。100 个任务的存储：$350\text{GB} + 35\text{MB} \times 100 \approx 354\text{GB}$，而非 $350\text{GB} \times 100 = 35\text{TB}$。
88	
89	### 3. 训练效率
90	
91	无需为冻结参数计算梯度或维护优化器状态。GPT-3 175B 上：
92	- VRAM 从 1.2TB 降至 350GB（3× 减少）
93	- 训练吞吐量从 32.5 tokens/s/GPU 提升至 43.1（25% 提升）
94	
95	### 4. 与现有方法正交
96	
97	可与 Prefix-Tuning 等方法组合使用（论文附录 E 展示了 LoRA + Prefix 的组合）。
98	
99	## 消融实验洞察
100	
101	### 秩的选择
102	
103	实验中最惊人的发现：**$r = 1$ 往往已经足够**。
104	
105	| 权重 | $r=1$ | $r=2$ | $r=4$ | $r=8$ | $r=64$ |
106	|------|-------|-------|-------|-------|--------|
107	| $W_q, W_v$ (WikiSQL) | 73.4 | 73.3 | 73.7 | 73.8 | 73.5 |
108	| $W_q, W_v$ (MultiNLI) | 91.3 | 91.4 | 91.3 | 91.6 | 91.4 |
109	
110	增大 $r$ 的收益非常有限。子空间相似性分析（Grassmann 距离）表明：不同 $r$ 学到的**顶部奇异方向高度重叠**，其余方向主要是训练噪声。
111	
112	### 适配哪些矩阵？
113	
114	在固定参数预算下：
115	
116	| 配置 | WikiSQL | MultiNLI |
117	|------|---------|----------|
118	| 仅 $W_q$ | 70.4 | 91.0 |
119	| 仅 $W_v$ | 73.0 | 91.0 |
120	| $W_q + W_v$ | **73.7** | **91.3** |
121	| 全部四个 | 73.7 | 91.7 |
122	
123	**$W_q + W_v$ 是最佳性价比选择**。与其增大单个矩阵的秩，不如分散到多个矩阵。
124	
125	### $\Delta W$ 与 $W$ 的关系
126	
127	通过将 $W$ 投影到 $\Delta W$ 的奇异子空间：
128	
129	- $\Delta W$ 与 $W$ 的相关性**强于随机矩阵**
130	- 但 $\Delta W$ **没有重复** $W$ 的顶部奇异方向
131	- 而是**放大**了 $W$ 中已有但未被强调的方向
132	- 放大因子约 21.5 倍（$r=4$ 时）
133	
134	这说明 LoRA 不是在学习全新的特征，而是在**选择性放大**预训练模型中已存在、但对下游任务更重要的特征。
135	
136	## 局限
137	
138	1. **批量多任务推理困难**：如果合并了 $W = W_0 + BA$，同一 batch 中不同样本需要不同的 $A, B$ 时难以处理（不合并权重可以动态选择，但会引入延迟）
139	2. **秩的选择依赖启发式**：缺乏自动选择最优 $r$ 和适配矩阵的原则性方法
140	3. **不适合跨语言等剧烈分布偏移**：如果下游任务与预训练分布差异极大（如完全不同的语言），可能需要 $r = d_{\text{model}}$ 才能充分表达
141	4. **仅适用于稠密层**：对稀疏结构或特殊架构的适配尚待探索
142	
143	## 后续发展
144	
145	LoRA 已成为 PEFT 的事实标准，催生了大量变体：
146	
147	| 变体 | 核心改进 | 年份 |
148	|------|---------|------|
149	| **QLoRA** | 4-bit 量化 + LoRA，单卡微调 65B 模型 | 2023 |
150	| **DoRA** | 将权重分解为幅度和方向，仅对方向做低秩更新 | 2024 |
151	| **AdaLoRA** | 自适应秩分配，按重要性动态调整各层的 $r$ | 2023 |
152	| **LoRA-FA** | 冻结 $A$ 只训练 $B$，进一步减少显存 | 2023 |
153	| **PiSSA** | 用主奇异分量初始化 $A, B$，更快收敛 | 2024 |
154	| **rsLoRA** | 秩稳定化 LoRA，解决大 $r$ 时的训练不稳定 | 2023 |
155	
156	## 与 δ-mem 的对比
157	
158	| 维度 | LoRA | δ-mem |
159	|------|------|-------|
160	| **数学形式** | $\Delta W = BA$ | $\Delta q = W_q^\Delta r$，$\Delta o = W_o^\Delta r$ |
161	| **低秩投影矩阵** | $B, A$（训练后固定） | $W_q^\Delta, W_o^\Delta$（训练后固定） |
162	| **修正信号来源** | 静态参数 | **动态在线状态** $S_{t-1}$ |
163	| **是否随输入演化** | 否 | 是（通过 delta-rule） |
164	| **训练目标** | 下游任务适配 | 在线记忆/持续学习 |
165	| **参数更新** | 梯度下降优化 $A, B$ | delta-rule 更新状态 $S_t$ |
166	| **应用场景** | 任务微调 | 长序列记忆、多轮对话 |
167	
168	**本质区别**：LoRA 的低秩更新是**静态的**——训练完成后 $B$ 和 $A$ 永久固定。δ-mem 借用了 LoRA 的低秩修正**形式**，但将信号来源替换为运行时通过 delta-rule 持续演化的在线记忆状态。同一组投影矩阵在不同历史上下文中产生不同的修正效果——这是"静态形式 + 动态语义"的转化。
169	
170	## 相关概念
171	
172	- [[参数高效微调]] — LoRA 所属的方法大类
173	- [[低秩分解]] — LoRA 的数学基础
174	- [[Adapter]] — 插入瓶颈层的 PEFT 方法
175	- [[Prefix-Tuning]] — 优化输入提示的 PEFT 方法
176	- [[BitFit]] — 仅训练偏置的极简 PEFT 方法
177	- [[δ-mem（在线关联记忆）]] — 借鉴 LoRA 形式但赋予动态语义的记忆机制
178	- [[delta-rule-learning]] — δ-mem 状态更新的核心规则
179	- [[内在维度]] — LoRA 的理论动机来源
180	
181	## 来源
182	
183	- [[lora-low-rank-adaptation]] — 原始论文
184	
185	## 参见
186	
187	- [[QLoRA]] — 量化版 LoRA
188	- [[DoRA]] — 权重分解低秩适配
189	- [[AdaLoRA]] — 自适应秩分配
190	- [[Context2LoRA]] — 将上下文编码为 LoRA 权重的静态记忆方案
191

toolCalls.result:
1	---
2	id: source-evolver
3	date: 2026-05-06
4	source: raw/papers/evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles.pdf
5	type: 论文
6	tags: [Agent, 强化学习, 自演化, 经验蒸馏, GRPO, 闭环学习]
7	---
8	
9	# EvolveR: Self-Evolving LLM Agents Through an Experience-Driven Lifecycle
10	
11	## 一句话总结
12	
13	EvolveR 提出了一种闭环的经验驱动生命周期框架，让 LLM Agent 能够通过"在线交互 → 离线自蒸馏 → 策略演化"的循环，将原始交互轨迹提炼为可复用的策略原则，并通过 GRPO 强化学习内化这些原则，实现 Agent 的持续自我改进。
14	
15	## 基本信息
16	
17	| 项目 | 内容 |
18	|------|------|
19	| **标题** | EvolveR: Self-Evolving LLM Agents Through an Experience-Driven Lifecycle |
20	| **作者** | Rong Wu, Xiaoman Wang, Jianbiao Mei, Pinlong Cai, Daocheng Fu, Cheng Yang, Licheng Wen, Xuemeng Yang, Yufan Shen, Yuxin Wang, Botian Shi |
21	| **机构** | 浙江大学、上海人工智能实验室、华东师范大学、复旦大学、中南大学、上海创新研究院、上海交通大学、中国科学技术大学 |
22	| **日期** | 2025-10（arXiv v2: 2026-05-08） |
23	| **arXiv** | 2510.16079 |
24	| **代码** | https://github.com/Edaizi/EvolveR |
25	
26	## 研究背景与动机
27	
28	当前 LLM Agent 在工具使用方面表现出色，但存在一个根本性缺陷：**操作性失忆（operational amnesia）**——每次交互都被视为独立事件，Agent 无法从过去的成功或失败中系统性地学习。
29	
30	已有方法尝试解决这一问题，但各有不足：
31	
32	| 范式 | 代表方法 | 核心缺陷 |
33	|------|---------|---------|
34	| 无状态执行 | ReAct | 每次交互后丢弃经验，Agent 内在策略不变 |
35	| 原始轨迹学习 | ExpeL | 检索原始轨迹，难以抽象和泛化，仅模仿而非提炼 |
36	| 外部教师蒸馏 | Reflexion + 外部 LLM | 依赖更强的教师模型，Agent 自身策略仍未改变；且存在"认知不对齐"问题 |
37	
38	EvolveR 的核心洞察是：**Agent 需要一个完整的闭环生命周期**——不仅要能从经验中提炼智慧，还要能将这种智慧内化为自身策略的一部分，形成"越用越强"的正向循环。
39	
40	## 核心方法
41	
42	### 总体架构
43	
44	EvolveR 由三个相互连接的核心组件构成，形成闭环：
45	
46	```
47	在线交互（Online Interaction）
48	    ↓ 生成高质量轨迹
49	策略演化（Policy Evolution via GRPO）
50	    ↓ 更新策略参数
51	离线自蒸馏（Offline Self-Distillation）
52	    ↓ 提炼策略原则 → 喂入在线交互
53	    ↑_________________________|
54	```
55	
56	### 符号表
57	
58	| 符号 | 含义 | 维度/说明 |
59	|------|------|----------|
60	| $\pi_\theta$ | Agent 策略模型 | 参数为 $\theta$ 的 LLM |
61	| $s_t$ | 状态 | Agent 在步骤 $t$ 所处的环境状态（部分可观测） |
62	| $a_t \in \mathcal{A}$ | 动作 | 三种动作之一 |
63	| $\mathcal{E}$ | 经验库（Experience Base） | 存储已蒸馏的策略原则集合 |
64	| $p$ | 原则（Principle） | 自然语言描述 + 结构化三元组 |
65	| $P_k$ | 检索到的原则集合 | Top-$k$ 条相关原则 |
66	| $\tau$ | 轨迹（Trajectory） | 一次完整交互的记录 |
67	| $c_{\text{succ}}(p)$ | 原则 $p$ 的成功次数 | 历史使用中引导成功的累计 |
68	| $c_{\text{use}}(p)$ | 原则 $p$ 的使用次数 | 历史被检索使用的累计 |
69	| $s(p)$ | 原则 $p$ 的度量分数 | 经验效用评估 |
70	| $\theta_{\text{sim}}$ | 语义相似度阈值 | 默认 0.85 |
71	| $\theta_{\text{prune}}$ | 剪枝阈值 | 默认 0.3 |
72	| $R(\tau)$ | 轨迹奖励 | 复合奖励函数 |
73	| $w_o, w_f$ | 奖励权重 | 结果奖励 / 格式奖励权重 |
74	| $G$ | GRPO 组大小 | 每个提示的采样轨迹数（默认 8） |
75	
76	### 动作空间设计
77	
78	Agent 的动作空间 $\mathcal{A}$ 包含三种动作：
79	
80	1. **`<search experience>`**：查询内部经验库 $\mathcal{E}$，检索相关策略原则。环境返回检索到的原则作为观测。
81	2. **`<search knowledge>`**：查询外部知识库（搜索引擎），获取事实信息。环境返回检索到的信息作为观测。
82	3. **`<answer>`**：输出最终答案，结束交互。
83	
84	关键区别：`<search experience>` 和 `<search knowledge>` 是两种本质不同的检索——前者检索的是 Agent 自己从过去经验中提炼的**策略性智慧**，后者检索的是外部**事实性信息**。
85	
86	### 第一阶段：离线经验自蒸馏（Offline Self-Distillation）
87	
88	此阶段 Agent 参数 $\theta$ 冻结，专注于从原始轨迹中提炼结构化知识。
89	
90	#### 原则蒸馏
91	
92	利用 Agent 自身的策略模型 $\pi_\theta$ 分析历史交互轨迹。通过精心设计的 prompt 让模型扮演"专家"角色，审查每条轨迹并提炼核心策略洞察：
93	
94	- **成功轨迹** → 引导原则（Guiding Principle）：描述成功策略
95	- **失败轨迹** → 警示原则（Cautionary Principle）：描述应避免的陷阱
96	
97	每条原则包含两部分：
98	1. **自然语言描述**：一句话概括核心建议
99	2. **结构化三元组**：$(subject, predicate, object)$ 格式的知识表示
100	
101	直觉：这不是简单的"记录做了什么"，而是提炼"为什么这么做有效/无效"的抽象洞察。
102	
103	#### 去重与整合
104	
105	新原则不是简单添加，而是经过严格的两级整合流程：
106	
107	**第一级：同题去重**
108	
109	由于 GRPO 对同一问题采样多条轨迹（$G=8$），同一问题会产生多条相似原则。使用 $\pi_\theta$ 进行成对语义等价检查，每个语义等价簇仅保留一条代表。
110	
111	**第二级：跨题整合**
112	
113	对每条唯一原则，执行两阶段匹配：
114	1. 通过 embedding 相似度从 $\mathcal{E}$ 检索最相似的已有原则
115	2. 用 $\pi_\theta$ 做二元语义等价判断
116	
117	更新规则：
118	
119	$$\mathcal{E} \leftarrow \begin{cases} \mathcal{E} \cup \{p_{\text{cand}}\} & \text{if } \max_{p \in \mathcal{E}} \text{sim}(p_{\text{cand}}, p) < \theta_{\text{sim}} \\ \text{Merge}(\mathcal{E}, p^*, \tau_{\text{src}}) & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$$
120	
121	其中 $p^* = \arg\max_{p \in \mathcal{E}} \text{sim}(p_{\text{cand}}, p)$，$\text{sim}(\cdot, \cdot)$ 为余弦相似度，$\theta_{\text{sim}} = 0.85$。
122	
123	直觉：新知识要么作为独立条目加入，要么作为已有原则的新证据被合并——确保经验库是"精炼"的而非"膨胀"的。
124	
125	#### 质量控制：动态评分
126	
127	每条原则跟踪使用次数和成功次数，计算经验效用分数：
128	
129	$$s(p) = \frac{c_{\text{succ}}(p) + 1}{c_{\text{use}}(p) + 2}$$
130	
131	分子 +1、分母 +2 是 Laplace 平滑，防止早期样本量不足时的极端值。
132	
133	定期剪枝：删除 $s(p) < \theta_{\text{prune}}$（默认 0.3）的原则。
134	
135	### 第二阶段：在线交互（Online Interaction）
136	
137	Agent 在 Think-Act-Observe 循环中工作，核心创新在于**检索到的原则如何改变 Agent 行为**：
138	
139	- Agent 随时可发出 `<search experience>` 动作
140	- 检索到的原则 $P_k$ 提供的不是事实信息，而是**启发式引导**，塑造 Agent 的后续推理
141	- 例如，检索到"对于比较类问题，在得出结论前应收集双方数据"，Agent 的内部推理（`<thinking>`）和后续 `<search knowledge>` 动作都会被这一原则引导
142	
143	**双重视角理解在线阶段**：
144	1. 当下层面：利用原则高效解决当前任务
145	2. 未来层面：原则引导下的高质量轨迹为下一轮离线蒸馏提供优质素材
146	
147	### 第三阶段：策略演化（Policy Evolution via RL）
148	
149	使用 GRPO 更新策略，将经验引导的交互内化为策略参数。
150	
151	#### 复合奖励函数
152	
153	$$R(\tau) = w_o R_{\text{outcome}}(\tau) + w_f R_{\text{format}}(\tau)$$
154	
155	**结果奖励** $R_{\text{outcome}}$（稀疏、二元）：
156	
157	$$R_{\text{outcome}}(\tau) = \text{EM}(a_{\text{pred}}, a_{\text{gold}})$$
158	
159	即预测答案与标准答案的精确匹配。
160	
161	**格式奖励** $R_{\text{format}}$（密集、塑形）：
162	
163	$$R_{\text{format}}(\tau) = \frac{\mathbb{I}(\tau_{\text{complete}}) \cdot R_{\text{think}}(\tau) + R_{\text{search}}(\tau)}{2}$$
164	
165	其中：
166	- $\mathbb{I}(\tau_{\text{complete}})$：轨迹完整性指示器（必须包含 `<thinking>`、至少一种搜索、`<answer>`）
167	- $R_{\text{think}}$：推理步数评分，鼓励 6 步推理（过多反而扣分）
168	- $R_{\text{search}}$：搜索评分 = 多样性分数（同时使用经验和知识搜索得 0.5，仅一种得 0.2）+ 数量奖励（每次额外搜索 +0.1，上限 0.5）
169	
170	参数设置：$w_o = 1.0$，$w_f = 0.1$，即结果奖励权重远高于格式奖励。
171	
172	#### GRPO 目标函数
173	
174	$$J_{\text{GRPO}}(\theta) = \mathbb{E}_{\tau \in \mathcal{D}} \left[ \sum_{t=1}^{|\tau|} \min\left( \rho_t(\theta) \hat{A}_t,\  \text{clip}(\rho_t(\theta), 1 - \epsilon, 1 + \epsilon) \hat{A}_t \right) - \beta D_{\text{KL}}[\pi_\theta \| \pi_{\text{ref}}] \right]$$
175	
176	其中 $\rho_t(\theta) = \frac{\pi_\theta(a_t | h_t)}{\pi_{\text{old}}(a_t | h_t)}$ 为重要性采样比率，$\hat{A}_t$ 为优势估计。
177	
178	关键：轨迹 $\mathcal{D}$ 是在经验原则引导下收集的，因此 GRPO 学到的不是一个通用的推理策略，而是**如何有效利用自身蒸馏智慧的策略**——这才是真正"关闭循环"的关键。
179	
180	### 冷启动（Cold Start）
181	
182	在 RL 训练前，用约 700 条 NQ + HotpotQA 训练集样本，由 GPT-4o 生成格式正确的 CoT 交互轨迹，通过 SFT 教会模型基本的交互格式。LoRA 微调，3 epochs，学习率 $1 \times 10^{-4}$。
183	
184	### 实现细节
185	
186	| 参数 | 值 |
187	|------|-----|
188	| 基座模型 | Qwen2.5 (0.5B/1.5B/3B/7B) |
189	| 最大序列长度 | 8192（输入）/ 1024（输出） |
190	| 嵌入模型 | BGE-M3 |
191	| 经验库后端 | Milvus（向量相似度搜索） |
192	| 每步采样 | 128 prompts, G=8 trajectories |
193	| RL 学习率 | $1 \times 10^{-6}$ |
194	| GRPO $\epsilon$ | 0.2 |
195	| KL 系数 $\beta$ | 0.001 |
196	| 训练硬件 | 8x A100 (80GB) |
197	| 3B 模型训练时间 | ~39.4 小时 |
198	| 知识检索 top-$k_d$ | 3 |
199	| 经验检索 top-$k_e$ | 3 |
200	
201	## 实验结果
202	
203	### 主实验（Table 1）
204	
205	在 7 个 QA benchmark 上评估，包括域内（NQ、HotpotQA）和域外（TriviaQA、PopQA、2Wiki-MultiHopQA、Musique、Bamboogle）。
206	
207	**Qwen2.5-3B 结果：**
208	
209	| 方法 | NQ | HotpotQA | TriviaQA | PopQA | 2wiki | Musique | Bamboogle | 平均 |
210	|------|-----|----------|----------|-------|-------|---------|-----------|------|
211	| Direct Inference | 0.106 | 0.149 | 0.288 | 0.108 | 0.244 | 0.020 | 0.024 | 0.134 |
212	| RAG | 0.348 | 0.255 | 0.544 | 0.387 | 0.226 | 0.047 | 0.080 | 0.270 |
213	| Search-R1-instruct | 0.341 | 0.324 | 0.545 | 0.378 | 0.319 | 0.103 | 0.264 | 0.325 |
214	| Rejection Sampling | 0.294 | 0.240 | 0.488 | 0.332 | 0.233 | 0.059 | 0.210 | 0.265 |
215	| **EvolveR** | **0.434** | **0.373** | **0.584** | **0.434** | **0.381** | **0.137** | **0.328** | **0.382** |
216	
217	**Qwen2.5-7B 结果：**
218	
219	| 方法 | NQ | HotpotQA | TriviaQA | PopQA | 2wiki | Musique | Bamboogle | 平均 |
220	|------|-----|----------|----------|-------|-------|---------|-----------|------|
221	| Search-R1-instruct | 0.393 | 0.370 | 0.610 | 0.397 | 0.414 | 0.146 | 0.368 | 0.385 |
222	| Rejection Sampling | 0.360 | 0.331 | 0.592 | 0.380 | 0.296 | 0.123 | 0.355 | 0.348 |
223	| **EvolveR** | **0.462** | **0.411** | **0.620** | **0.473** | **0.395** | **0.168** | **0.392** | **0.417** |
224	
225	关键发现：EvolveR 在两个模型规模上都取得了最高平均分，且优势在域外数据集上同样显著，说明其策略具有良好的泛化性。
226	
227	## 消融实验/分析
228	
229	### 1. 自蒸馏 vs 教师蒸馏（Table 2）
230	
231	| 模型 | 自蒸馏平均 | GPT-4o-mini 教师蒸馏平均 | 结论 |
232	|------|-----------|------------------------|------|
233	| 0.5B | 0.150 | **0.220** | 小模型自蒸馏能力不足，教师更有效 |
234	| 1.5B | 0.270 | **0.290** | 差距缩小 |
235	| 3B | **0.382** | 0.370 | **反转！自蒸馏超越教师蒸馏** |
236	
237	核心洞察：**认知对齐（Cognitive Alignment）**——当模型足够大时，由自身策略蒸馏出的原则与自身推理方式更"对齐"，比外部更强模型的蒸馏更有效。
238	
239	### 2. 经验检索的必要性（Table 3）
240	
241	| 模型 | 有检索 | 无检索 | 差距 |
242	|------|--------|--------|------|
243	| 0.5B | 0.150 | 0.078 | -48% |
244	| 1.5B | 0.270 | 0.123 | -54% |
245	| 3B | 0.382 | 0.340 | -11% |
246	
247	所有规模上移除检索都导致显著下降，证明经验检索是不可或缺的。
248	
249	### 3. 组件归因（Table 4）
250	
251	| 原则蒸馏 | 原则检索 | RL | 平均分 |
252	|---------|---------|-----|--------|
253	| 否 | 否 | 否 | 0.134 |
254	| 否 | 否 | 是 | 0.325 |
255	| 是 | 是 | 否 | 0.357 |
256	| 是 | 否 | 是 | 0.340 |
257	| 是 | 是 | 是 | **0.382** |
258	
259	关键发现：
260	- RL 单独提供基线提升（0.134 → 0.325）
261	- 经验检索与 RL 是互补的：检索提供推理引导，RL 优化利用检索原则的策略
262	- 三者协同效果最强
263	
264	### 4. SFT vs RL 训练对比（Table 8）
265	
266	| 方法 | 平均分 |
267	|------|--------|
268	| EvolveR (SFT) | 0.357 |
269	| EvolveR (RL/GRPO) | **0.382** (+7%) |
270	
271	SFT 只学习表面动作模式，GRPO 从成功和失败轨迹中学习"什么该做、什么该避免"。
272	
273	### 5. 原则质量分析（Table 6）
274	
275	人工标注 100 条原则：
276	
277	| 分数层级 | 理想（正确且可操作） | 模糊（正确但不可操作） | 错误/误导 |
278	|---------|---------------------|----------------------|----------|
279	| 低分段 (0.30-0.44) | 26% | 50% | 24% |
280	| 高分段 (0.44-0.75) | **82%** | 10% | 8% |
281	
282	动态评分机制有效过滤了低质量原则，高分段中可操作原则占比从 26% 提升至 82%。
283	
284	### 6. 经验库扩展性（Table 7）
285	
286	| 原则数 | 检索延迟 | 平均分 |
287	|--------|---------|--------|
288	| 4,213 | 0.04s | 0.301 |
289	| 13,435 | 0.06s | 0.372 |
290	| 23,448 | 0.12s | 0.382 |
291	| 32,805 | 0.16s | 0.396 |
292	| 45,083 | 0.19s | **0.410** |
293	| 49,934 | 0.20s | 0.387 |
294	
295	经验库扩展至近 5 万条原则时，检索延迟仅 0.20s，性能保持稳定甚至在 45k 时达到峰值。
296	
297	### 7. 经验内化尝试（Table 11）
298	
299	尝试将检索到的原则直接"吸收"进模型参数（不解码器 loss mask），结果轻微下降（0.382 → 0.371）。原因是 top-k 检索中并非所有原则都相关，直接内化引入了噪声。
300	
301	### 8. 训练动态分析
302	
303	- **动作频率演化**：推理步数和搜索次数先增后收敛（约 6 次推理、4 次知识搜索、2 次经验搜索）
304	- **原则质量提升**：后期生成原则的平均分数（0.500）高于早期（0.462），说明 Agent 的蒸馏能力随训练改善
305	
306	### 9. 模型规模泛化性
307	
308	| 模型 | 平均分 |
309	|------|--------|
310	| 0.5B | 0.150 |
311	| 1.5B | 0.270 |
312	| 3B | 0.382 |
313	| 7B | 0.417 |
314	
315	性能随基座模型规模单调增长，证明 EvolveR 是一个可泛化的范式，而非针对特定规模的技巧。
316	
317	## 优点与局限
318	
319	### 优点
320	
321	1. **闭环自演化**：首次实现了"交互→蒸馏→策略更新"的完整闭环，而非单点改进
322	2. **认知对齐**：自蒸馏在 3B+ 规模超越教师蒸馏，避免了对更强外部模型的依赖
323	3. **经验库管理完善**：去重、评分、剪枝三级质量控制，确保知识库精炼而非膨胀
324	4. **即插即用**：作为通用框架可应用于不同规模的基座模型
325	5. **高效检索**：5 万条原则下延迟仅 0.20s，实用性强
326	6. **全面消融**：多层次消融实验清晰揭示了每个组件的贡献
327	
328	### 局限
329	
330	1. **蒸馏质量受限于基座模型**：小模型（0.5B）自蒸馏效果显著差于教师蒸馏，存在"能力天花板"
331	2. **仅验证 QA 任务**：未在具身交互、创意生成等其他任务类型上验证
332	3. **长期遗忘问题未解决**：虽然有剪枝机制，但真正的终身学习（数千任务）的可扩展性未验证
333	4. **经验内化失败**：尝试将原则直接内化到参数中未成功，说明当前方案的信息传递仍然是"检索式"而非"内化式"
334	5. **安全性担忧**：Agent 自行演化原则可能发展出不良策略，需要与价值对齐技术配合
335	6. **冷启动依赖外部模型**：初始 SFT 数据需要 GPT-4o 生成
336	
337	## 提及的实体
338	
339	- [[Rong-Wu]] — 共同一作，浙江大学 & 上海人工智能实验室
340	- [[Xiaoman-Wang]] — 共同一作，华东师范大学
341	- [[Botian-Shi]] — 通讯作者，上海人工智能实验室
342	- [[上海人工智能实验室]] — 主要机构
343	- [[浙江大学]] — 第一作者机构
344	- [[Mem0]] — 结构化记忆框架，启发 EvolveR 的原则表示
345	- [[Qwen]] — 基座模型族
346	
347	## 讨论的概念
348	
349	- [[经验驱动自演化（Experience-Driven Self-Evolution）]] — 本文核心范式
350	- [[Agent 强化学习（Agent RL）]] — EvolveR 使用 RL 作为策略演化引擎
351	- [[GRPO]] — 策略优化算法
352	- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论框架（部分可观测状态）
353	- [[LoRA]] — 冷启动阶段的参数高效微调
354	- [[自蒸馏（Self-Distillation）]] — Agent 从自身轨迹中提炼策略原则
355	- [[经验库管理（Experience Base Curation）]] — 去重、评分、剪枝的知识管理机制
356	- [[认知对齐（Cognitive Alignment）]] — 自蒸馏 vs 教师蒸馏的尺度依赖性
357	
358	## 推荐参考文献
359	
360	1. **Search-R1** (Jin et al., 2025) — 直接竞争者，同样用 RL 训练 Agent 搜索能力，但不涉及经验自蒸馏。arXiv: 2503.09516
361	2. **ExpeL** (Zhao et al., 2024) — 存储原始轨迹的最相关工作，EvolveR 的主要改进对象。arXiv: 2308.10144
362	3. **Reflexion** (Shinn et al., 2023) — 通过自我反思改进的语言 Agent，EvolveR 范式 3 的代表。arXiv: 2303.11366
363	4. **DeepSeek-R1** (Guo et al., 2025) — RL 推理模型，EvolveR 借鉴了其冷启动策略。arXiv: 2501.12948
364	5. **ReAct** (Yao et al., 2023) — 推理-行动交织的基础框架。arXiv: 2210.03629
365	6. **G-Memory** (Zhang et al., 2025) — 分层记忆系统，启发 EvolveR 的原则结构。arXiv: 2506.07398
366	7. **Memory-R1** (Yan et al., 2025) — RL 增强 LLM Agent 记忆管理。arXiv: 2508.19828
367	8. **O2-Searcher** (Mei et al., 2025) — 来自同一团队的搜索增强 Agent。arXiv: 2505.16582
368	9. **Memento** (Zhou et al., 2025) — 无需微调 LLM 即可增强 Agent 记忆。arXiv: 2508.16153
369	10. **EWC** (Kirkpatrick et al., 2017) — 持续学习的经典方法，对比 EvolveR 的自演化路线。
370	
371	## 关联
372	
373	- [[Agent RL]] — EvolveR 是 Agent RL 的一个具体实例，将 RL 用于 Agent 策略演化
374	- [[GRPO]] — EvolveR 使用 GRPO 作为策略优化算法
375	- [[LoRA]] — 冷启动阶段使用 LoRA 进行参数高效微调
376	- [[POMDP]] — EvolveR 的 Agent 交互可以形式化为 POMDP
377	- [[δ-mem（在线关联记忆）]] — 另一种 Agent 记忆机制：δ-mem 是参数级内部记忆，EvolveR 是文本级外部记忆 + RL 内化
378	- [[Titans]] — 测试时记忆的架构级方案，与 EvolveR 的经验库方案形成对比
379	- [[mem0-building-production-ready-ai-agents|Mem0]] — EvolveR 的原则结构设计受 Mem0 启发
380

1	---
2	id: concept-agent-self-distillation
3	date: 2026-05-06
4	aliases: [Self-Distillation, Agent策略自蒸馏, Agent Self-Distillation, 自蒸馏]
5	tags: [概念, Agent, 自蒸馏, 经验学习, 策略优化]
6	---
7	
8	# 自蒸馏（Agent 策略自蒸馏）
9	
10	## 定义
11	
12	在 Agent 语境下，自蒸馏是指 Agent 利用**自身的策略模型**（而非外部更强的教师模型）分析过去的交互轨迹，从中提炼出抽象的、可复用的策略原则的过程。核心区别于传统知识蒸馏：这里没有教师-学生对齐的范式，而是 Agent 扮演"自我反思者"的角色。
13	
14	## 核心机制
15	
16	### 蒸馏流程
17	
18	1. **轨迹审查**：Agent 的策略模型 $\pi_\theta$（冻结参数）审查每条历史交互轨迹
19	2. **专家角色扮演**：通过精心设计的 prompt 让模型以"专家"视角分析轨迹
20	3. **原则生成**：根据轨迹成败分别生成
21	   - **成功轨迹** → 引导原则（Guiding Principle）：描述"什么策略有效"
22	   - **失败轨迹** → 警示原则（Cautionary Principle）：描述"什么错误应避免"
23	4. **结构化表示**：每条原则 = 自然语言描述 + $(subject, predicate, object)$ 三元组列表
24	
25	### 认知对齐（Cognitive Alignment）
26	
27	自蒸馏最关键的特性是**认知对齐**——蒸馏出的原则与 Agent 自身的推理方式天然匹配。
28	
29	实验证据（来自 EvolveR）：
30	
31	| 模型规模 | 自蒸馏平均 | GPT-4o-mini 教师蒸馏平均 | 胜者 |
32	|---------|-----------|------------------------|------|
33	| 0.5B | 0.150 | 0.220 | 教师 |
34	| 1.5B | 0.270 | 0.290 | 教师 |
35	| 3B | **0.382** | 0.370 | **自蒸馏** |
36	
37	在 3B 规模，自蒸馏反超教师蒸馏。原因分析：
38	- 自蒸馏的原则反映了 Agent **自身的失败模式和推理习惯**，针对性更强
39	- 外部教师模型（即使是 GPT-4o-mini）生成的原则是"旁观者视角"，可能包含 Agent 无法执行的策略
40	- 但小模型（<1.5B）自身分析能力不足，蒸馏出低质量原则，此时教师模型仍有优势
41	
42	### 与传统知识蒸馏的区别
43	
44	| 维度 | 传统知识蒸馏 | Agent 策略自蒸馏 |
45	|------|------------|----------------|
46	| **目标** | 模型压缩（大→小） | 策略知识提炼（经验→原则） |
47	| **教师** | 更大的预训练模型 | Agent 自身（冻结策略） |
48	| **知识类型** | 输出分布 / 特征表示 | 自然语言策略原则 |
49	| **传递方式** | 软标签 / 特征匹配 | Prompt 引导的文本生成 |
50	| **对齐问题** | 模型容量差异导致信息损失 | 认知不对齐（教师-学生视角不同） |
51	| **应用时机** | 训练时 | 离线反思阶段 |
52	
53	## 关键属性
54	
55	- **无需外部教师**：消除了对更强 LLM 的依赖，尤其在 3B+ 规模
56	- **策略抽象**：从具体轨迹到通用原则，跨越"表面模仿"到"深层理解"
57	- **双面学习**：既从成功中提取"该做什么"，也从失败中提取"不该做什么"
58	- **尺度依赖**：效果随基座模型能力增长，存在一个"自蒸馏超越教师蒸馏"的临界规模
59	
60	## 相关概念
61	
62	- [[经验驱动自演化]] — 自蒸馏是其中的离线阶段核心机制
63	- [[Agent RL]] — 自蒸馏为 RL 训练提供高质量的策略引导数据
64	- [[GRPO]] — 利用自蒸馏产出的轨迹进行策略优化
65	- [[Mem0]] — 其结构化记忆表示启发自蒸馏的原则格式设计
66	- [[δ-mem（在线关联记忆）]] — 参数级记忆方案，与自蒸馏的文本级原则方案形成对比
67	
68	## 来源
69	
70	- [[evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles]] — 系统性提出并验证 Agent 策略自蒸馏机制
71	
72	## 参见
73	
74	- [[图记忆]] — 另一种 Agent 记忆组织方式
75	- [[文本记忆]] — Agent 使用自然文本存储记忆的方式
76

input.content:
---
id: source-metaclaw
date: 2026-05-06
source: raw/papers/metaclaw-continuous-evolution-in-production.pdf
type: 论文
tags: [Agent, 元学习, 持续学习, 技能库, 强化学习, GRPO, LoRA, 生产部署, 无停机]
---

# MetaClaw: Just Talk – An Agent That Meta-Learns and Evolves in the Wild

## 一句话总结

MetaClaw 提出了一种持续元学习框架，通过"技能驱动的快速适配"（梯度无关，零停机）和"机会主义策略优化"（RL + Cloud LoRA，仅在用户空闲时训练）两种互补机制，让部署在生产环境中的 LLM Agent 能够在无需停机的情况下从使用经验中持续自我进化，将 Kimi-K2.5 的准确率从 21.4% 提升至 40.6%（接近 GPT-5.2 基线 41.1%）。

## 基本信息

| 项目 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| **标题** | MetaClaw: Just Talk – An Agent That Meta-Learns and Evolves in the Wild |
| **作者** | Peng Xia*, Jianwen Chen*, Xinyu Yang*, Haoqin Tu*, Jiaqi Liu*, Kaiwen Xiong*, Siwei Han, Shi Qiu, Haonian Ji, Yuyin Zhou, Zeyu Zheng, Cihang Xie, Huaxiu Yao* |
| **机构** | UNC-Chapel Hill, Carnegie Mellon University, UC Santa Cruz, UC Berkeley |
| **日期** | 2026-03（arXiv v1: 2026-03-17） |
| **arXiv** | 2603.17187 |
| **代码** | https://github.com/aiming-lab/MetaClaw |

## 研究背景与动机

### 核心矛盾

部署在生产环境中的 LLM Agent 面临一个根本性矛盾：**必须持续无中断地服务用户，但其能力会随着任务分布的漂移而逐渐过时**。在 OpenClaw 这样的平台上，单个 Agent 连接 20+ 消息通道，处理多样化、不断演化的工作负载——本周可能是文件系统操作，下周可能变成多 Agent 消息工作流。

### 已有方法的三重困境

| 范式 | 代表方法 | 核心缺陷 |
|------|---------|---------|
| **基于记忆** | Reflexion, Mem0, SimpleMem | 存储原始轨迹但不提炼可迁移的行为知识 |
| **基于技能** | ExpeL, Voyager, EvolveR | 将技能库视为静态数据库，从不与权重优化协调 |
| **基于 RL** | PPO, GRPO | 仅在小规模/离线场景运行，忽略数据有效性问题 |

关键盲点：一旦技能演化，旧技能上下文下收集的轨迹携带**过时奖励（stale rewards）**，如果未过滤就复用会污染梯度更新。三种方法各只解决一个维度的适配问题，互补维度完全未被利用。

### 核心洞察

两种根本不同时间尺度的适配是**天然互补**的：

1. **行为启发式**（如"读取前验证文件路径"）：从单次失败对话中数秒内蒸馏完成，立即作为技能指令注入——**秒级、梯度无关**
2. **策略优化**：改善模型跨多样任务类型的底层策略，需要许多轨迹上的梯度优化——**分钟到小时级、梯度驱动**

二者形成**良性循环（virtuous cycle）**：更好的策略产生更有信息量的失败供技能合成，更丰富的技能产出更高奖励的轨迹供策略优化。

## 核心方法

**精读级别**：完整符号表、逐步拆解公式、架构直觉。

### 总体架构

MetaClaw 维护一个**元模型** $\mathcal{M} = (\theta, \mathcal{S})$，通过两个互补循环在不同时间尺度上运行：

```
失败轨迹 ──→ 技能演化器 (LLM Evolver) ──→ 新技能立即注入
    ↑                                           ↓
    │                                     后续任务使用新技能
    │                                           ↓
    │                              收集后适配轨迹（查询数据）
    │                                           ↓
    └── 用户空闲时 ←── OMLS 调度器 ←── Cloud LoRA RL 训练
```

### 符号表

| 符号 | 含义 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| $\mathcal{M} = (\theta, \mathcal{S})$ | 元模型 | 基座 LLM 策略参数 + 技能库 |
| $\theta$ | 基座 LLM 策略参数 | 通过 RL 更新 |
| $\mathcal{S} = \{s_1, \ldots, s_K\}$ | 技能库 | 可复用的行为指令集合 |
| $g$ | 技能代际（generation） | 每次技能库变化时递增 |
| $\mathcal{S}_g$ | 第 $g$ 代技能库 | 单调递增：$\mathcal{S}_{g+1} \supseteq \mathcal{S}_g$ |
| $\mathcal{E}$ | 技能演化器（Skill Evolver） | LLM，分析失败并合成新技能 |
| $\pi_\theta$ | 策略 | 以 $\theta$ 为参数的 LLM |
| $\text{Retrieve}(\mathcal{S}, \tau)$ | 技能检索 | 基于 embedding 的余弦相似度 top-$k$ 检索 |
| $D_g^{\text{sup}}$ | 支撑集（Support Data） | 第 $g$ 代下触发技能演化的失败轨迹 |
| $D_{g+1}^{\text{qry}}$ | 查询集（Query Data） | 第 $g+1$ 代生效后收集的轨迹 |
| $\mathcal{B}$ | RL 训练缓冲区 | 仅包含查询数据 |
| $\mathcal{R}$ | 过程奖励模型（PRM） | 评估轨迹质量 |
| OMLS | 机会主义元学习调度器 | 监控用户空闲信号 |

### 动作生成公式

给定任务 $\tau$，Agent 的行为完全由元模型决定：

$$a \sim \pi_\theta(\cdot \mid \tau, \text{Retrieve}(\mathcal{S}, \tau))$$

其中 $\text{Retrieve}(\mathcal{S}, \tau) \subseteq \mathcal{S}$ 通过 embedding 相似度选择与当前任务最相关的技能。

**直觉**：技能库 $\mathcal{S}$ 不是一个被动数据库，而是元模型的"快适配参数"——无需梯度计算，通过 prompt 注入即可在推理时即时生效。

### 机制一：技能驱动的快速适配（Skill-Driven Fast Adaptation）

#### 核心公式

$$\mathcal{S}_{g+1} = \mathcal{S}_g \cup \mathcal{E}(\mathcal{S}_g, D_g^{\text{sup}})$$

其中 $\mathcal{E}$ 是技能演化器——一个 LLM，分析失败轨迹并合成新的行为指令。

#### 设计哲学

**梯度无关是设计选择，而非近似**。技能库 $\mathcal{S}$ 生活在离散自然语言空间中，梯度下降在此空间中是不良定义的；LLM 驱动的失败分析是该空间的**天然适配机制**。

#### 技能库的双重角色

1. **元参数（Meta-Parameter）**：$\mathcal{S}$ 在整个任务流上积累行为知识，$\mathcal{S}_{g+1} \supseteq \mathcal{S}_g$ 代表系统不断增长的操作知识
2. **适配基（Adaptation Basis）**：$\text{Retrieve}(\mathcal{S}, \tau)$ 在推理时提取任务特定子集，提供即时专业化

与 EvolveR 的关键区别：MetaClaw 的技能库是**严格单调递增**的（只增不减），不设动态评分和剪枝机制，侧重于快速积累而非精细管理。

### 机制二：机会主义策略优化（Opportunistic Policy Optimization）

#### 核心公式

$$\theta_{t+1} = \theta_t + \alpha \nabla_\theta \mathbb{E}_{(\tau, \xi, g') \sim \mathcal{B}} \left[ \mathcal{R}(\pi_\theta(\cdot \mid \tau, \mathcal{S}_{g'})) \right]$$

其中 $g' \leq g^*$ 是每条轨迹收集时的技能代际，$\mathcal{R}$ 是过程奖励模型（PRM）分数。

#### 关键设计要点

1. **优化的是后适配行为**：不优化 $\theta$ 对原始任务的执行能力，而是优化 $\theta$ 在**经过技能适配后**的表现——这是真正的元学习目标
2. **代际混合训练**：缓冲区 $\mathcal{B}$ 包含不同代际 $g'$ 下的轨迹，每条轨迹都用其对应代际 $\mathcal{S}_{g'}$ 的技能进行评估
3. **数据门控**：训练仅在查询缓冲区积累了足够轨迹后启动——样本太少导致高方差梯度估计和不稳定更新

#### 实际实现

- 训练算法：GRPO（组相对策略优化）
- 微调方式：Cloud LoRA（通过 TML Tinker API）
- 训练时机：OMLS 调度器检测到用户空闲窗口时触发

**与 EvolveR 的区别**：EvolveR 的 RL 训练在离线批次中进行（8x A100 集群，约 39 小时），而 MetaClaw 的训练是**在线、渐进、碎片化**的——利用零散的空闲窗口累积梯度步骤，无需单一连续训练块。

### 技能代际版本化（Skill Generation Versioning）

这是 MetaClaw 最核心的数据治理创新，解决**支撑-查询分离**（support-query separation）问题。

#### 问题场景

一条轨迹 $(\tau_i, \xi_i)$ 触发了技能从 $\mathcal{S}_g$ 到 $\mathcal{S}_{g+1}$ 的演化，其奖励 $r_i$ 反映的是 $\mathcal{S}_g$ 下的表现（新技能尚不存在）。如果此轨迹进入 RL 缓冲区，策略优化会**因一个已被技能适配纠正的失败而惩罚 $\theta$**——优化的是前适配表现而非后适配表现，违反元学习目标。

#### 解决方案：版本化标记

每条收集的样本标记其技能代际 $g_i$：

- **支撑集** $D_g^{\text{sup}}$：在 $\mathcal{S}_g$ 下收集的、失败触发 $\mathcal{S}_g \to \mathcal{S}_{g+1}$ 的轨迹。被技能演化器消费，**从 RL 缓冲区中排除**
- **查询集** $D_{g+1}^{\text{qry}}$：在 $\mathcal{S}_{g+1}$ 生效后收集的轨迹。反映后适配行为，**仅这些有资格进入 RL 缓冲区**

当代际计数器从 $g$ 推进到 $g+1$ 时，训练器**清空缓冲区中所有版本 $\leq g$ 的样本**。

**直觉**：这保证了策略优化始终基于"技能适配后"的行为更新 $\theta$，维护了元学习结构的完整性。

### 机会主义元学习调度器（OMLS）

OMLS 是一个后台守护进程，监控三种互补的空闲信号：

| 信号 | 机制 | 特点 |
|------|------|------|
| **睡眠窗口** | 用户配置睡眠时间表（如 23:00-07:00） | 最大连续训练块 |
| **系统不活跃** | 轮询操作系统输入设备空闲计时器（如 macOS 的 `ioreg HIDIdleTime`），默认 $\delta=30$ 分钟无键盘/鼠标活动 | 自适应、即时的 |
| **日历感知调度** | 查询 Google Calendar API，当前时间处于已安排会议中则视为不可用 | 最具预见性的——利用用户日程主动预测空闲 |

**窗口控制逻辑**：
- 打开条件：任意信号指示用户不在
- 关闭条件：任意信号指示用户已返回
- RL 训练器支持跨碎片化空闲窗口的**暂停/恢复**，通过 mid-batch checkpointing 实现

### 完整算法（Algorithm 1）

```
输入：元模型 M = (θ₀, S₀), 技能演化器 E, 任务流 {τᵢ}, PRM R, OMLS
输出：持续改进的元模型 M

初始化：g ← 0, RL 缓冲区 B ← ∅

for 每个任务 τᵢ in 任务流 do
    // 用当前元模型服务任务
    S_τᵢ ← Retrieve(S_g, τᵢ)           // 检索相关技能
    ξᵢ ← Execute(π_θ(·|τᵢ, S_τᵢ))       // 收集轨迹
    rᵢ ← R(ξᵢ); 标记 (τᵢ, ξᵢ, rᵢ) 为代际 g

    if ξᵢ 揭示失败 then
        加入支撑集 D_g^sup
    else
        加入 RL 缓冲区 B
    end if

    // 技能驱动的快速适配（失败累积时触发）
    if |D_g^sup| ≥ 阈值 then
        ΔS ← E(S_g, D_g^sup)             // 从失败中合成新技能
        S_{g+1} ← S_g ∪ ΔS               // 演化技能库
        清空 B 中所有版本 ≤ g 的样本      // 支撑-查询分离
        g ← g + 1
    end if

    // 机会主义策略优化（用户空闲时触发）
    if OMLS 检测到空闲窗口 AND |B| ≥ batch_size then
        θ ← θ + α∇_θ E[R(π_θ(·|τ, S_g'))]  // RL 更新
        热切换模型权重                       // 部署更新后的 θ
    end if
end for
```

## 实验结果

### 基准：MetaClaw-Bench

新构建的持续 Agent 基准，包含两个互补评估部分（共 934 题，44 模拟工作日）：

| 部分 | 天数 | 题数 | 任务类型 | 特点 |
|------|------|------|---------|------|
| **Part I** | 30 天 | 346 | CLI 任务（file-check + multi-choice） | 执行导向，难度单调递增 |
| **Part II** | 14 天 | 588 | 规则转化任务（434 multi-choice + 154 file-check） | 规则遵从导向，高密度任务流 |

**评估指标**：
- **整体准确率**（Acc.）：每题得分的均值
- **文件检查完成率**（Compl.）：通过所有自动化检查器断言的 file-check 输出比例

### 主实验结果（Table 1）

| 模型 | 条件 | Part I Acc. | Part I Compl. | Part II Acc. | Part II Compl. |
|------|------|------------|---------------|--------------|----------------|
| GPT-5.2 | Baseline | 41.1% | 14.7% | 44.9% | 58.4% |
| GPT-5.2 | MetaClaw (Skills) | 44.0% (+7.1%) | 17.1% | 49.1% (+9.4%) | 67.5% |
| Kimi-K2.5 | Baseline | 21.4% | 2.0% | 21.1% | 18.2% |
| Kimi-K2.5 | MetaClaw (Skills) | 28.3% (+32.2%) | 2.0% | 26.9% (+27.5%) | 33.8% |
| **Kimi-K2.5** | **MetaClaw (Full)** | **40.6%** | **16.5%** | **39.6%** | **51.9%** |

### 关键发现

#### 1. 强模型受益少，弱模型受益多

GPT-5.2 起点高（41.1%），留给技能驱动提升的空间有限。Kimi-K2.5 缺乏隐式过程知识，技能库显式提供后收益更大。**MetaClaw (Full) + Kimi-K2.5（40.6%）几乎追平 GPT-5.2 基线（41.1%）**——技能注入 + 梯度策略优化可以很大程度上补偿模型能力差异。

#### 2. 全流程解锁端到端完成，仅技能不行

在 Part I 上，MetaClaw (Skills) 对两个模型的完成率都没有改变——技能注入提高了部分执行质量，但不能可靠实现零缺陷输出。MetaClaw (Full) 将 Kimi-K2.5 的完成率从 2.0% 跳升至 16.5%（8.25 倍）。**权重级 RL 更新是实现端到端完成率的必要条件。**

#### 3. 跨域泛化到开放式多阶段流水线（Table 2）

在 AutoResearchClaw（23 阶段自主研究流水线）上，仅用技能注入（无 RL）：

| 指标 | Baseline | + MetaClaw (Skills) | 相对变化 |
|------|----------|---------------------|---------|
| 阶段重试率 | 10.5% | 7.9% | ↓24.8% |
| 精炼循环数 | 2.0 | 1.2 | ↓40.0% |
| 流水线阶段完成 | 18/19 | 19/19 | ↑5.3% |
| 复合鲁棒性分数 | 0.714 | 0.845 | ↑18.3% |

这证明 MetaClaw 的轻量级、零停机技能注入可以跨域迁移到复杂、长视野的 Agent 工作流。

## 消融实验

### 逐日准确率趋势（Figure 2）

- **早期（Day 1-10）**：任务简单，所有条件都表现尚可
- **中期（Day 11-22）**：任务需要多步过程遵从，MetaClaw 优势最显著；MetaClaw (Full) 在 Day 19-20 达到峰值约 0.8 准确率
- **晚期（Day 23-30）**：复杂度超过积累知识能力，所有条件趋于低表现

**技能先行、RL 后至**的两阶段模式直接验证了 MetaClaw 设计的互补时间尺度假说。

### 任务类型分解（Figure 3）

两种 MetaClaw 组件解决根本不同的瓶颈：

- **技能单独**：提升 multi-choice 通过率，file-check 完成率不变（过程知识助推理不助执行）
- **全流程**：file-check 完成率跳升匹配 GPT-5.2 基线，multi-choice 准确率略降（策略在训练中偏向文件执行行为）

### RL 训练动态

Part II 中 MetaClaw (Full)–Kimi-K2.5 的 file-check 完成率曲线在 Day 8 出现**清晰拐点**：
- Day 1-4：~9%
- Day 5-8：27-36%
- Day 9-10：55-64%
- Day 12、14：100%

这反映了 MAML 内循环更新结构：前期积累支撑轨迹，拐点标记 LoRA 微调收集到足够梯度信号，后期收敛表示策略已内化过程规则。

### 技能库分析

30 天会话中，技能演化器合成的技能围绕三类反复出现的失败类别：

| 类别 | 示例 |
|------|------|
| **时间格式遵从** | 自然语言时间表达 → ISO 8601 格式 + 时区偏移 |
| **修改前备份协议** | 执行任何破坏性文件操作前创建 `.bak` 文件 |
| **命名规范遵守** | 遵循日期前缀文件命名模式（如 `20260408_*.json`） |

这些跨领域行为启发式解释了为何单一失败可以产出改善后续结构不同问题性能的技能。

### 案例研究（Table 3）

**Case 1（GPT-5.2 + Skills）**：Day 19 的任务要求修改 `sprint8_board.json`。Baseline 直接覆盖文件，检查器检测到缺少 `.bak` 文件。MetaClaw 注入的技能"Always create .bak before modifying"来自 Day 2 的失败——**一条规则跨文件类型和后续天数泛化，零权重更新**。

**Case 2（Kimi-K2.5 + Full）**：Day 18 的任务要求追加部署记录到 `deploy_log.json`。Baseline 用了 `date` 而非 `timestamp` 字段名。技能注入了"使用 ISO 8601 + 时区偏移"，但仅技能的 Kimi 仍然遗漏 `changes` 数组。**RL 之后：四个字段全部存在、schema 有效、备份已创建**——技能提供声明式格式上下文，权重更新内化了技能注入无法强制执行的可靠性。

## 优点与局限

### 优点

1. **统一两种时间尺度**：首次将梯度无关的技能适配与基于梯度的策略优化整合为连贯的元学习框架，并利用二者的良性循环
2. **零停机生产部署**：技能注入通过 prompt 生效；权重更新仅在空闲窗口进行；基于代理架构无需本地 GPU
3. **数据有效性治理**：技能代际版本化机制严格分离支撑/查询数据，防止过时奖励污染——这是在线 Agent RL 设置中此前被忽视的关键问题
4. **跨域泛化**：从 CLI 任务到自主研究流水线，技能注入无需领域特定调优即可迁移
5. **弱模型补偿**：MetaClaw (Full) 可让较弱模型接近强模型基线水平
6. **实用调度器**：OMLS 利用三种互补空闲信号，在真实部署场景中具有实用性

### 局限

1. **空闲窗口检测依赖用户配置**：日历感知调度需要 Google Calendar API 权限，不适用所有部署环境
2. **技能库单调递增**：没有 EvolveR 那样的动态评分和剪枝机制，长期可能面临技能膨胀问题
3. **仅评估模拟基准**：MetaClaw-Bench 是作者构建的模拟环境，非真实用户会话，绝对增益幅度可能无法直接迁移到生产负载
4. **策略优化仅验证一个模型**：MetaClaw (Full) 仅在 Kimi-K2.5 上评估（需配置 Cloud LoRA 端点），GPT-5.2 未评估全流程
5. **长时间跨度的遗忘未讨论**：44 天模拟内的表现是持续的，但更长时间（月/年级）的知识保留未验证
6. **基准可能有设计偏差**：Part II 的隐式规则（P1-P5）恰好是技能蒸馏擅长捕获的类型，可能高估技能驱动收益

## 提及的实体

- [[Peng-Xia]] — 核心贡献者，UNC-Chapel Hill
- [[Huaxiu-Yao]] — 通讯作者/核心贡献者，UNC-Chapel Hill
- [[Cihang-Xie]] — 作者，UC Santa Cruz
- [[Zeyu-Zheng]] — 作者，UC Berkeley
- [[UNC-Chapel-Hill]] — 主要机构
- [[UC-Santa-Cruz]] — 作者机构
- [[UC-Berkeley]] — 作者机构
- [[Carnegie-Mellon-University]] — 作者机构
- [[OpenClaw]] — 开源 CLI Agent 平台，MetaClaw 的部署载体
- [[AutoResearchClaw]] — 23 阶段自主研究流水线，跨域评估基准

## 讨论的概念

- [[持续元学习（Continual Meta-Learning）]] — MetaClaw 的核心范式：在非平稳任务流上持续学习，同时提高适配能力
- [[技能代际版本化（Skill Generation Versioning）]] — MetaClaw 的数据治理创新，支撑-查询分离
- [[Agent RL]] — MetaClaw 使用 RL 作为策略优化引擎
- [[GRPO]] — 策略优化的具体算法
- [[LoRA]] — Cloud LoRA 微调策略参数
- [[经验驱动自演化]] — MetaClaw 是此范式的生产级扩展
- [[自蒸馏（Agent策略自蒸馏）]] — 技能演化器可视为自蒸馏的一种实例
- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论框架
- [[过程奖励模型（Process Reward Model）]] — 评估轨迹质量用于 RL 训练

## 推荐参考文献

1. **EvolveR** (Wu et al., 2025) — 最直接的前序工作。MetaClaw 的技能演化器继承自 EvolveR 的自蒸馏思想，但增加了代际版本化和生产调度。arXiv: 2510.16079。参见 [[evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles]]
2. **SkillRL** (Xia et al., 2026) — 同一团队的技能增强 RL 工作，MetaClaw 的技能-RL 协同设计部分基于此。arXiv: 2602.08234
3. **AutoResearchClaw** (Liu et al., 2026b) — 跨域评估使用的 23 阶段自主研究流水线。arXiv: 未标注
4. **MAML** (Finn et al., 2017) — MetaClaw 的元学习理论基础。MetaClaw 将 MAML 的内外循环映射到技能适配和策略优化。ICML 2017
5. **Reflexion** (Shinn et al., 2023) — 基于记忆的 Agent 适配代表方法，存储语言自我反思。NeurIPS 2023
6. **GRPO / DeepSeekMath** (Shao et al., 2024) — MetaClaw 使用的策略优化算法。arXiv: 2402.03300
7. **LoRA** (Hu et al., 2021) — Cloud LoRA 微调的基础技术。参见 [[lora-low-rank-adaptation]]
8. **ExpeL** (Zhao et al., 2024) — 跨任务经验蒸馏的早期工作，MetaClaw 在数据有效性方面显著改进。AAAI 2024
9. **Mem0** (Chhikara et al., 2025) — 生产级 Agent 记忆框架。arXiv: 2504.19413
10. **SimpleMem** (Liu et al., 2026a) — 轻量级终身记忆方案。arXiv: 2601.02553

## 关联

- [[evolver-from-trajectories-to-principles|EvolveR]] — MetaClaw 的技能演化思想直接继承自 EvolveR 的自蒸馏范式；MetaClaw 将其扩展到生产环境，增加了代际版本化和调度器
- [[经验驱动自演化]] — MetaClaw 是经验驱动自演化范式在生产级场景的实现
- [[Agent RL]] — MetaClaw 使用 RL（GRPO + Cloud LoRA）进行策略优化
- [[GRPO]] — 策略优化的具体算法
- [[LoRA]] — Cloud LoRA 微调策略参数
- [[POMDP]] — Agent 交互的理论建模框架
- [[自蒸馏（Agent策略自蒸馏）]] — 技能演化器本质上是自蒸馏的一种实例
- [[Mem0]] — 结构化记忆框架，与 MetaClaw 的技能库形成对比
- [[agent-world|AgentWorld]] — 另一种 Agent RL 训练框架
